title: The Woman Who Reads on the Bus
title_zh: 公共汽车上看书的女人
text_zh: 每天早上，我坐公共汽车去上班。车上有一个女人，她总是坐在最后面，看一本很旧的书。虽然车上人很多，但是她好像什么都听不到。我觉得她比别人都安静。有一天，她没有来，我才知道我已经习惯了看到她。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang, wǒ zuò gōnggòng qìchē qù shàngbān. Chē shàng yǒu yí gè nǚrén, tā zǒngshì zuò zài zuì hòumiàn, kàn yì běn hěn jiù de shū. Suīrán chē shàng rén hěn duō, dànshì tā hǎoxiàng shénme dōu tīng bú dào. Wǒ juéde tā bǐ biéren dōu ānjìng. Yǒu yì tiān, tā méiyǒu lái, wǒ cái zhīdào wǒ yǐjīng xíguàn le kàn dào tā.
text_en: Every morning, I take the bus to work. There's a woman who always sits in the very back, reading an old book. Even though the bus is crowded, she seems to hear nothing. I think she's quieter than everyone else. One day she didn't come, and only then did I realize I'd gotten used to seeing her.

questions.q_zh: 如果故事继续，最可能发生什么？
questions.q_en: If the story continued, what would most likely happen?

options.pinyin: huì yǒu hěn dà de wèntí

questions.explanation: The tone is calm and routine — no conflict or tension, just quiet daily life continuing.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么选择写这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why did the author choose to include these details?

options.text_en: to help the reader feel the scene
because it'll be on the test

questions.explanation: The old book, the back seat, her stillness — these sensory details put you on that bus.

questions.q_zh: 她没有来的时候，「我」怎么了？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel when she didn't come?

options.text_en: realized I was already used to it

questions.explanation: 才知道 ('only then did I realize') signals a sudden awareness — her absence revealed the habit that had formed without noticing.

title: The Shoe Repair Man
text_zh: 路旁边有一个老人，他每天都在那里修鞋。他的手很快，眼睛一直看着鞋。有人问他做了多少年了，他说：「从我年轻的时候到现在。」虽然这个工作不让他有很多钱，但是他好像很喜欢。
text_pinyin: Lù pángbiān yǒu yí gè lǎorén, tā měi tiān dōu zài nàli xiū xié. Tā de shǒu hěn kuài, yǎnjing yìzhí kànzhe xié. Yǒu rén wèn tā zuò le duōshao nián le, tā shuō: 「Cóng wǒ niánqīng de shíhou dào xiànzài.」Suīrán zhège gōngzuò bú ràng tā yǒu hěn duō qián, dànshì tā hǎoxiàng hěn xǐhuan.
text_en: By the road there's an old man who repairs shoes every day. His hands are fast, his eyes always on the shoes. Someone asked how many years he'd been doing it. He said: 'From when I was young until now.' Although this job doesn't give him much money, he seems to like it.

questions.q_zh: 读完这段话，你觉得作者想说什么？
questions.q_en: After reading this, what do you think the author wants to say?

options.text: 平凡的日子也值得珍惜
options.pinyin: píngfán de rìzi yě zhíde zhēnxī

options.text_en: should move to a big city

questions.explanation: He does quiet, steady work every day with care — the passage values that ordinary dedication.

questions.q_zh: 他做这个工作多长时间了？
questions.q_en: How long has he done this work?
questions.explanation: He said he started as a young person and has done it ever since.

questions.q_zh: 这个工作让他有很多钱吗？
questions.q_en: Does this job give him a lot of money?
questions.explanation: He said 'not much money' but his hands keep working — the pay isn't the point.

title: Two Cups of Coffee
text_zh: 咖啡店里，一个男人坐在那里，桌子上有两杯咖啡。他一直看手表，好像在等人。一个小时过了，另一杯咖啡已经冷了。他站起来，把两杯都喝了，然后走了。
text_pinyin: Kāfēidiàn lǐ, yí gè nánrén zuò zài nàli, zhuōzi shàng yǒu liǎng bēi kāfēi. Tā yìzhí kàn shǒubiǎo, hǎoxiàng zài děng rén. Yí gè xiǎoshí guò le, lìng yì bēi kāfēi yǐjīng lěng le. Tā zhàn qǐlái, bǎ liǎng bēi dōu hē le, ránhòu zǒu le.
text_en: In a coffee shop, a man sits with two cups of coffee on the table. He keeps checking his watch, as if waiting for someone. An hour passes. The other cup is already cold. He stands up, drinks both cups, and leaves.

questions.q_zh: 安静的场景传达了什么信息？
questions.q_en: What does the quiet scene communicate?
questions.explanation: He waited, no one came, but he sat calmly and drank both — no drama, just quiet acceptance.

questions.q_zh: 观察的行为说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the act of observing suggest?
questions.explanation: The narrator watches small details closely — the two cups, the watch, the waiting.

questions.q_zh: 最后他怎么做了？
questions.q_en: What did he do in the end?

options.pinyin: zhǐ hē le yì bēi jiù zǒu le
options.text_en: drank only one cup and left

options.pinyin: bǎ liǎng bēi dōu hē le
děng dào nàge rén lái le

questions.explanation: The passage ends with him drinking both cups of coffee before leaving.

title: Sounds of the Night Market
text_zh: 晚上的夜市很热闹。卖东西的人大声说话，让人来看。但是我最喜欢听的不是他们的声音，是一个老人在旁边拉二胡的声音。别人都不看他，但是他的音乐比什么都好听。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang de yèshì hěn rènao. Mài dōngxi de rén dà shēng shuō huà, ràng rén lái kàn. Dànshì wǒ zuì xǐhuan tīng de búshì tāmen de shēngyīn, shì yí gè lǎorén zài pángbiān lā èrhú de shēngyīn. Biéren dōu bú kàn tā, dànshì tā de yīnyuè bǐ shénme dōu hǎotīng.
text_en: The night market is lively in the evening. Sellers call out loudly, getting people to come look. But what I like listening to most isn't their voices — it's the sound of an old man playing erhu off to the side. Nobody watches him, but his music is better to listen to than anything.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话你能了解到人物什么？
questions.q_en: What can you learn about the character from this passage?

options.text: 他/她是一个有感情的人
options.pinyin: tā shì yí gè yǒu gǎnqíng de rén
options.text_en: they are a person with feelings

questions.explanation: The narrator notices the erhu player's feelings, not just the market — that takes emotional depth.

questions.q_zh: 故事里最重要的不是发生了什么，而是什么？
questions.q_en: The most important thing in this story is not what happened, but what?
questions.explanation: The story focuses on how the narrator feels listening, not on what happened at the market.

questions.q_zh: 别人看那个拉二胡的老人吗？
questions.q_en: Do other people watch the erhu player?
questions.explanation: 没有人看他 — he plays while others pass by, confirming no audience watches.

title: The Convenience Store at Midnight
text_zh: 半夜十二点，便利店里只有一个人在工作。他看起来很累，但是还在笑着跟每个人说「欢迎」。买东西的人很少，大多是一个人来的。我觉得半夜来便利店的人都有自己的故事。
text_pinyin: Bànyè shí'èr diǎn, biànlìdiàn lǐ zhǐ yǒu yí gè rén zài gōngzuò. Tā kàn qǐlái hěn lèi, dànshì hái zài xiàozhe gēn měi gè rén shuō「huānyíng」. Mǎi dōngxi de rén hěn shǎo, dàduō shì yí gè rén lái de. Wǒ juéde bànyè lái biànlìdiàn de rén dōu yǒu zìjǐ de gùshi.
text_en: At midnight, there's only one person working in the convenience store. He looks tired, but he's still smiling and saying 'welcome' to everyone. Few people come to buy things, and most come alone. I think everyone who comes to the convenience store at midnight has their own story.

questions.q_zh: 便利店的工作人员看起来怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the store worker look?
questions.explanation: 他看起来很累 is stated in the opening line, directly describing his appearance as tired.

questions.q_zh: 半夜来买东西的人多吗？
questions.q_en: Are there many people buying things at midnight?
questions.explanation: At midnight, 很少有人进来买东西 — late hours mean few customers, which is why most come alone.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的语气/氛围是怎样的？
questions.q_en: What is the tone/atmosphere of this passage?
questions.explanation: Late night, one tired worker, few customers, still smiling — the mood is still and reflective.

title: The Bicycle Left Behind
text_zh: 学校门口有一辆旧自行车。从春天到夏天，它一直在那里。没有人骑它，也没有人把它拿走。它旁边的花已经长得很高了。我想知道它的主人去了哪里，为什么不回来了。
text_pinyin: Xuéxiào ménkǒu yǒu yí liàng jiù zìxíngchē. Cóng chūntiān dào xiàtiān, tā yìzhí zài nàli. Méiyǒu rén qí tā, yě méiyǒu rén bǎ tā ná zǒu. Tā pángbiān de huā yǐjīng zhǎng de hěn gāo le. Wǒ xiǎng zhīdào tā de zhǔrén qù le nǎli, wèishénme bù huílái le.
text_en: There's an old bicycle outside the school gate. From spring to summer, it's been there. Nobody rides it, and nobody takes it away. The flowers next to it have already grown tall. I wonder where its owner went, and why they never came back.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么选择写这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why did the author choose to include these details?

options.text_en: to help the reader feel the scene
because it'll be on the test

questions.explanation: The rusting bike, changing seasons, growing flowers — these details create a vivid scene you can feel.

questions.q_zh: 自行车在那里多长时间了？
questions.q_en: How long has the bicycle been there?
questions.explanation: 从春天到夏天 gives the time range directly — it has sat there across two seasons.

questions.q_zh: 从这里可以看出他/她什么样的性格？
questions.q_en: What does this reveal about their personality?
questions.explanation: The narrator watches and tracks the bike's decay over months — that's focused, deliberate attention.

title: The Dog That Waits
text_zh: 每天下午五点，一只狗坐在小区门口。它不走，也不叫，只是看着路上的人。五点半左右，一个女孩从学校回来，狗就跑过去。因为它每天都等，所以我知道它在等谁。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān xiàwǔ wǔ diǎn, yì zhī gǒu zuò zài xiǎoqū ménkǒu. Tā bù zǒu, yě bú jiào, zhǐshì kànzhe lù shàng de rén. Wǔ diǎn bàn zuǒyòu, yí gè nǚhái cóng xuéxiào huílái, gǒu jiù pǎo guòqù. Yīnwèi tā měi tiān dōu děng, suǒyǐ wǒ zhīdào tā zài děng shéi.
text_en: Every day at five in the afternoon, a dog sits at the entrance of the residential compound. It doesn't leave, doesn't bark — just watches the people on the road. Around five-thirty, a girl comes back from school, and the dog runs over. Because it waits every day, I know who it's waiting for.

questions.q_zh: 狗每天什么时候开始等？
questions.q_en: What time does the dog start waiting every day?
questions.explanation: 每天下午五点 specifies the exact daily time the dog begins its wait.

questions.q_zh: 如果故事继续，最可能发生什么？
questions.q_en: If the story continued, what would most likely happen?

options.pinyin: huì yǒu hěn dà de wèntí

questions.explanation: The dog waits quietly every day, nothing dramatic changes — just peaceful routine.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用了什么方式让读者理解人物？
questions.q_en: How does the passage help the reader understand the character?

options.pinyin: tōngguò miáoxiě tā zuò de shì

options.text: 直接说他/她是什么样的人
options.pinyin: zhíjiē shuō tā shì shénme yàng de rén
options.text_en: by directly stating what kind of person they are

questions.explanation: The passage describes the dog's actions — sitting, watching, waiting — to show its character.

title: The Light on the Third Floor
text_zh: 对面的楼里，三楼的灯每天晚上都很晚才关。从窗户可以看到一个人的影子，好像一直在写东西。我不知道他是谁，但是我觉得他一定很忙。有时候我起来喝水，看到那个灯还开着，就觉得自己不是一个人。
text_pinyin: Duìmiàn de lóu lǐ, sān lóu de dēng měi tiān wǎnshang dōu hěn wǎn cái guān. Cóng chuānghu kěyǐ kàn dào yí gè rén de yǐngzi, hǎoxiàng yìzhí zài xiě dōngxi. Wǒ bù zhīdào tā shì shéi, dànshì wǒ juéde tā yídìng hěn máng. Yǒu shíhou wǒ qǐlái hē shuǐ, kàn dào nàge dēng hái kāizhe, jiù juéde zìjǐ búshì yí gè rén.
text_en: In the building across the way, the light on the third floor is always turned off late at night. Through the window you can see a person's shadow, seemingly always writing. I don't know who they are, but they must be busy. Sometimes I get up to drink water, see that light still on, and feel like I'm not alone.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中，什么是最有意思的部分？
questions.q_en: What is the most interesting part of this passage?

options.text: 平常中有不平常的东西
options.pinyin: píngcháng zhōng yǒu bù píngcháng de dōngxi
fāshēng le hěn dà de shì

questions.explanation: The late-night shadow doing ordinary things is unexpected — something quietly unusual in a normal building.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话你能了解到人物什么？
questions.q_en: What can you learn about the character from this passage?

options.text: 他/她是一个有感情的人
options.pinyin: tā shì yí gè yǒu gǎnqíng de rén
options.text_en: they are a person with feelings

questions.explanation: The narrator wonders about someone they've never met — that curiosity shows feeling.

questions.q_zh: 看到灯还开着，「我」有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel seeing the light still on?

options.pinyin: juéde zìjǐ búshì yí gè rén

questions.explanation: Seeing the light still on late at night, the narrator feels 'not alone' — someone else is awake too.

title: The Subway at Rush Hour
text_zh: 早上八点的地铁，人比平时多很多。大家都在看手机，没有人说话。虽然人很多，但是很安静。从这一站到下一站只要两分钟，但是感觉比两分钟长。因为太挤了，我只能站着。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang bā diǎn de dìtiě, rén bǐ píngshí duō hěn duō. Dàjiā dōu zài kàn shǒujī, méiyǒu rén shuō huà. Suīrán rén hěn duō, dànshì hěn ānjìng. Cóng zhè yí zhàn dào xià yí zhàn zhǐ yào liǎng fēnzhōng, dànshì gǎnjué bǐ liǎng fēnzhōng cháng. Yīnwèi tài jǐ le, wǒ zhǐ néng zhànzhe.
text_en: The subway at eight in the morning — there are far more people than usual. Everyone is looking at their phones. Nobody talks. Even though it's crowded, it's quiet. From this station to the next takes only two minutes, but it feels longer. Because it's too crowded, I can only stand.

questions.q_zh: 观察的行为说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the act of observing suggest?
questions.explanation: The narrator notices phones, silence, the two-minute gap — paying close attention to ordinary moments.

questions.q_zh: 从这一站到下一站要多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long from this station to the next?
questions.explanation: 从这一站到下一站只要两分钟 — the travel time between stations is stated as two minutes.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么只能站着？
questions.q_en: Why can 'I' only stand?
questions.explanation: 人太多了,没有地方坐 — the crowd has filled every seat, leaving standing as the only option.

title: The Street After Rain
text_zh: 雨刚停，街上还很湿。路灯的光在地上变成了一片一片的。卖水果的老人把水果从里面搬出来了。空气比下雨前好多了，我决定不坐车，走路回家。走在雨后的路上，我觉得这个城市比平时好看。
text_pinyin: Yǔ gāng tíng, jiē shàng hái hěn shī. Lùdēng de guāng zài dì shàng biànchéng le yí piàn yí piàn de. Mài shuǐguǒ de lǎorén bǎ shuǐguǒ cóng lǐmiàn bān chūlái le. Kōngqì bǐ xià yǔ qián hǎo duō le, wǒ juédìng bú zuò chē, zǒu lù huí jiā. Zǒu zài yǔ hòu de lù shàng, wǒ juéde zhège chéngshì bǐ píngshí hǎokàn.
text_en: The rain just stopped. The street is still wet. The streetlight reflects in patches on the ground. The fruit seller has moved his fruit back outside. The air is much better than before the rain. I decide not to take a bus — I'll walk home. Walking on the post-rain road, I think this city looks better than usual.

questions.q_zh: 雨停了以后，空气怎么样？
questions.q_en: How is the air after the rain stops?
questions.explanation: 空气比下雨以前好多了 — the comparison 比...好多了 directly shows improvement after the rain.

questions.q_zh: 「我」决定怎么回家？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' decide to go home?
questions.explanation: The narrator decides 'I don't want to take the bus, I'll walk home.'

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得雨后的城市怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel about the city after rain?
questions.explanation: The narrator says the city 'looks better than usual' after the rain.

title: The Old Apartment Building
text_zh: 我住的公寓楼已经很旧了。楼下的门有时候关不上。邻居是一个老太太，她每天都把门口打扫得很干净。虽然楼旧了，但是因为有她，这里还是让人觉得很好。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de gōngyù lóu yǐjīng hěn jiù le. Lóu xià de mén yǒu shíhou guān bú shàng. Línjū shì yí gè lǎo tàitai, tā měi tiān dōu bǎ ménkǒu dǎsǎo de hěn gānjìng. Suīrán lóu jiù le, dànshì yīnwèi yǒu tā, zhèlǐ háishi ràng rén juéde hěn hǎo.
text_en: The apartment building I live in is already old. The door downstairs sometimes won't close. My neighbor is an old lady who sweeps the entrance clean every day. Although the building is old, because of her, this place still feels good.

questions.q_zh: 如果打破了这个习惯，可能会怎样？
questions.q_en: What might happen if this routine were broken?

options.pinyin: huì zhǎodào gèng hǎo de xíguàn

questions.explanation: The narrator relies on the neighbor's daily sweeping — without it, the place would feel wrong.

questions.q_zh: 如果打破了这个习惯，可能会怎样？
questions.q_en: What might happen if this routine were broken?

options.pinyin: huì zhǎodào gèng hǎo de xíguàn

questions.explanation: The narrator depends on the neighbor's routine for comfort — breaking it would feel unsettling.

questions.q_zh: 因为邻居，「我」觉得这里怎么样？
questions.q_en: Because of the neighbor, how does 'I' feel about this place?

options.pinyin: háishi ràng rén juéde hěn hǎo

questions.explanation: Because the neighbor keeps it clean and cares, the narrator says it 'still feels good' despite the old building.

title: The Bridge Over the River
text_zh: 城市中间有一条河，河上有一座旧桥。每天早上，很多人从桥上走过去上班。晚上，有人在桥上看河水。桥上的灯不太亮，但是从桥上看到的城市很好看。这座桥已经有五十多年了，比很多楼都老。
text_pinyin: Chéngshì zhōngjiān yǒu yì tiáo hé, hé shàng yǒu yí zuò jiù qiáo. Měi tiān zǎoshang, hěn duō rén cóng qiáo shàng zǒu guòqù shàngbān. Wǎnshang, yǒu rén zài qiáo shàng kàn hé shuǐ. Qiáo shàng de dēng bú tài liàng, dànshì cóng qiáo shàng kàn dào de chéngshì hěn hǎokàn. Zhè zuò qiáo yǐjīng yǒu wǔshí duō nián le, bǐ hěn duō lóu dōu lǎo.
text_en: There's a river in the middle of the city, with an old bridge over it. Every morning, many people cross the bridge to work. In the evening, people stand on the bridge watching the water. The bridge lights aren't very bright, but the city seen from the bridge is beautiful. This bridge is over fifty years old — older than many buildings.

questions.q_zh: 如果故事继续，最可能发生什么？
questions.q_en: If the story continued, what would most likely happen?

options.pinyin: huì yǒu hěn dà de wèntí

questions.explanation: People cross in the morning, watch the river at night — the same calm routine repeats, nothing disrupts it.

questions.q_zh: 晚上人们在桥上做什么？
questions.q_en: What do people do on the bridge in the evening?
questions.explanation: 晚上人们站在桥上看水 — the evening activity described is standing on the bridge watching the water below.

questions.q_zh: 这座桥有多少年了？
questions.q_en: How old is this bridge?
questions.explanation: 五十多年了 gives the bridge's age directly — more than fifty years.

title: The Rooftop View
text_zh: 我家住在八楼。从楼顶上可以看到很远的地方。白天能看到很多车在路上走，像很小的东西。晚上比白天好看，因为到处都是灯。我最喜欢刚下完雨的晚上，那时候灯的光比平时更亮。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā zhù zài bā lóu. Cóng lóu dǐng shàng kěyǐ kàn dào hěn yuǎn de dìfang. Báitiān néng kàn dào hěn duō chē zài lù shàng zǒu, xiàng hěn xiǎo de dōngxi. Wǎnshang bǐ báitiān hǎokàn, yīnwèi dàochù dōu shì dēng. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan gāng xià wán yǔ de wǎnshang, nà shíhou dēng de guāng bǐ píngshí gèng liàng.
text_en: My home is on the eighth floor. From the rooftop you can see very far. During the day you see many cars on the road, looking like tiny things. The nighttime is more beautiful than daytime because there are lights everywhere. I like evenings right after rain the most — the light from the lamps is brighter than usual then.

questions.q_zh: 「我」家住在几楼？
questions.q_en: What floor does 'I' live on?
questions.explanation: 我家在八楼 states the floor number directly — eighth floor.

questions.q_zh: 晚上为什么比白天好看？
questions.q_en: Why is nighttime more beautiful than daytime?
questions.explanation: 到处都是灯 — the lights everywhere at night create the beauty that makes nighttime better than daytime.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的主要感受是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the main feeling of this passage?
questions.explanation: The rooftop view, the lights, the quiet observation — the overall feeling is warm and comforting.

title: The Library's Unwritten Rule
text_zh: 我们学校的图书馆有一个大家都知道的规矩：靠窗的座位是给考试前要学习的人的。虽然没有人写下来，但是别的学生都知道别坐那里。有一个新来的学生坐了那里，旁边的人看了他一眼，他就懂了，换了一个座位。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xuéxiào de túshūguǎn yǒu yí gè dàjiā dōu zhīdào de guījǔ: kào chuāng de zuòwèi shì gěi kǎoshì qián yào xuéxí de rén de. Suīrán méiyǒu rén xiě xiàlái, dànshì biéde xuéshēng dōu zhīdào bié zuò nàli. Yǒu yí gè xīn lái de xuéshēng zuò le nàli, pángbiān de rén kàn le tā yì yǎn, tā jiù dǒng le, huàn le yí gè zuòwèi.
text_en: Our school library has a rule everyone knows: the window seats are for people studying before exams. Although nobody wrote it down, other students all know not to sit there. A new student sat there once. The person next to him gave him a look, and he understood — he switched seats.

questions.q_zh: 故事里最重要的不是发生了什么，而是什么？
questions.q_en: The most important thing in this story is not what happened, but what?
questions.explanation: The story is about how the new student felt and adapted — the emotions matter more than the events.

questions.q_zh: 这个规矩写下来了吗？
questions.q_en: Was this rule written down?
questions.explanation: 虽然没有人写下来 confirms it was never formally recorded — everyone simply learned it through observation.

questions.q_zh: 新来的学生最后怎么做了？
questions.q_en: What did the new student end up doing?

options.pinyin: gēn pángbiān de rén shuō huà
options.text_en: talked to the person next to him

questions.explanation: The new student eventually understood and switched to a different seat.

title: The Hospital Waiting Room
text_zh: 医院的等候室里，大家都在等。有的人看手机，有的人看墙上的电视。一个妈妈让她的小孩别跑，坐下来。护士出来叫名字的时候，大家都看她。这里的时间好像比外面慢。
text_pinyin: Yīyuàn de děnghòushì lǐ, dàjiā dōu zài děng. Yǒude rén kàn shǒujī, yǒude rén kàn qiáng shàng de diànshì. Yí gè māma ràng tā de xiǎohái bié pǎo, zuò xiàlái. Hùshi chūlái jiào míngzi de shíhou, dàjiā dōu kàn tā. Zhèlǐ de shíjiān hǎoxiàng bǐ wàimiàn màn.
text_en: In the hospital waiting room, everyone waits. Some look at phones, some watch the TV on the wall. A mother tells her child to stop running and sit down. When the nurse comes out to call a name, everyone looks at her. Time here seems to move slower than outside.

questions.q_zh: 这些人之间最重要的是什么？
questions.q_en: What matters most between these people?
questions.explanation: Everyone's waiting with their own problem — they share the space quietly, respecting each other.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中，什么是最有意思的部分？
questions.q_en: What is the most interesting part of this passage?

options.text: 平常中有不平常的东西
options.pinyin: píngcháng zhōng yǒu bù píngcháng de dōngxi
fāshēng le hěn dà de shì

questions.explanation: A hospital waiting room full of unspoken worries — ordinary but emotionally layered.

questions.q_zh: 等候室里的时间感觉怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does time feel in the waiting room?
questions.explanation: 时间好像比外面慢 — the 好像 ('seems like') captures how waiting distorts the perception of time.

title: School Lunch
text_zh: 我们学校的午饭，大家都说不好吃。但是每天中午，大家还是去吃。因为学校旁边没有别的饭馆，而且学校的饭比外面便宜。有一天学校换了一个新的做饭的人，大家都觉得比以前好吃了一点。只是一点，但是大家都很高兴。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xuéxiào de wǔfàn, dàjiā dōu shuō bù hǎochī. Dànshì měi tiān zhōngwǔ, dàjiā háishi qù chī. Yīnwèi xuéxiào pángbiān méiyǒu biéde fànguǎn, érqiě xuéxiào de fàn bǐ wàimiàn piányi. Yǒu yì tiān xuéxiào huàn le yí gè xīn de zuò fàn de rén, dàjiā dōu juéde bǐ yǐqián hǎochī le yìdiǎn. Zhǐshì yìdiǎn, dànshì dàjiā dōu hěn gāoxìng.
text_en: Everyone says our school lunch isn't good. But every noon, everyone still goes to eat. Because there are no other restaurants nearby, and the school food is cheaper than outside. One day the school got a new cook, and everyone thought it was a little better than before. Just a little — but everyone was happy.

questions.q_zh: 大家为什么还是去吃学校的午饭？
questions.q_en: Why does everyone still eat the school lunch?

options.text: 因为没有别的饭馆，而且便宜
options.pinyin: yīnwèi méiyǒu biéde fànguǎn, érqiě piányi
options.text_en: no other restaurants and it's cheap

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tāmen bù xiǎng zǒu lù
options.text_en: because they don't want to walk

questions.explanation: No other restaurants nearby and the school food is cheap — two practical reasons.

questions.q_zh: 学校换了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the school change?
questions.explanation: 学校换了厨师 — 换 means 'replaced/changed,' and 厨师 is the cook.

questions.q_zh: 换了以后，饭好吃了多少？
questions.q_en: After the change, how much better was the food?
questions.explanation: After the change, the food was 'a little better' — not a big improvement.

title: The Wrong Umbrella
text_zh: 下雨了，我从饭馆出来，拿了一把伞。走了一会儿，我发现这不是我的伞——我的伞是黑的，这把是蓝的。伞里面有一张小纸条，上面写着一个电话号码和一个名字。我想，这个人现在可能正在找这把伞。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ le, wǒ cóng fànguǎn chūlái, ná le yì bǎ sǎn. Zǒu le yíhuìr, wǒ fāxiàn zhè búshì wǒ de sǎn——wǒ de sǎn shì hēi de, zhè bǎ shì lán de. Sǎn lǐmiàn yǒu yì zhāng xiǎo zhǐtiáo, shàngmiàn xiězhe yí gè diànhuà hàomǎ hé yí gè míngzi. Wǒ xiǎng, zhège rén xiànzài kěnéng zhèngzài zhǎo zhè bǎ sǎn.
text_en: It was raining. I came out of a restaurant and took an umbrella. After walking a bit, I realized it wasn't mine — my umbrella is black, this one is blue. Inside the umbrella there was a small note with a phone number and a name. I thought: this person is probably looking for this umbrella right now.

questions.q_zh: 「我」的伞是什么颜色的？
questions.q_en: What color is 'my' umbrella?
questions.explanation: 我的伞是黑色的 directly states the color — black, which is how the narrator realized this blue one wasn't theirs.

questions.q_zh: 伞里面有什么？
questions.q_en: What was inside the umbrella?
questions.explanation: 伞里面有一张小纸条 — a small note was found inside, with a phone number written on it.

questions.q_zh: 纸条上写了什么？
questions.q_en: What was written on the note?
questions.explanation: The note had 'a phone number and a name' written on it.

title: The Extra Key
text_zh: 我搬到新家以后，在一个旧桌子的里面发现了一把钥匙。这把钥匙不是这个房间的门的——我试过了。我问了房东，他说不知道。我又问了以前住在这里的人，她也说不是她的。那这把钥匙是谁的？它能开什么门？
text_pinyin: Wǒ bān dào xīn jiā yǐhòu, zài yí gè jiù zhuōzi de lǐmiàn fāxiàn le yì bǎ yàoshi. Zhè bǎ yàoshi búshì zhège fángjiān de mén de——wǒ shì guò le. Wǒ wèn le fángdōng, tā shuō bù zhīdào. Wǒ yòu wèn le yǐqián zhù zài zhèlǐ de rén, tā yě shuō búshì tā de. Nà zhè bǎ yàoshi shì shéi de? Tā néng kāi shénme mén?
text_en: After moving to my new home, I found a key inside an old desk. This key doesn't belong to this room's door — I tried. I asked the landlord; he didn't know. I asked the previous tenant; she said it wasn't hers either. So whose key is it? What door does it open?

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么选择写这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why did the author choose to include these details?

options.text_en: to help the reader feel the scene
because it'll be on the test

questions.explanation: The key, the old desk, the unanswered questions — these details pull you into the mystery.

questions.q_zh: 这把钥匙能开这个房间的门吗？
questions.q_en: Can this key open this room's door?
questions.explanation: 我试过了 — the narrator already tested it on the door and confirmed it doesn't fit this room's lock.

questions.q_zh: 「我」问了谁？
questions.q_en: Who did 'I' ask?

options.text: 问了房东和以前住这里的人
options.pinyin: wèn le fángdōng hé yǐqián zhù zhèlǐ de rén
options.text_en: asked the landlord and previous tenant

questions.explanation: The narrator asked both the landlord and the previous tenant about the key.

title: The Missing Cat Returns
text_zh: 邻居的猫走了三天了。大家都帮她找，但是没有找到。第四天早上，猫自己回来了。它看起来不累也不饿，身上还很干净。最奇怪的是，它的脖子上多了一个小铃铛。邻居说那不是她的。那是谁给猫的？
text_pinyin: Línjū de māo zǒu le sān tiān le. Dàjiā dōu bāng tā zhǎo, dànshì méiyǒu zhǎo dào. Dì sì tiān zǎoshang, māo zìjǐ huílái le. Tā kàn qǐlái bú lèi yě bú è, shēn shàng hái hěn gānjìng. Zuì qíguài de shì, tā de bózi shàng duō le yí gè xiǎo língdang. Línjū shuō nà búshì tā de. Nà shì shéi gěi māo de?
text_en: The neighbor's cat has been gone for three days. Everyone helped look, but couldn't find it. On the fourth morning, the cat came back on its own. It didn't look tired or hungry, and was still clean. The strangest thing: there was a new little bell on its neck. The neighbor said it wasn't hers. So who gave the cat the bell?

questions.q_zh: 这种感情最可能来自什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most likely source of this feeling?
questions.explanation: The cat returned clean with a new bell — it stirs wonder about where it went, echoing half-remembered feelings.

questions.q_zh: 猫回来的时候看起来怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did the cat look when it came back?

options.pinyin: bú lèi yě bú è, hěn gānjìng

questions.explanation: 不累也不饿,还很干净 — the cat returned in good condition, suggesting it was well cared for wherever it went.

questions.q_zh: 最奇怪的是什么？
questions.q_en: What was the strangest thing?

options.text_en: the cat came back on its own

options.text: 脖子上多了一个小铃铛
options.pinyin: bózi shàng duō le yí gè xiǎo língdang
options.text_en: a new little bell on its neck

questions.explanation: The strangest thing was 'a new little bell on its neck' that wasn't there before.

title: The Polite Misunderstanding
text_zh: 我在电梯里遇到了邻居。我们两个都想让对方先出去，都说「你先走」。说了三次以后，电梯门关了。我们又到了下一层楼。这次我们两个都不说话了，一起走了出去。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài diàntī lǐ yù dào le línjū. Wǒmen liǎng gè dōu xiǎng ràng duìfāng xiān chūqù, dōu shuō「nǐ xiān zǒu」. Shuō le sān cì yǐhòu, diàntī mén guān le. Wǒmen yòu dào le xià yì céng lóu. Zhè cì wǒmen liǎng gè dōu bù shuō huà le, yìqǐ zǒu le chūqù. Ránhòu wǒ cái fāxiàn, zhè búshì wǒ yào qù de lóucéng.
text_en: I ran into my neighbor in the elevator. We both wanted to let the other go first, both saying 'you go first.' After saying it three times, the elevator doors closed. We ended up on the next floor. This time neither of us said anything — we both walked out together.

questions.q_zh: 他们在电梯里说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did they say in the elevator?
questions.explanation: They kept saying 'you go first' back and forth.

questions.q_zh: 电梯门为什么关了？
questions.q_en: Why did the elevator doors close?

options.text: 因为他们说了太多次「你先走」
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tāmen shuō le tài duō cì「nǐ xiān zǒu」
options.text_en: because they said 'you first' too many times

options.pinyin: yīnwèi yǒu rén àn le ànniǔ
yīnwèi tāmen xiǎng qù xià yì céng
options.text_en: because they wanted the next floor

questions.explanation: They said 'you first' three times without moving, so the elevator doors closed.

questions.q_zh: 「我」最后发现了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' realize in the end?

options.text: 这不是自己要去的楼层
options.pinyin: zhè búshì zìjǐ yào qù de lóucéng

questions.explanation: After the doors closed, the narrator realized it wasn't even the right floor.

title: The Phone Call Mix-up
text_zh: 妈妈给我打电话，说了很多话。她说：「你要多吃饭，别太累了，早点儿睡觉。」我说：「好的好的。」她又说了五分钟。最后她说：「好了，你去忙吧，小王。」我说：「妈，我不是小王，我是你儿子。」她说：「啊？那我刚才说的那些话，你也要听！」
text_pinyin: Māma gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà, shuō le hěn duō huà. Tā shuō:「Nǐ yào duō chī fàn, bié tài lèi le, zǎo diǎnr shuì jiào.」Wǒ shuō:「Hǎo de hǎo de.」Tā yòu shuō le wǔ fēnzhōng. Zuìhòu tā shuō:「Hǎo le, nǐ qù máng ba, Xiǎo Wáng.」Wǒ shuō:「Mā, wǒ búshì Xiǎo Wáng, wǒ shì nǐ érzi.」Tā shuō:「À? Nà wǒ gāngcái shuō de nàxiē huà, nǐ yě yào tīng!」
text_en: Mom called me and talked a lot. She said: 'You should eat more, don't get too tired, go to sleep early.' I said: 'Okay okay.' She talked for another five minutes. Finally she said: 'Alright, go get busy, Xiao Wang.' I said: 'Mom, I'm not Xiao Wang. I'm your son.' She said: 'Oh? Well, everything I just said — you should listen too!'

questions.q_zh: 妈妈在电话里说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did Mom say on the phone?

options.text: 多吃饭，别太累，早点儿睡
options.pinyin: duō chī fàn, bié tài lèi, zǎo diǎnr shuì
options.text_en: eat more, don't get too tired, sleep early

questions.explanation: Mom said 'eat more, don't get too tired, go to sleep early' — classic mom advice.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈以为在跟谁说话？
questions.q_en: Who did Mom think she was talking to?
questions.explanation: Mom realized partway through she'd dialed the wrong number — she meant to call Xiao Wang.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈知道打错了以后说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did Mom say after realizing the mistake?
questions.explanation: Even after realizing the mistake, Mom said the advice still applies — 'you should listen too.'

title: Grandma's Dumplings
text_zh: 每年过年的时候，奶奶都做饺子。她做的饺子比饭馆的好吃。我问她为什么，她说：「因为我知道你们每个人喜欢吃什么。」哥哥的饺子里面放的多一些，我的放的少一些。虽然看起来都一样，但是吃的时候就知道了。
text_pinyin: Měi nián guònián de shíhou, nǎinai dōu zuò jiǎozi. Tā zuò de jiǎozi bǐ fànguǎn de hǎochī. Wǒ wèn tā wèishénme, tā shuō:「Yīnwèi wǒ zhīdào nǐmen měi gè rén xǐhuan chī shénme.」Gēge de jiǎozi lǐmiàn fàng de duō yìxiē, wǒ de fàng de shǎo yìxiē. Suīrán kàn qǐlái dōu yíyàng, dànshì chī de shíhou jiù zhīdào le.
text_en: Every New Year, Grandma makes dumplings. Her dumplings taste better than restaurant ones. I asked her why. She said: 'Because I know what each of you likes to eat.' My brother's dumplings have more filling, mine have less. They look the same, but you can tell when you eat them.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话你能了解到人物什么？
questions.q_en: What can you learn about the character from this passage?

options.text: 他/她是一个有感情的人
options.pinyin: tā shì yí gè yǒu gǎnqíng de rén
options.text_en: they are a person with feelings

questions.explanation: Grandma tailors each dumpling to each person — that personal touch shows deep caring.

questions.q_zh: 奶奶说她的饺子为什么好吃？
questions.q_en: Why does Grandma say her dumplings taste good?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi yòng le hǎo de dōngxi

options.text: 因为她知道每个人喜欢什么
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā zhīdào měi gè rén xǐhuan shénme
options.text_en: because she knows what everyone likes

options.pinyin: yīnwèi zuò le hěn duō nián
options.text_en: because she's made them many years
because they're bigger than restaurant ones

questions.explanation: She said 'I know what each of you likes to eat' — that's why they taste better.

questions.q_zh: 哥哥和「我」的饺子有什么不同？
questions.q_en: How are the brother's and 'my' dumplings different?
questions.explanation: Brother likes more filling, the narrator likes less — Grandma adjusts each one.

title: The Noodle Shop Regulars
text_zh: 我家楼下有一个小面条店。老板认识每一个老客人。有的人一进来，他就知道要做什么。「老张，还是大碗牛肉面，不要辣的，对吧？」老张笑着点头。我觉得这比大饭馆好——在大饭馆，没有人记得你是谁。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā lóu xià yǒu yí gè xiǎo miàntiáo diàn. Lǎobǎn rènshi měi yí gè lǎo kèrén. Yǒude rén yí jìnlái, tā jiù zhīdào yào zuò shénme.「Lǎo Zhāng, háishi dà wǎn niúròu miàn, búyào là de, duì ba?」Lǎo Zhāng xiàozhe diǎntóu. Wǒ juéde zhè bǐ dà fànguǎn hǎo——zài dà fànguǎn, méiyǒu rén jìde nǐ shì shéi.
text_en: There's a small noodle shop downstairs from my home. The owner knows every regular. Some people walk in and he already knows what to make. 'Old Zhang, large bowl of beef noodles, no spice, right?' Old Zhang smiles and nods. I think this is better than a big restaurant — in a big restaurant, nobody remembers who you are.

questions.q_zh: 老板怎么知道老客人要什么？
questions.q_en: How does the owner know what regulars want?

options.pinyin: tā rènshi měi yí gè lǎo kèrén

questions.explanation: People walk in and he already knows what to make — he's memorized every regular.

questions.q_zh: 老张要什么面？
questions.q_en: What noodles does Old Zhang want?

options.pinyin: xiǎo wǎn niúròu miàn, là de
dà wǎn niúròu miàn, búyào là de

questions.explanation: The owner calls out 'Old Zhang, large beef noodles, no spice' before Zhang orders.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得小面条店比大饭馆好在哪里？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think the small shop is better than a big restaurant?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi yǒu rén jìde nǐ shì shéi
options.text_en: because someone remembers who you are

questions.explanation: Someone remembering your order means they remember you — that's more personal than a big restaurant.

title: Learning to Cook
text_zh: 我第一次做饭的时候，做了西红柿炒鸡蛋。看起来很简单，但是我做的不好。鸡蛋炒得太老了，西红柿放得太少了。我给爸爸吃，他说：「比我第一次做的好。」我知道他是在说好话，但是我还是很高兴。
text_pinyin: Wǒ dì yī cì zuò fàn de shíhou, zuò le xīhóngshì chǎo jīdàn. Kàn qǐlái hěn jiǎndān, dànshì wǒ zuò de bù hǎo. Jīdàn chǎo de tài lǎo le, xīhóngshì fàng de tài shǎo le. Wǒ gěi bàba chī, tā shuō:「Bǐ wǒ dì yī cì zuò de hǎo.」Wǒ zhīdào tā shì zài shuō hǎo huà, dànshì wǒ háishi hěn gāoxìng.
text_en: The first time I cooked, I made scrambled eggs with tomato. It looked simple, but I didn't do well. The eggs were overcooked, and I used too few tomatoes. I gave it to Dad to eat. He said: 'Better than my first time.' I knew he was being nice, but I was still happy.

questions.q_zh: 「我」第一次做了什么菜？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' cook the first time?
questions.explanation: 第一次做饭,我做了西红柿炒鸡蛋 — scrambled eggs with tomato was the first dish attempted.

questions.q_zh: 做得不好的地方是什么？
questions.q_en: What went wrong?

options.pinyin: jīdàn tài lǎo, xīhóngshì tài shǎo

questions.explanation: The eggs were overcooked and there weren't enough tomatoes.

questions.q_zh: 爸爸说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did Dad say?

options.pinyin: bǐ wǒ dì yī cì zuò de hǎo

questions.explanation: Dad encouraged by saying 'better than my first time' — kind and supportive.

title: The Man Who Watches Trains
text_zh: 火车站旁边有一个小饭馆。每天下午，一个男人坐在门口，看火车从左边到右边，又从右边到左边。饭馆的人问他：「你为什么每天都来？」他笑了笑，说：「因为火车知道去哪里，我不知道。」
text_pinyin: Huǒchēzhàn pángbiān yǒu yí gè xiǎo fànguǎn. Měi tiān xiàwǔ, yí gè nánrén zuò zài ménkǒu, kàn huǒchē cóng zuǒbiān dào yòubiān, yòu cóng yòubiān dào zuǒbiān. Fànguǎn de rén wèn tā:「Nǐ wèishénme měi tiān dōu lái?」Tā xiào le xiào, shuō:「Yīnwèi huǒchē zhīdào qù nǎlǐ, wǒ bù zhīdào.」
text_en: Next to the train station there's a small restaurant. Every afternoon, a man sits at the entrance watching trains go from left to right, then right to left. The restaurant people ask him: 'Why do you come every day?' He smiles and says: 'Because the trains know where they're going. I don't.'

questions.q_zh: 这个场景给你的第一印象是什么？
questions.q_en: What is your first impression of this scene?
questions.explanation: A man quietly watching trains at a small restaurant — the scene feels calm and beautiful.

questions.q_zh: 男人为什么每天来？
questions.q_en: Why does the man come every day?

options.text_en: because he works at the restaurant
because he wants to take the train

options.text: 因为火车知道去哪里，他不知道
options.pinyin: yīnwèi huǒchē zhīdào qù nǎlǐ, tā bù zhīdào
options.text_en: because trains know where to go, he doesn't

questions.explanation: He said 'trains know where to go, but I don't' — that's his reason for watching.

questions.q_zh: 男人说话的时候，他怎么样？
questions.q_en: How is the man when he speaks?
questions.explanation: 他笑了 describes his expression — a smile while speaking, suggesting contentment rather than sadness.

title: Afternoon in the Teahouse
text_zh: 这个茶馆很旧了，但是每天都有人来。有的老人从早上坐到下午，从来不说话。他们喝茶，看报纸，有时候看看窗外面。外面的路上，人走得很快。里面的人走得很慢。我觉得这里的时间和外面的不一样。
text_pinyin: Zhège cháguǎn hěn jiù le, dànshì měi tiān dōu yǒu rén lái. Yǒude lǎorén cóng zǎoshang zuò dào xiàwǔ, cónglái bù shuōhuà. Tāmen hē chá, kàn bàozhǐ, yǒu shíhou kànkan chuāng wàimiàn. Wàimiàn de lù shàng, rén zǒu de hěn kuài. Lǐmiàn de rén zǒu de hěn màn. Wǒ juéde zhèlǐ de shíjiān hé wàimiàn de bù yíyàng.
text_en: This teahouse is old, but people come every day. Some old people sit from morning until afternoon without ever speaking. They drink tea, read newspapers, sometimes look out the window. On the road outside, people walk fast. Inside, people move slowly. I feel the time here is different from outside.

questions.q_zh: 老人在茶馆做什么？
questions.q_en: What do the old people do in the teahouse?
questions.explanation: 喝茶、看报纸 — these are quiet, unhurried activities that fill their long hours at the teahouse.

questions.q_zh: 茶馆里面和外面有什么不一样？
questions.q_en: What's different between inside and outside the teahouse?

options.pinyin: lǐmiàn hěn rè, wàimiàn hěn lěng
options.text_en: inside is hot, outside is cold
inside is slow, outside is fast
inside is big, outside is small
inside is new, outside is old

questions.explanation: Inside the teahouse is slow; outside people rush by — two different paces of life.

questions.q_zh: 这个茶馆是新的还是旧的？
questions.q_en: Is this teahouse new or old?
questions.explanation: 这个茶馆很旧 opens the description — 旧 means old/worn, establishing its age from the first line.

title: The Cat on the Roof
text_zh: 我房间的窗外面，有一只黑猫。它每天早上都在对面的房顶上走。从左走到右，又从右走到左。有时候它停下来，看下面的路。我觉得它也在看我。我们每天早上都这样，我看它，它看我。
text_pinyin: Wǒ fángjiān de chuāng wàimiàn, yǒu yì zhī hēi māo. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang dōu zài duìmiàn de fángdǐng shàng zǒu. Cóng zuǒ zǒu dào yòu, yòu cóng yòu zǒu dào zuǒ. Yǒu shíhou tā tíng xiàlái, kàn xiàmiàn de lù. Wǒ juéde tā yě zài kàn wǒ. Wǒmen měi tiān zǎoshang dōu zhèyàng, wǒ kàn tā, tā kàn wǒ.
text_en: Outside my room window, there's a black cat. Every morning it walks on the rooftop across the way. Left to right, then right to left. Sometimes it stops and looks at the road below. I think it's also watching me. Every morning we're like this — I watch it, it watches me.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么每天早上都看那只猫？
questions.q_en: Why does the narrator watch the cat every morning?

options.text: 因为这已经成了一种安静的习惯
options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhè yǐjīng chéng le yì zhǒng ānjìng de xíguàn
options.text_en: because it has become a quiet habit

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā xiǎng bǎ māo dài huí jiā
options.text_en: because he wants to take the cat home

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā juéde māo hěn kěpà
options.text_en: because he finds the cat scary

options.text: 因为他没有别的事情做
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā méiyǒu bié de shìqíng zuò
options.text_en: because he has nothing else to do

questions.explanation: The narrator watches the cat every morning without thinking — it's become a quiet daily habit.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的主要感受是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the main feeling of this passage?
questions.explanation: The cat and the narrator share a silent morning routine — it's gentle and comforting.

questions.q_zh: 「我」和猫每天早上做什么？
questions.q_en: What do 'I' and the cat do every morning?
questions.explanation: 有时候它停下来看下面 and the narrator watches from the window — both observe each other as part of the morning routine.

title: A Bench by the Lake
text_zh: 公园里有一个很长的椅子，在湖旁边。白天，很多人坐在那里。但是晚上七点以后，只有一个女人来。她不看手机，不看书，只是坐着看水。她每天都穿白色的衣服。我很想知道她在想什么，但是我从来没有问过。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yí gè hěn cháng de yǐzi, zài hú pángbiān. Báitiān, hěn duō rén zuò zài nàlǐ. Dànshì wǎnshang qī diǎn yǐhòu, zhǐ yǒu yí gè nǚrén lái. Tā bú kàn shǒujī, bú kàn shū, zhǐ shì zuòzhe kàn shuǐ. Tā měi tiān dōu chuān báisè de yīfu. Wǒ hěn xiǎng zhīdào tā zài xiǎng shénme, dànshì wǒ cónglái méiyǒu wèn guò.
text_en: In the park there's a long bench by the lake. During the day, many people sit there. But after seven in the evening, only one woman comes. She doesn't look at her phone or read — she just sits watching the water. She always wears white clothes. I want to know what she's thinking, but I've never asked.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中，什么是最有意思的部分？
questions.q_en: What is the most interesting part of this passage?

options.text: 平常中有不平常的东西
options.pinyin: píngcháng zhōng yǒu bù píngcháng de dōngxi
fāshēng le hěn dà de shì

questions.explanation: A park bench busy by day but claimed by one silent woman at night — quietly unusual.

questions.q_zh: 女人的行为说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the woman's behavior suggest?

options.text: 她需要一个安静的地方想事情
options.pinyin: tā xūyào yí gè ānjìng de dìfang xiǎng shìqíng
options.text_en: she needs a quiet place to think

options.pinyin: tā zài děng yí gè rén lái
options.text_en: she is waiting for someone to come

options.pinyin: tā bù xǐhuan báitiān de gōngyuán
options.text_en: she doesn't like the park during the day
she is bored and has no friends

questions.explanation: She comes alone after 7pm, doesn't talk or use her phone — she needs a quiet place to think.

questions.q_zh: 「我」问过女人吗？
questions.q_en: Has 'I' ever asked the woman?
questions.explanation: 我从来没有问过她 — 从来没有 ('never once') makes clear no conversation has ever happened.

title: Two Cups
text_zh: 咖啡店里，一个女人面前有两个杯子。一个是她的，一个是谁的？对面的椅子上没有人。她有时候看看那个杯子，有时候看看门。咖啡慢慢地冷了。她等了很长时间，最后一个人也没有来。她站起来的时候，没有拿走那个杯子。
text_pinyin: Kāfēidiàn lǐ, yí gè nǚrén miànqián yǒu liǎng gè bēizi. Yí gè shì tā de, yí gè shì shéi de? Duìmiàn de yǐzi shàng méiyǒu rén. Tā yǒu shíhou kànkan nàge bēizi, yǒu shíhou kànkan mén. Kāfēi mànmàn de lěng le. Tā děng le hěn cháng shíjiān, zuìhòu yí gè rén yě méiyǒu lái. Tā zhàn qǐlái de shíhou, méiyǒu ná zǒu nàge bēizi.
text_en: In a coffee shop, a woman has two cups in front of her. One is hers — whose is the other? The chair across from her is empty. Sometimes she looks at that cup, sometimes at the door. The coffee slowly grows cold. She waits a long time. In the end, nobody comes. When she stands up, she doesn't take that cup.

questions.q_zh: 对面的椅子上有人吗？
questions.q_en: Is anyone in the chair across from her?
questions.explanation: The chair across from her is empty — nobody is sitting there.

questions.q_zh: 从前后的对比可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we learn from the before-and-after contrast?

options.pinyin: jīnglì dàilái le xīn de rènshi

questions.explanation: She came with two cups but leaves the second behind — the contrast reveals new understanding about loss.

questions.q_zh: 最后她走的时候怎么样？
questions.q_en: What happens when she finally leaves?

options.pinyin: tā ná zǒu le liǎng gè bēizi
tā méiyǒu ná zǒu nàge bēizi
tā yòu yào le yí gè bēizi

questions.explanation: When she left, she didn't take that second cup — she left it on the table.

title: The Shoe Repairman
text_zh: 路旁边有一个老人，他帮别人修鞋。他的手很快，但是他从来不说话。人们把鞋给他，他看一看，就开始做了。做完了，他告诉你多少钱。虽然他不笑，但是每双鞋他都修得很好。我觉得他是用手说话的人。
text_pinyin: Lù pángbiān yǒu yí gè lǎorén, tā bāng biéren xiū xié. Tā de shǒu hěn kuài, dànshì tā cónglái bù shuōhuà. Rénmen bǎ xié gěi tā, tā kàn yi kàn, jiù kāishǐ zuò le. Zuò wán le, tā gàosu nǐ duōshao qián. Suīrán tā bú xiào, dànshì měi shuāng xié tā dōu xiū de hěn hǎo. Wǒ juéde tā shì yòng shǒu shuōhuà de rén.
text_en: By the road there's an old man who repairs shoes for people. His hands are quick, but he never speaks. People give him their shoes, he takes a look, then starts working. When he's done, he tells you the price. Although he doesn't smile, every pair of shoes he fixes well. I think he's someone who speaks with his hands.

questions.q_zh: 这个场景给你的第一印象是什么？
questions.q_en: What is your first impression of this scene?
questions.explanation: An old man quietly repairing shoes by the road — the scene feels peaceful and still.

questions.q_zh: 虽然老人不笑，但是……
questions.q_en: Although the old man doesn't smile, ...
questions.explanation: He never smiles or speaks, but every pair of shoes comes back well-repaired.

questions.q_zh: 故事里最重要的不是发生了什么，而是什么？
questions.q_en: The most important thing in this story is not what happened, but what?
questions.explanation: The story focuses on how the narrator perceives the old man's quiet dignity — mood over events.

title: Night Bus
text_zh: 晚上十点的公共汽车上，人很少。一个女人靠着窗户睡着了，手里还拿着手机。一个学生在看书，但是眼睛快要闭上了。司机开得很慢，好像他也不想快到。车外面的路灯一个一个过去了。这是一天里最安静的时候。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shí diǎn de gōnggòng qìchē shàng, rén hěn shǎo. Yí gè nǚrén kàozhe chuānghu shuìzháo le, shǒu lǐ hái názhe shǒujī. Yí gè xuéshēng zài kàn shū, dànshì yǎnjing kuài yào bì shàng le. Sījī kāi de hěn màn, hǎoxiàng tā yě bù xiǎng kuài dào. Chē wàimiàn de lùdēng yí gè yí gè guòqù le. Zhè shì yì tiān lǐ zuì ānjìng de shíhou.
text_en: On the ten o'clock bus, there are few people. A woman has fallen asleep leaning against the window, still holding her phone. A student is reading, but his eyes are about to close. The driver goes slowly, as if he doesn't want to arrive quickly either. The streetlights outside pass by one by one. This is the quietest moment of the day.

questions.q_zh: 公共汽车上的人多吗？
questions.q_en: Are there many people on the bus?
questions.explanation: 人很少 describes the ten o'clock bus — late evening means few riders remain.

questions.q_zh: 这个场景给你的第一印象是什么？
questions.q_en: What is your first impression of this scene?
questions.explanation: A sleeping woman, a drowsy student, a slow driver — the scene is quiet and still.

questions.q_zh: 司机开车开得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the driver drive?
questions.explanation: 司机开得很慢 — the slow driving matches the late-night atmosphere, as if preserving the passengers' sleep.

title: The Sound of Rain on Leaves
text_zh: 今天下午又下雨了。我坐在房间里，窗户开着。外面有一棵大树，雨打在树上，声音很好听。比音乐还好听。楼下有人快跑过去了，但是我不想动。有时候，什么都不做也很好。就这样听着雨，从三点到五点。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān xiàwǔ yòu xià yǔ le. Wǒ zuò zài fángjiān lǐ, chuānghu kāizhe. Wàimiàn yǒu yì kē dà shù, yǔ dǎ zài shù shàng, shēngyīn hěn hǎotīng. Bǐ yīnyuè hái hǎotīng. Lóu xià yǒu rén kuài pǎo guòqù le, dànshì wǒ bù xiǎng dòng. Yǒu shíhou, shénme dōu bú zuò yě hěn hǎo. Jiù zhèyàng tīngzhe yǔ, cóng sān diǎn dào wǔ diǎn.
text_en: It rained again this afternoon. I sit in my room with the window open. Outside there's a big tree; the rain hitting the leaves sounds beautiful. More beautiful than music. Downstairs someone runs past quickly, but I don't want to move. Sometimes doing nothing at all is fine. Just listening to rain like this, from three to five.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得雨的声音怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel about the sound of rain?
questions.explanation: 比音乐还好听 — the comparison 比...还 ('even more than') expresses that rain on leaves surpasses music in beauty.

questions.q_zh: 「我」听雨听了多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long did 'I' listen to the rain?
questions.explanation: The narrator sat and listened for 'two hours' without getting bored.

questions.q_zh: 这段话说明了什么道理？
questions.q_en: What principle does this passage illustrate?

options.text: 简单的生活也能很快乐
options.pinyin: jiǎndān de shēnghuó yě néng hěn kuàilè
options.text_en: a simple life can also be happy

options.pinyin: yí gè rén bù néng kuàilè

questions.explanation: Two hours of just listening to rain and feeling content — simplicity can bring happiness.

title: Two Sides of One Street
text_zh: 这条路很有意思。左边都是新的商店，很贵，里面的人穿得很好。右边都是旧的小店，很便宜，门口坐着老人。从左边走到右边，只要十步，但是好像从一个地方到了另一个地方。我喜欢在右边吃面条，然后去左边买咖啡。
text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù hěn yǒu yìsi. Zuǒbiān dōu shì xīn de shāngdiàn, hěn guì, lǐmiàn de rén chuān de hěn hǎo. Yòubiān dōu shì jiù de xiǎodiàn, hěn piányi, ménkǒu zuòzhe lǎorén. Cóng zuǒbiān zǒu dào yòubiān, zhǐ yào shí bù, dànshì hǎoxiàng cóng yí gè dìfang dào le lìng yí gè dìfang. Wǒ xǐhuan zài yòubiān chī miàntiáo, ránhòu qù zuǒbiān mǎi kāfēi.
text_en: This street is interesting. The left side is all new shops, expensive, people inside dressed well. The right side is all old little shops, cheap, old people sitting in doorways. From left to right is only ten steps, but it feels like going from one world to another. I like eating noodles on the right side, then going to the left for coffee.

questions.q_zh: 路的左边和右边有什么不一样？
questions.q_en: What's different about the left and right sides?

options.pinyin: zuǒbiān xīn, guì; yòubiān jiù, piányi
options.text_en: left is new, expensive; right is old, cheap
left is far, right is near

options.text: 左边有学校，右边有医院
options.pinyin: zuǒbiān yǒu xuéxiào, yòubiān yǒu yīyuàn
options.text_en: left has school, right has hospital

questions.explanation: Left side: new, expensive shops. Right side: old, cheap shops. Just ten steps apart.

questions.q_zh: 从左边到右边要走多远？
questions.q_en: How far is it from left to right?
questions.explanation: 只有十步 — just ten steps separate the expensive new shops from the cheap old ones, emphasizing how close two worlds are.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的主要感受是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the main feeling of this passage?
questions.explanation: Old and new coexisting on one street — the passage treats both sides with warmth.

title: The Overnight Bookstore
text_zh: 我家旁边有一个书店，它从来不关门。晚上十二点去，还有人在里面看书。有的人坐在地上，有的人靠着书架睡着了。店里的灯不太亮，很暖。我有时候晚上睡不着，就去那里坐一坐。不买书也没关系。这个书店让我觉得，这个城市还是有一些好的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā pángbiān yǒu yí gè shūdiàn, tā cónglái bù guān mén. Wǎnshang shí'èr diǎn qù, hái yǒu rén zài lǐmiàn kàn shū. Yǒude rén zuò zài dì shàng, yǒude rén kàozhe shūjià shuìzháo le. Diàn lǐ de dēng bú tài liàng, hěn nuǎn. Wǒ yǒu shíhou wǎnshang shuì bu zháo, jiù qù nàlǐ zuò yi zuò. Bù mǎi shū yě méi guānxi. Zhège shūdiàn ràng wǒ juéde, zhège chéngshì háishi yǒu yìxiē hǎo de dìfang.
text_en: Near my home there's a bookstore that never closes. Go at midnight and people are still reading inside. Some sit on the floor, some have fallen asleep leaning against shelves. The lights are dim and warm. Sometimes when I can't sleep, I go sit there for a bit. It's fine not to buy anything. This bookstore makes me feel the city still has some good places.

questions.q_zh: 观察的行为说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the act of observing suggest?
questions.explanation: The narrator notices people sleeping on the floor, warm lights — close attention to the scene.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么去书店？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' go to the bookstore?

options.text: 睡不着的时候去坐一坐
options.pinyin: shuì bu zháo de shíhou qù zuò yi zuò
options.text_en: to sit for a bit when can't sleep

questions.explanation: 睡不着的时候去坐一会儿 — insomnia drives the visit, not a need to buy books.

questions.q_zh: 书店让「我」有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How does the bookstore make 'I' feel?

options.pinyin: chéngshì hái yǒu hǎo de dìfang
options.text_en: the city still has good places

questions.explanation: The bookstore makes the narrator feel 'the city still has good places.'

title: The Fruit Seller's Corner
text_zh: 每天早上，一个女人在路口卖水果。西瓜、苹果，还有别的。她的声音很大：「便宜！便宜！今天的西瓜很好！」旁边的人都认识她了。下雨的时候她也在，很热的时候也在。有一天她没来，旁边卖报纸的人说：「今天觉得少了什么。」
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang, yí gè nǚrén zài lùkǒu mài shuǐguǒ. Xīguā, píngguǒ, hái yǒu bié de. Tā de shēngyīn hěn dà:「Piányi! Piányi! Jīntiān de xīguā hěn hǎo!」Pángbiān de rén dōu rènshi tā le. Xià yǔ de shíhou tā yě zài, hěn rè de shíhou yě zài. Yǒu yì tiān tā méi lái, pángbiān mài bàozhǐ de rén shuō:「Jīntiān juéde shǎo le shénme.」
text_en: Every morning, a woman sells fruit at the corner. Watermelons, apples, and more. Her voice is loud: 'Cheap! Cheap! Today's watermelons are good!' Everyone nearby knows her. She's there when it rains, there when it's hot. One day she didn't come, and the newspaper seller next to her said: 'Today it feels like something's missing.'

questions.q_zh: 这种感情最可能来自什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most likely source of this feeling?
questions.explanation: The newspaper seller misses her when she's gone — her daily presence is tied to familiar routine and memory.

questions.q_zh: 下雨的时候她在吗？
questions.q_en: Is she there when it rains?
questions.explanation: 下雨天她也在 — rain or shine, her presence at the corner doesn't change.

questions.q_zh: 她没来的那天，卖报纸的人觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did the newspaper seller feel the day she didn't come?
questions.explanation: The newspaper seller said 'today felt like something was missing' when she didn't come.

title: Upstairs, Downstairs
text_zh: 我住在三楼。楼上四楼住着一个人，每天晚上很晚回来。我听得到他开门、走路、洗东西的声音。楼下二楼的人很早起来，每天早上六点就开始做饭。我闻得到。虽然我们从来没见过面，但是我知道他们什么时候起来，什么时候睡觉。住在一起的人，不一定认识。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù zài sān lóu. Lóu shàng sì lóu zhùzhe yí gè rén, měi tiān wǎnshang hěn wǎn huílái. Wǒ tīng de dào tā kāi mén, zǒulù, xǐ dōngxi de shēngyīn. Lóu xià èr lóu de rén hěn zǎo qǐlái, měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn jiù kāishǐ zuò fàn. Wǒ wén de dào. Suīrán wǒmen cónglái méi jiàn guò miàn, dànshì wǒ zhīdào tāmen shénme shíhou qǐlái, shénme shíhou shuìjiào. Zhù zài yìqǐ de rén, bù yídìng rènshi.
text_en: I live on the third floor. Upstairs on the fourth floor, someone comes back very late every night. I can hear them open the door, walk, wash things. The person below on the second floor wakes early — starts cooking at six every morning. I can smell it. Although we've never met, I know when they wake up and when they sleep. People who live together don't necessarily know each other.

questions.q_zh: 这段话说明了什么道理？
questions.q_en: What principle does this passage illustrate?

options.text: 简单的生活也能很快乐
options.pinyin: jiǎndān de shēnghuó yě néng hěn kuàilè
options.text_en: a simple life can also be happy

options.pinyin: yí gè rén bù néng kuàilè

questions.explanation: Hearing footsteps above and smelling cooking below — ordinary life has its own quiet meaning.

questions.q_zh: 「我」怎么知道二楼的人做饭？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' know the second-floor person is cooking?
questions.explanation: 我能闻到 — the cooking smell drifts between floors, so scent is the evidence, not sight or sound.

questions.q_zh: 他们见过面吗？
questions.q_en: Have they met?
questions.explanation: 我们从来没有见过面 — despite sharing a building, they know each other only through sounds and smells.

title: The Bike Lane at Rush Hour
text_zh: 早上八点，自行车路上有很多人。大家都骑得很快，从这边到公司去。有的人一只手骑车，一只手拿着早饭。有的人还在打电话。有一个人骑得很慢，后面的人都很着急。但是她不着急。她一边骑车，一边看路旁边的花。我想：也许她是对的。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang bā diǎn, zìxíngchē lù shàng yǒu hěn duō rén. Dàjiā dōu qí de hěn kuài, cóng zhèbiān dào gōngsī qù. Yǒude rén yì zhī shǒu qí chē, yì zhī shǒu názhe zǎofàn. Yǒude rén hái zài dǎ diànhuà. Yǒu yí gè rén qí de hěn màn, hòumiàn de rén dōu hěn zhāojí. Dànshì tā bù zhāojí. Tā yìbiān qí chē, yìbiān kàn lù pángbiān de huā. Wǒ xiǎng: yěxǔ tā shì duì de.
text_en: At eight in the morning, the bike lane is packed. Everyone rides fast, heading to their companies. Some ride with one hand, holding breakfast in the other. Some are on the phone. One person rides very slowly — everyone behind is anxious. But she isn't. She rides while looking at the flowers by the road. I think: maybe she's the one who's right.

questions.q_zh: 大家为什么骑得很快？
questions.q_en: Why is everyone riding fast?
questions.explanation: 大家骑得很快,去自己的公司 — heading to their companies explains the rush; it's the morning commute.

questions.q_zh: 食物在这段话中代表什么？
questions.q_en: What does food represent in this passage?
questions.explanation: One hand holds breakfast bought by someone else — the food represents someone's care.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的语气/氛围是怎样的？
questions.q_en: What is the tone/atmosphere of this passage?
questions.explanation: Thousands rushing silently, one hand on breakfast — the mood is still and contemplative.

title: The Hospital Waiting Room
text_zh: 医院的等候室里，大家都在等。有的人看手机，有的人看报纸，有的人什么都不做，只是坐着。一个妈妈让她的孩子别哭，但是孩子还是哭。一个老人闭着眼睛，我不知道他是在睡觉还是在想事情。这里每个人都有一个问题，但是没有人说出来。大家只是等。
text_pinyin: Yīyuàn de děnghòushì lǐ, dàjiā dōu zài děng. Yǒude rén kàn shǒujī, yǒude rén kàn bàozhǐ, yǒude rén shénme dōu bú zuò, zhǐ shì zuòzhe. Yí gè māma ràng tā de háizi bié kū, dànshì háizi háishi kū. Yí gè lǎorén bìzhe yǎnjing, wǒ bù zhīdào tā shì zài shuìjiào háishi zài xiǎng shìqing. Zhèlǐ měi gè rén dōu yǒu yí gè wèntí, dànshì méiyǒu rén shuō chūlái. Dàjiā zhǐ shì děng.
text_en: In the hospital waiting room, everyone waits. Some look at phones, some read newspapers, some do nothing — just sit. A mother tells her child not to cry, but the child still cries. An old man has his eyes closed; I don't know if he's sleeping or thinking. Everyone here has a problem, but nobody says it aloud. They just wait.

questions.q_zh: 这种感情最可能来自什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most likely source of this feeling?
questions.explanation: The quiet waiting room with unspoken worries stirs a sense of shared human experience and memory.

questions.q_zh: 读完这段话，你觉得作者想说什么？
questions.q_en: After reading this, what do you think the author wants to say?

options.text: 平凡的日子也值得珍惜
options.pinyin: píngfán de rìzi yě zhíde zhēnxī

options.text_en: should move to a big city

questions.explanation: Everyone waiting quietly with their own problem — the passage finds meaning in ordinary shared moments.

questions.q_zh: 等候室里的人怎么样？
questions.q_en: How are the people in the waiting room?

options.text: 每个人都有问题，但没人说出来
options.pinyin: měi gè rén dōu yǒu wèntí, dàn méi rén shuō chūlái
options.text_en: everyone has a problem but nobody says it

questions.explanation: 每个人都有问题,但是没有人说 — everyone carries a health concern but keeps it private in the shared silence.

title: The School After Hours
text_zh: 下午四点，学生们都走了。教室里只有桌子和椅子。黑板上还有老师写的字。地上有一支笔，不知道是谁的。窗户还开着，风吹进来。白天这里有很多声音，现在什么声音都没有了。学校在白天和晚上是两个不一样的地方。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ sì diǎn, xuéshēngmen dōu zǒu le. Jiàoshì lǐ zhǐ yǒu zhuōzi hé yǐzi. Hēibǎn shàng hái yǒu lǎoshī xiě de zì. Dì shàng yǒu yì zhī bǐ, bù zhīdào shì shéi de. Chuānghu hái kāizhe, fēng chuī jìnlái. Báitiān zhèlǐ yǒu hěn duō shēngyīn, xiànzài shénme shēngyīn dōu méiyǒu le. Xuéxiào zài báitiān hé wǎnshang shì liǎng gè bù yíyàng de dìfang.
text_en: At four in the afternoon, all the students have left. The classroom has only desks and chairs. On the blackboard, the teacher's writing remains. On the floor there's a pen — no one knows whose. The window is still open; wind blows in. During the day this place is full of sounds. Now there's nothing. School during the day and at night are two different places.

questions.q_zh: 这段话说明了什么道理？
questions.q_en: What principle does this passage illustrate?

options.text: 简单的生活也能很快乐
options.pinyin: jiǎndān de shēnghuó yě néng hěn kuàilè
options.text_en: a simple life can also be happy

options.pinyin: yí gè rén bù néng kuàilè

questions.explanation: An empty classroom with leftover traces of the day — simple moments carry quiet meaning.

questions.q_zh: 地上有什么？
questions.q_en: What's on the floor?
questions.explanation: 地上有一支笔 — a pen remains on the floor, a small trace of the students who were there hours ago.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这个人以后会怎样？
questions.q_en: What do you think will happen to this person in the future?

options.pinyin: huì bān dào bié de dìfang

questions.explanation: The calm, routine nature of the scene suggests life will keep going this way peacefully.

title: The Post Office Queue
text_zh: 邮局里只有两个窗口，但是有二十个人在等。大家都站着，一个在另一个后面。前面的人很慢，后面的人很着急。一个老人要寄东西，他准备了很多纸，但是写错了。工作的人让他再写一次。后面的人都看着他，但是没有人帮他。我帮他写完了。他对我笑了笑，说了一声谢谢。
text_pinyin: Yóujú lǐ zhǐ yǒu liǎng gè chuāngkǒu, dànshì yǒu èrshí gè rén zài děng. Dàjiā dōu zhànzhe, yí gè zài lìng yí gè hòumiàn. Qiánmiàn de rén hěn màn, hòumiàn de rén hěn zhāojí. Yí gè lǎorén yào jì dōngxi, tā zhǔnbèi le hěn duō zhǐ, dànshì xiě cuò le. Gōngzuò de rén ràng tā zài xiě yí cì. Hòumiàn de rén dōu kànzhe tā, dànshì méiyǒu rén bāng tā. Wǒ bāng tā xiě wán le. Tā duì wǒ xiào le xiào, shuō le yì shēng xièxie.
text_en: The post office has only two windows, but twenty people are waiting. Everyone stands in line, one behind another. The people in front are slow; the people behind are anxious. An old man wants to mail something. He prepared lots of papers but wrote it wrong. The worker tells him to write it again. Everyone behind watches him, but nobody helps. I help him finish writing. He smiles at me and says thank you.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这个人以后会怎样？
questions.q_en: What do you think will happen to this person in the future?

options.pinyin: huì bān dào bié de dìfang

questions.explanation: The narrator helps the old man and waits patiently — this peaceful patience will likely continue.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的语气/氛围是怎样的？
questions.q_en: What is the tone/atmosphere of this passage?
questions.explanation: Twenty people waiting silently, small acts of kindness — the tone is quiet and thoughtful.

questions.q_zh: 谁帮了老人？
questions.q_en: Who helped the old man?
questions.explanation: 我帮他 — the narrator stepped in to assist, identifying themselves as the one who helped.

title: The Wrong Ticket
text_zh: 我在火车上找到了我的座位，但是上面坐着一个男人。我给他看我的票，他也给我看他的票。一样的座位号。我们都觉得很奇怪。我又看了一下我的票——日期是对的，时间是对的，但是火车号不对。这不是我的火车。我的火车已经走了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài huǒchē shàng zhǎo dào le wǒ de zuòwèi, dànshì shàngmiàn zuòzhe yí gè nánrén. Wǒ gěi tā kàn wǒ de piào, tā yě gěi wǒ kàn tā de piào. Yíyàng de zuòwèi hào. Wǒmen dōu juéde hěn qíguài. Wǒ yòu kàn le yíxià wǒ de piào——rìqī shì duì de, shíjiān shì duì de, dànshì huǒchē hào búduì. Zhè búshì wǒ de huǒchē. Wǒ de huǒchē yǐjīng zǒu le.
text_en: I find my seat on the train, but a man is sitting there. I show him my ticket; he shows me his. Same seat number. We're both confused. I look at my ticket again — the date is right, the time is right, but the train number is wrong. This isn't my train. My train has already left.

questions.q_zh: 为什么两个人的座位号一样？
questions.q_en: Why do both people have the same seat number?

options.text_en: the man sat in the wrong seat
the station sold a wrong ticket

questions.explanation: Looking more carefully at the ticket, the narrator realized the train number was wrong — wrong train.

questions.q_zh: 「我」的票上什么是错的？
questions.q_en: What's wrong on 'my' ticket?
questions.explanation: 车次不一样 — upon re-checking, the train number (车次) is the detail that doesn't match, explaining the seat conflict.

questions.q_zh: 「我」的火车怎么了？
questions.q_en: What happened to 'my' train?
questions.explanation: The narrator's actual train has already departed — they missed it.

title: The Extra Umbrella
text_zh: 下雨天，我去咖啡店。进门的时候，门口有三把伞。我把我的伞也放在那里。喝完咖啡以后，我去拿伞。但是现在有五把伞了。我只带了一把来，店里的人说他们也只带了一把。那多出来的那把是谁的？我看了看外面——雨已经停了。也许那把伞不想走了。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ tiān, wǒ qù kāfēidiàn. Jìn mén de shíhou, ménkǒu yǒu sān bǎ sǎn. Wǒ bǎ wǒ de sǎn yě fàng zài nàlǐ. Hē wán kāfēi yǐhòu, wǒ qù ná sǎn. Dànshì xiànzài yǒu wǔ bǎ sǎn le. Wǒ zhǐ dài le yì bǎ lái, diàn lǐ de rén shuō tāmen yě zhǐ dài le yì bǎ. Nà duō chūlái de nà bǎ shì shéi de? Wǒ kàn le kàn wàimiàn——yǔ yǐjīng tíng le. Yěxǔ nà bǎ sǎn bù xiǎng zǒu le.
text_en: On a rainy day, I go to a coffee shop. When I enter, there are three umbrellas at the door. I put mine there too. After finishing my coffee, I go to get my umbrella. But now there are five. I only brought one; the people in the shop say they also only brought one each. Whose is the extra one? I look outside — the rain has stopped. Maybe that umbrella doesn't want to leave.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这个人以后会怎样？
questions.q_en: What do you think will happen to this person in the future?

options.pinyin: huì bān dào bié de dìfang

questions.explanation: The narrator takes a different umbrella, and the rain stops — life goes on with small mix-ups.

questions.q_zh: 读完这段话，你觉得作者想说什么？
questions.q_en: After reading this, what do you think the author wants to say?

options.text: 平凡的日子也值得珍惜
options.pinyin: píngfán de rìzi yě zhíde zhēnxī

options.text_en: should move to a big city

questions.explanation: A small umbrella swap on a rainy day — the passage finds quiet meaning in ordinary moments.

questions.q_zh: 「我」出来的时候，外面怎么样？
questions.q_en: What's it like outside when 'I' comes out?
questions.explanation: When the narrator comes out, the rain has stopped — no umbrella needed after all.

title: The Phone Call at Midnight
text_zh: 昨天晚上十二点，我的手机响了。我看了一下——是一个我不认识的号码。我接了。对面的人说：「你好，我找王先生。」我说：「你打错了，我不是王先生。」他说：「对不起。」然后就挂了。但是今天早上，我又接到了一样的电话，一样的声音，一样的话。他又说：「你好，我找王先生。」我说：「你昨天也打过。」他没说话，就挂了。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān wǎnshang shí'èr diǎn, wǒ de shǒujī xiǎng le. Wǒ kàn le yíxià——shì yí gè wǒ bú rènshi de hàomǎ. Wǒ jiē le. Duìmiàn de rén shuō:「Nǐ hǎo, wǒ zhǎo Wáng xiānsheng.」Wǒ shuō:「Nǐ dǎ cuò le, wǒ búshì Wáng xiānsheng.」Tā shuō:「Duìbuqǐ.」Ránhòu jiù guà le. Dànshì jīntiān zǎoshang, wǒ yòu jiē dào le yíyàng de diànhuà, yíyàng de shēngyīn, yíyàng de huà. Tā yòu shuō:「Nǐ hǎo, wǒ zhǎo Wáng xiānsheng.」Wǒ shuō:「Nǐ zuótiān yě dǎ guò.」Tā méi shuōhuà, jiù guà le.
text_en: Last night at midnight, my phone rang. I looked — an unknown number. I answered. The person said: 'Hello, I'm looking for Mr. Wang.' I said: 'Wrong number, I'm not Mr. Wang.' He said: 'Sorry.' Then hung up. But this morning, I got the same call. Same voice, same words. He said again: 'Hello, I'm looking for Mr. Wang.' I said: 'You called yesterday too.' He said nothing and hung up.

questions.q_zh: 安静的场景传达了什么信息？
questions.q_en: What does the quiet scene communicate?
questions.explanation: A wrong number at midnight, brief words, silence — the scene has a calm, undisturbed quality.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用了什么方式让读者理解人物？
questions.q_en: How does the passage help the reader understand the character?

options.pinyin: tōngguò miáoxiě tā zuò de shì

options.text: 直接说他/她是什么样的人
options.pinyin: zhíjiē shuō tā shì shénme yàng de rén
options.text_en: by directly stating what kind of person they are

questions.explanation: The passage reveals the narrator through actions — answering the phone, noticing the repeat call.

questions.q_zh: 「我」告诉他什么以后，他怎么样？
questions.q_en: What did the caller do after 'I' told him he called yesterday?

options.pinyin: tā méi shuōhuà jiù guà le
options.text_en: he said nothing and hung up

options.pinyin: tā yòu wèn le yí cì

questions.explanation: After being told he called yesterday too, the caller said nothing and hung up.

title: Two Names
text_zh: 我有两个名字。在公司，大家叫我英文名字David。在家里，妈妈叫我中文名字大伟。有时候我觉得David和大伟不是一个人。David说英语，穿黑色衣服，走得很快。大伟说中文，喜欢慢慢吃妈妈做的面条。晚上回家，我从David变成大伟。这两个人，我都是。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu liǎng gè míngzi. Zài gōngsī, dàjiā jiào wǒ yīngwén míngzi David. Zài jiā lǐ, māma jiào wǒ zhōngwén míngzi Dàwěi. Yǒu shíhou wǒ juéde David hé Dàwěi búshì yí gè rén. David shuō yīngyǔ, chuān hēisè yīfu, zǒu de hěn kuài. Dàwěi shuō zhōngwén, xǐhuan mànmàn chī māma zuò de miàntiáo. Wǎnshang huí jiā, wǒ cóng David biànchéng Dàwěi. Zhè liǎng gè rén, wǒ dōu shì.
text_en: I have two names. At the company, everyone calls me David. At home, Mom calls me Dawei. Sometimes I feel David and Dawei aren't the same person. David speaks English, wears black, walks fast. Dawei speaks Chinese, likes slowly eating Mom's noodles. At night when I go home, I change from David into Dawei. Both of them are me.

questions.q_zh: 在公司，大家叫他什么？
questions.q_en: What do people call him at work?
questions.explanation: 在公司大家叫我David — the work environment uses the English name, establishing the dual-name split.

questions.q_zh: 这段话对工作态度的看法是什么？
questions.q_en: What view of work attitude does this passage present?

options.text_en: work is less important than life

questions.explanation: David works hard and talks a lot at work — that conscientiousness defines his work self.

questions.q_zh: 他觉得David和大伟怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does he feel about David and Dawei?

options.text: 有时候觉得不是一个人
options.pinyin: yǒu shíhou juéde búshì yí gè rén
options.text_en: sometimes feels they're not the same person

questions.explanation: 有时候觉得David和大伟不是同一个人 — the two names represent two different identities and behaviors.

title: The Accent
text_zh: 我在北京住了三年了。我说中文的时候，别人总是问我：「你是哪里人？」因为我的口音和北京人不一样。我来自一个很小的城市，那里的人说话很慢，声音很轻。在北京大家说话很快，声音很大。有时候我想让自己说得像北京人，但是我做不到。我朋友告诉我：「别变，你的口音很好听。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài Běijīng zhù le sān nián le. Wǒ shuō zhōngwén de shíhou, biéren zǒngshì wèn wǒ:「Nǐ shì nǎlǐ rén?」Yīnwèi wǒ de kǒuyīn hé Běijīng rén bù yíyàng. Wǒ láizì yí gè hěn xiǎo de chéngshì, nàlǐ de rén shuōhuà hěn màn, shēngyīn hěn qīng. Zài Běijīng dàjiā shuōhuà hěn kuài, shēngyīn hěn dà. Yǒu shíhou wǒ xiǎng ràng zìjǐ shuō de xiàng Běijīng rén, dànshì wǒ zuò bu dào. Wǒ péngyou gàosu wǒ:「Bié biàn, nǐ de kǒuyīn hěn hǎotīng.」
text_en: I've lived in Beijing for three years. When I speak Chinese, people always ask: 'Where are you from?' Because my accent is different from Beijing people's. I come from a small city where people speak slowly and softly. In Beijing everyone speaks fast and loud. Sometimes I want to make myself sound like a Beijing person, but I can't. My friend tells me: 'Don't change — your accent sounds nice.'

questions.q_zh: 别人为什么总问他是哪里人？
questions.q_en: Why do people always ask where he's from?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā chuān de bù yíyàng
yīnwèi tā de kǒuyīn bù yíyàng
yīnwèi tā de liǎn bù yíyàng

questions.explanation: 我的口音和北京人不一样 — the accent difference is what triggers the question; people can hear he's not local.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用了什么方式让读者理解人物？
questions.q_en: How does the passage help the reader understand the character?

options.pinyin: tōngguò miáoxiě tā zuò de shì

options.text: 直接说他/她是什么样的人
options.pinyin: zhíjiē shuō tā shì shénme yàng de rén
options.text_en: by directly stating what kind of person they are

questions.explanation: The narrator notices language differences, food from home, daily details — actions reveal identity.

questions.q_zh: 文中哪个细节最能体现作者的态度？
questions.q_en: Which detail best reflects the author's attitude?
questions.explanation: The narrator pays attention to accents, language, and belonging — ordinary things that matter.

title: The Wrong Medicine
text_zh: 我生病了，医生给了我两种药。白色的早上吃，红色的晚上吃。但是我吃错了——早上吃了红色的，晚上吃了白色的。第二天我告诉医生，他问我：「你觉得怎么样？」我说：「我觉得很好啊。」他笑了：「那就对了，因为两种药是一样的。我只是想让你别忘了吃。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ shēngbìng le, yīshēng gěi le wǒ liǎng zhǒng yào. Báisè de zǎoshang chī, hóngsè de wǎnshang chī. Dànshì wǒ chī cuò le——zǎoshang chī le hóngsè de, wǎnshang chī le báisè de. Dì-èr tiān wǒ gàosu yīshēng, tā wèn wǒ:「Nǐ juéde zěnmeyàng?」Wǒ shuō:「Wǒ juéde hěn hǎo a.」Tā xiào le:「Nà jiù duì le, yīnwèi liǎng zhǒng yào shì yíyàng de. Wǒ zhǐ shì xiǎng ràng nǐ bié wàng le chī.」
text_en: I got sick and the doctor gave me two kinds of medicine. White ones in the morning, red ones at night. But I mixed them up — red in the morning, white at night. The next day I told the doctor. He asked: 'How do you feel?' I said: 'Pretty good, actually.' He laughed: 'That's right, because both medicines are the same. I just wanted to make sure you wouldn't forget to take them.'

questions.q_zh: 如果把这段话和新闻报道比较，最大的区别是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the biggest difference between this passage and a news report?
questions.explanation: The passage focuses on how the narrator felt confused and amused — feelings over medical facts.

questions.q_zh: 两种药有什么不一样？
questions.q_en: What's different about the two medicines?

options.pinyin: yì zhǒng dà, yì zhǒng xiǎo
options.text_en: one is big, one is small

options.text: 颜色不一样，但其实一样
options.pinyin: yánsè bù yíyàng, dàn qíshí yíyàng
options.text_en: different colors but actually the same

options.pinyin: yì zhǒng guì, yì zhǒng piányi
options.text_en: one is expensive, one is cheap

questions.explanation: 颜色不同但其实是一样的药 — despite the white/red distinction, the doctor reveals they're identical medicine.

questions.q_zh: 医生为什么给两种颜色的药？
questions.q_en: Why did the doctor give two colors of medicine?

options.pinyin: xiǎng ràng tā bié wàng le chī yào
options.text_en: to make sure he doesn't forget
because there were no white ones left

questions.explanation: Two colors so the patient remembers morning vs. night — a practical trick.

title: The Birthday Surprise
text_zh: 今天是我的生日。我的朋友们说要给我一个惊喜，让我晚上去他们家。我到了以后，门关着，灯也没开。我开了门，大声说：「我来了！」没有人回答。我找了半天，他们都不在家。后来他们打电话告诉我：「我们在你家等你呢！惊喜！」我站在他们空空的客厅里，看着他们的电视。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān shì wǒ de shēngrì. Wǒ de péngyoumen shuō yào gěi wǒ yí gè jīngxǐ, ràng wǒ wǎnshang qù tāmen jiā. Wǒ dào le yǐhòu, mén guānzhe, dēng yě méi kāi. Wǒ kāi le mén, dàshēng shuō:「Wǒ lái le!」Méiyǒu rén huídá. Wǒ zhǎo le bàntiān, tāmen dōu bú zài jiā. Hòulái tāmen dǎ diànhuà gàosu wǒ:「Wǒmen zài nǐ jiā děng nǐ ne! Jīngxǐ!」Wǒ xiào le:「Nǐmen zài wǒ jiā, wǒ zài nǐmen jiā, zhè shì shénme jīngxǐ?」
text_en: Today is my birthday. My friends said they'd give me a surprise and told me to come to their place tonight. When I arrived, the door was shut and lights off. I opened the door and called out: 'I'm here!' No answer. I looked around forever — nobody home. Later they called: 'We're at YOUR place waiting for you! Surprise!' I was standing in their empty living room, staring at their TV.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中的'重复'有什么深意？
questions.q_en: What deeper meaning does the 'repetition' in this passage carry?
questions.explanation: They all went to different places trying to plan — the repeated miscommunication is comically reassuring.

questions.q_zh: 作者想通过这个故事探讨什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author want to explore through this story?

options.text: 日常生活中的深层意义
options.pinyin: rìcháng shēnghuó zhōng de shēncéng yìyì

questions.explanation: A botched surprise party reveals how much friends care, even when plans fall apart.

questions.q_zh: 这个「惊喜」怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did the 'surprise' turn out?

options.text: 大家去了不一样的地方
options.pinyin: dàjiā qù le bù yíyàng de dìfang

questions.explanation: Everyone went to different places to prepare, so nobody was actually at the apartment.

title: Grandma's Egg Noodles
text_zh: 小时候，每次生日奶奶都给我做鸡蛋面条。她说：「面条长，所以吃面条的人也会长长地活着。」她做的面条很简单——只有鸡蛋、面条和一点点盐。但是比饭馆的好吃。现在奶奶不在了。我有时候自己做鸡蛋面条，但是怎么做都不是那个味道。也许不是面条的问题，是因为少了一个人。
text_pinyin: Xiǎo shíhou, měi cì shēngrì nǎinai dōu gěi wǒ zuò jīdàn miàntiáo. Tā shuō:「Miàntiáo cháng, suǒyǐ chī miàntiáo de rén yě huì cháng cháng de huózhe.」Tā zuò de miàntiáo hěn jiǎndān——zhǐ yǒu jīdàn, miàntiáo hé yìdiǎndiǎn yán. Dànshì bǐ fànguǎn de hǎochī. Xiànzài nǎinai bú zài le. Wǒ yǒu shíhou zìjǐ zuò jīdàn miàntiáo, dànshì zěnme zuò dōu búshì nàge wèidào. Yěxǔ búshì miàntiáo de wèntí, shì yīnwèi shǎo le yí gè rén.
text_en: When I was little, Grandma made me egg noodles for every birthday. She said: 'Noodles are long, so people who eat noodles will live a long life.' Her noodles were simple — just eggs, noodles, and a little salt. But better than any restaurant. Now Grandma is gone. Sometimes I make egg noodles myself, but no matter how I make them, they don't taste the same. Maybe it's not the noodles — maybe it's because someone is missing.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: The narrator can't recreate Grandma's taste — the noodles carry longing for something gone.

questions.q_zh: 奶奶的面条里有什么？
questions.q_en: What's in Grandma's noodles?
questions.explanation: Grandma's noodles had 'eggs, noodles, and salt' — just three simple ingredients.

questions.q_zh: 现在「我」自己做的面条怎么样？
questions.q_en: How are the noodles 'I' makes now?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'the taste is not the same' no matter how many times they try.

title: The Watermelon Test
text_zh: 我去买西瓜，旁边一个老人教我：「你要用手打一下西瓜。声音好听的，就是好的。」我打了一下——我觉得声音很好听。老人摇了摇头说：「不对，这个不好。」我又打了一个，他又说不好。我问他：「到底什么样的声音是好的？」他笑着说：「这个你要自己慢慢学。我学了三十年了。」最后他帮我选了一个。回家吃了，真的很好吃。
text_pinyin: Wǒ qù mǎi xīguā, pángbiān yí gè lǎorén jiāo wǒ:「Nǐ yào yòng shǒu dǎ yíxià xīguā. Shēngyīn hǎotīng de, jiù shì hǎo de.」Wǒ dǎ le yíxià——wǒ juéde shēngyīn hěn hǎotīng. Lǎorén yáo le yáo tóu shuō:「Búduì, zhège bù hǎo.」Wǒ yòu dǎ le yí gè, tā yòu shuō bù hǎo. Wǒ wèn tā:「Dàodǐ shénmeyàng de shēngyīn shì hǎo de?」Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Zhège nǐ yào zìjǐ mànmàn xué. Wǒ xué le sānshí nián le.」Zuìhòu tā bāng wǒ xuǎn le yí gè. Huí jiā chī le, zhēn de hěn hǎochī.
text_en: I go to buy watermelon. An old man next to me teaches me: 'Tap the watermelon with your hand. If it sounds good, it's a good one.' I tap one — I think it sounds good. The old man shakes his head: 'No, this one's not good.' I tap another; he says that's not good either. I ask: 'What exactly does a good one sound like?' He smiles: 'You have to learn this slowly on your own. I've been learning for thirty years.' In the end he picks one for me. I eat it at home — really delicious.

questions.q_zh: 怎么知道西瓜好不好？
questions.q_en: How do you tell if a watermelon is good?
questions.explanation: The old man said 'tap it with your hand and listen' to judge the watermelon.

questions.q_zh: 老人学了多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long has the old man been learning?
questions.explanation: The old man said he's been practicing this skill for 'thirty years.'

questions.q_zh: 最后西瓜好吃吗？
questions.q_en: Was the watermelon good in the end?
questions.explanation: 真的很好吃 — the old man's technique for picking watermelons proved correct when tasted.

title: The Sleeping Guard
text_zh: 小区的保安每天晚上都坐在门口。有一天我很晚回来，发现他睡着了。桌子上有一杯已经冷了的茶和一本打开的书。我走过去的时候，他突然醒了，说：「你回来了。」好像他一直在等我。
text_pinyin: Xiǎoqū de bǎo'ān měi tiān wǎnshang dōu zuò zài ménkǒu. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ hěn wǎn huílái, fāxiàn tā shuìzháo le. Zhuōzi shàng yǒu yì bēi yǐjīng lěng le de chá hé yì běn dǎkāi de shū. Wǒ zǒu guòqù de shíhou, tā tūrán xǐng le, shuō:「Nǐ huílái le.」Hǎoxiàng tā yìzhí zài děng wǒ.
text_en: The compound's security guard sits at the entrance every night. One day I came back very late and found him asleep. On the table was a cold cup of tea and an open book. When I walked past, he suddenly woke up and said: 'You're back.' As if he'd been waiting for me all along.

questions.q_zh: 为什么这段话值得仔细读第二遍？
questions.q_en: Why is this passage worth reading a second time?

options.text: 能发现更多隐含的意义
options.pinyin: néng fāxiàn gèng duō yǐnhán de yìyì
options.text_en: you can discover more hidden meanings

options.text: 内容太复杂，一遍读不懂
options.pinyin: nèiróng tài fùzá, yí biàn dú bù dǒng
options.text_en: it's too complex to understand in one reading

options.pinyin: bù zhíde dú dì èr biàn

questions.explanation: The cold tea, the open book, the simple 'you're back' — small details carry deeper meaning on second reading.

questions.q_zh: 桌子上有什么？
questions.q_en: What was on the table?

options.text: 一杯冷茶和一本打开的书
options.pinyin: yì bēi lěng chá hé yì běn dǎkāi de shū
options.text_en: a cold tea and an open book

questions.explanation: 一杯凉了的茶和一本打开的书 — the cold tea and open book show he'd been there a while before falling asleep.

questions.q_zh: 保安醒了以后说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the guard say when he woke up?
questions.explanation: When the guard woke up, he simply said 'you're back' — as if nothing happened.

title: The Piano in the Rain
text_zh: 每天晚上八点，楼上都有人弹钢琴。有时候弹得快，有时候弹得慢。下雨的晚上，钢琴的声音听起来比平时更好听。我不知道弹钢琴的人是谁，但是因为他，下雨的晚上变成了我最喜欢的时候。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān wǎnshang bā diǎn, lóu shàng dōu yǒu rén tán gāngqín. Yǒu shíhou tán de kuài, yǒu shíhou tán de màn. Xià yǔ de wǎnshang, gāngqín de shēngyīn tīng qǐlái bǐ píngshí gèng hǎotīng. Wǒ bù zhīdào tán gāngqín de rén shì shéi, dànshì yīnwèi tā, xià yǔ de wǎnshang biànchéng le wǒ zuì xǐhuan de shíhou.
text_en: Every evening at eight, someone upstairs plays the piano. Sometimes fast, sometimes slow. On rainy evenings, the piano sounds even better than usual. I don't know who plays, but because of them, rainy evenings have become my favorite time.

questions.q_zh: 楼上的人每天几点弹钢琴？
questions.q_en: What time does the person upstairs play piano?
questions.explanation: 每天晚上八点 specifies the exact recurring time — eight in the evening, every day.

questions.q_zh: 这段话让你想到了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this passage make you think of?
questions.explanation: Hearing piano through the ceiling at night — it reminds you of your own quiet evening moments.

questions.q_zh: 「我」知道弹钢琴的人是谁吗？
questions.q_en: Does 'I' know who plays the piano?
questions.explanation: 我不知道是谁弹的 — the pianist remains anonymous; the narrator only knows them through the music.

title: The Last Customer
