text_zh: 面包店晚上九点关门。每天快到九点的时候，总有一个女人来。她每次都买最后剩下的面包。店员已经认识她了，有时候会多给她一个。她拿了面包，说了谢谢，走进了夜里。我想她可能不是因为喜欢面包才来的。
text_pinyin: Miànbāo diàn wǎnshang jiǔ diǎn guān mén. Měi tiān kuài dào jiǔ diǎn de shíhou, zǒng yǒu yí gè nǚrén lái. Tā měi cì dōu mǎi zuìhòu shèng xià de miànbāo. Diànyuán yǐjīng rènshi tā le, yǒu shíhou huì duō gěi tā yí gè. Tā ná le miànbāo, shuō le xièxie, zǒu jìn le yè lǐ. Wǒ xiǎng tā kěnéng búshì yīnwèi xǐhuan miànbāo cái lái de.
text_en: The bakery closes at nine. Every day near nine, a woman always comes. She buys whatever bread is left. The clerk already knows her, sometimes giving her an extra one. She takes the bread, says thank you, and walks into the night. I think maybe she doesn't come because she likes bread.

questions.q_zh: 读完后，读者最可能有什么感受？
questions.q_en: How is the reader most likely to feel after reading this?
questions.explanation: A woman buying leftover bread every night — the scene invites you to sit with it and think.

questions.q_zh: 这段话让你想到了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this passage make you think of?
questions.explanation: The daily routine of the bakery woman echoes familiar patterns from your own life.

questions.q_zh: 为什么这段话值得仔细读第二遍？
questions.q_en: Why is this passage worth reading a second time?

options.text: 能发现更多隐含的意义
options.pinyin: néng fāxiàn gèng duō yǐnhán de yìyì
options.text_en: you can discover more hidden meanings

options.text: 内容太复杂，一遍读不懂
options.pinyin: nèiróng tài fùzá, yí biàn dú bù dǒng
options.text_en: it's too complex to understand in one reading

options.pinyin: bù zhíde dú dì èr biàn

questions.explanation: The clerk's extra bread, the silent routine — second reading reveals the kindness underneath.

title: Shoes at the Door
text_zh: 从一家人门口的鞋，可以知道很多事情。有小鞋的，家里一定有小孩。有很多双鞋的，家里人一定不少。我邻居的门口只有一双鞋，总是放得很整齐。他一个人住，但是从来不让门口乱。我觉得一个人的生活也可以过得很好。
text_pinyin: Cóng yì jiā rén ménkǒu de xié, kěyǐ zhīdào hěn duō shìqing. Yǒu xiǎo xié de, jiā lǐ yídìng yǒu xiǎohái. Yǒu hěn duō shuāng xié de, jiā lǐ rén yídìng bù shǎo. Wǒ línjū de ménkǒu zhǐ yǒu yì shuāng xié, zǒngshì fàng de hěn zhěngqí. Tā yí gè rén zhù, dànshì cónglái bú ràng ménkǒu luàn. Wǒ juéde yí gè rén de shēnghuó yě kěyǐ guò de hěn hǎo.
text_en: You can tell a lot from the shoes at a family's door. Small shoes mean there must be children. Many pairs mean the family must be big. My neighbor's door has only one pair, always neatly placed. He lives alone, but never lets the doorway get messy. I think a life alone can be lived well too.

questions.q_zh: 门口有小鞋说明什么？
questions.q_en: What do small shoes at the door tell you?
questions.explanation: 小鞋子说明一定有小孩 — small shoe size is the evidence; only children would wear shoes that small.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: One pair of old shoes, dusty and still — someone who was there isn't anymore.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: The lonely old shoes at the neighbor's door suggest someone is missing from the home.

title: The Map Seller
text_zh: 火车站外面有一个老人在卖地图。现在大家都用手机看地图了，买纸地图的人很少。但是他还是每天都来。有一次我问他为什么不卖别的东西，他说：「因为总有人不知道路。」我买了一张，虽然我也有手机。
text_pinyin: Huǒchē zhàn wàimiàn yǒu yí gè lǎorén zài mài dìtú. Xiànzài dàjiā dōu yòng shǒujī kàn dìtú le, mǎi zhǐ dìtú de rén hěn shǎo. Dànshì tā háishi měi tiān dōu lái. Yǒu yí cì wǒ wèn tā wèishénme bú mài biéde dōngxi, tā shuō:「Yīnwèi zǒng yǒu rén bù zhīdào lù.」Wǒ mǎi le yì zhāng, suīrán wǒ yě yǒu shǒujī.
text_en: Outside the train station, an old man sells maps. Now everyone uses phone maps, so few people buy paper ones. But he still comes every day. Once I asked why he doesn't sell something else. He said: 'Because there's always someone who doesn't know the way.' I bought one, even though I have a phone too.

questions.q_zh: 为什么买纸地图的人很少？
questions.q_en: Why do few people buy paper maps?

options.text: 因为大家都用手机看地图
options.pinyin: yīnwèi dàjiā dōu yòng shǒujī kàn dìtú

options.text_en: because the station is too far

questions.explanation: 现在大家都用手机地图 — phone maps replaced paper ones, eliminating most of his customer base.

questions.q_zh: 老人为什么不卖别的东西？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the old man sell something else?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā zhǐ huì mài dìtú
options.text_en: because he can only sell maps

options.pinyin: yīnwèi zǒng yǒu rén bù zhīdào lù
options.text_en: because there's always someone who doesn't know the way
because other things are too expensive

questions.explanation: He said 'because there's always someone who doesn't know the way' — that's reason enough.

questions.q_zh: 「我」最后做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' do in the end?

options.pinyin: yòng shǒujī gěi tā kàn lù

questions.explanation: The passage ends with the narrator buying a map from him.

title: The Empty Chair
text_zh: 公园里有两把椅子，一直面对面放着。每天下午，两个老人坐在那里下棋。上个月，只有一个老人来了。他还是坐在同一把椅子上，看着对面的空椅子。有人问他要不要一起下棋，他摇了摇头，说：「我在等我的老朋友。」
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu liǎng bǎ yǐzi, yìzhí miàn duì miàn fàngzhe. Měi tiān xiàwǔ, liǎng gè lǎorén zuò zài nàli xià qí. Shàng gè yuè, zhǐ yǒu yí gè lǎorén lái le. Tā háishi zuò zài tóng yì bǎ yǐzi shàng, kànzhe duìmiàn de kōng yǐzi. Yǒu rén wèn tā yào bu yào yìqǐ xià qí, tā yáo le yáo tóu, shuō:「Wǒ zài děng wǒ de lǎo péngyou.」
text_en: In the park there are two chairs, always facing each other. Every afternoon, two old men sit there playing chess. Last month, only one came. He still sits in the same chair, looking at the empty one across from him. Someone asked if he wanted to play chess together. He shook his head: 'I'm waiting for my old friend.'

questions.q_zh: 两把椅子怎么放的？
questions.q_en: How are the two chairs positioned?

options.pinyin: yí gè zài qián yí gè zài hòu

questions.explanation: 总是面对面的 — the chairs are permanently positioned facing one another, set up for the chess partners.

questions.q_zh: 上个月发生了什么？
questions.q_en: What happened last month?

options.pinyin: liǎng gè rén dōu méi lái
zhǐ yǒu yí gè lǎorén lái le

questions.explanation: Last month 'only one came' — the other stopped showing up.

questions.q_zh: 人物的行为暗示了什么内心想法？
questions.q_en: What inner thoughts does the character's behavior hint at?
questions.explanation: He still sits facing the empty chair — he's holding onto something meaningful and quiet.

title: The Taxi Driver's Radio
text_zh: 昨天坐出租车，司机在听收音机。不是音乐，是一个人在讲故事。司机听得很认真，有时候还笑。到了以后，他让我等一下，说：「快讲完了。」我们一起在车里听了最后两分钟。故事讲完了，他才跟我说多少钱。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān zuò chūzūchē, sījī zài tīng shōuyīnjī. Búshì yīnyuè, shì yí gè rén zài jiǎng gùshi. Sījī tīng de hěn rènzhēn, yǒu shíhou hái xiào. Dào le yǐhòu, tā ràng wǒ děng yíxià, shuō:「Kuài jiǎng wán le.」Wǒmen yìqǐ zài chē lǐ tīng le zuìhòu liǎng fēnzhōng. Gùshi jiǎng wán le, tā cái gēn wǒ shuō duōshao qián.
text_en: Yesterday in a taxi, the driver was listening to the radio. Not music — someone telling a story. The driver listened intently, sometimes laughing. When we arrived, he asked me to wait, saying: 'It's almost done.' We listened to the last two minutes together in the car. Only after the story ended did he tell me the fare.

questions.q_zh: 司机在听什么？
questions.q_en: What was the driver listening to?

options.pinyin: yí gè rén zài jiǎng gùshi

questions.explanation: The driver was listening to 'someone telling a story' on the radio.

questions.q_zh: 到了以后司机让「我」做什么？
questions.q_en: What did the driver ask 'me' to do after arriving?
questions.explanation: After arriving, the driver asked the narrator to 'wait a moment' to hear the ending.

questions.q_zh: 他们一起听了多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long did they listen together?
questions.explanation: They sat together and listened for 'two minutes' until the story ended.

title: Construction Across the Street
text_zh: 对面的空地上开始盖楼了。从早上七点到晚上六点，一直有声音。邻居们都觉得太吵了，但是工人们每天都很辛苦。我从窗户看他们工作，觉得这个城市一直在变。也许明年，从我窗户看出去的东西就完全不一样了。
text_pinyin: Duìmiàn de kòng dì shàng kāishǐ gài lóu le. Cóng zǎoshang qī diǎn dào wǎnshang liù diǎn, yìzhí yǒu shēngyīn. Línjūmen dōu juéde tài chǎo le, dànshì gōngrénmen měi tiān dōu hěn xīnkǔ. Wǒ cóng chuānghu kàn tāmen gōngzuò, juéde zhège chéngshì yìzhí zài biàn. Yěxǔ míngnián, cóng wǒ chuānghu kàn chūqù de dōngxi jiù wánquán bù yíyàng le.
text_en: They've started building on the empty lot across the street. From seven AM to six PM, there's constant noise. The neighbors all think it's too loud, but the workers work hard every day. I watch them from my window and think this city is always changing. Maybe next year, the view from my window will be completely different.

questions.q_zh: 盖楼的声音从几点到几点？
questions.q_en: What hours is the construction noise?

options.pinyin: cóng bā diǎn dào wǔ diǎn
cóng qī diǎn dào liù diǎn
cóng liù diǎn dào qī diǎn

questions.explanation: 从早上七点到下午六点 — the hours are stated directly as 7 AM to 6 PM.

questions.q_zh: 邻居们觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How do the neighbors feel?
questions.explanation: 邻居们都觉得太吵 — 太吵 ('too noisy') captures their complaint about the construction.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: The noise and the narrator's reaction mirror each other — external weather matches internal state.

title: The Underground Passage
text_zh: 火车站旁边有一个地下通道。虽然只要走两分钟，但是里面有很多东西。有人在卖花，有人在唱歌。墙上有很多画，都是不同的人画的。每次走过去，我都觉得这不只是一条路——它是一个小小的世界。
text_pinyin: Huǒchē zhàn pángbiān yǒu yí gè dìxià tōngdào. Suīrán zhǐ yào zǒu liǎng fēnzhōng, dànshì lǐmiàn yǒu hěn duō dōngxi. Yǒu rén zài mài huā, yǒu rén zài chàng gē. Qiáng shàng yǒu hěn duō huà, dōu shì bùtóng de rén huà de. Měi cì zǒu guòqù, wǒ dōu juéde zhè bù zhǐshì yì tiáo lù——tā shì yí gè xiǎoxiǎo de shìjiè.
text_en: Next to the train station there's an underground passage. Although it takes only two minutes to walk through, there's a lot inside. Someone sells flowers, someone sings. The walls are covered in paintings by different people. Every time I pass through, I feel it's not just a path — it's a tiny world.

questions.q_zh: 走过地下通道要多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long does it take to walk through?
questions.explanation: 只要两分钟就能走完 — despite the short two-minute walk, there's a lot happening inside.

questions.q_zh: 墙上有什么？
questions.q_en: What's on the walls?
questions.explanation: 墙上有很多画 — the walls are covered in paintings, part of what fills the short passage with interest.

questions.q_zh: 这段话想要读者从中获得什么？
questions.q_en: What does the passage want the reader to gain from it?

options.text: 一种新的看事物的方式
options.pinyin: yì zhǒng xīn de kàn shìwù de fāngshì
options.text_en: a new way of seeing things

questions.explanation: A two-minute walk full of unexpected life — the passage shifts how you see ordinary spaces.

title: The Late Bus
text_zh: 公共汽车应该十分钟一辆，但是今天等了二十分钟都没来。站在旁边的人越来越多。有人看手机，有人看路。一个老太太坐在那里，好像不着急。她说：「急也没有用，车来了就好了。」我觉得她说得对。
text_pinyin: Gōnggòng qìchē yīnggāi shí fēnzhōng yí liàng, dànshì jīntiān děng le èrshí fēnzhōng dōu méi lái. Zhàn zài pángbiān de rén yuè lái yuè duō. Yǒu rén kàn shǒujī, yǒu rén kàn lù. Yí gè lǎo tàitai zuò zài nàli, hǎoxiàng bù zháojí. Tā shuō:「Jí yě méiyǒu yòng, chē lái le jiù hǎo le.」Wǒ juéde tā shuō de duì.
text_en: The bus should come every ten minutes, but today I waited twenty minutes and it still hadn't come. More and more people gathered. Some checked phones, some watched the road. An old lady sat there, seeming not worried. She said: 'Being anxious is useless. When the bus comes, everything's fine.' I think she's right.

questions.q_zh: 公共汽车应该多长时间一辆？
questions.q_en: How often should the bus come?
questions.explanation: 应该每十分钟来一趟 — 应该 ('should') sets the expected frequency at ten minutes, which today was not met.

questions.q_zh: 老太太觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the old lady feel?
questions.explanation: The old lady wasn't worried at all — she stood there calmly.

questions.q_zh: 老太太说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the old lady say?

options.text: 我已经等了一个小时了
options.pinyin: wǒ yǐjīng děng le yí gè xiǎoshí le

questions.explanation: The old lady said 'being anxious is useless' — the bus comes when it comes.

title: The Park After Dark
text_zh: 白天的公园是给小孩和老人的。但是天黑以后，公园变了。跑步的人出来了，在路灯下面跑。有人在黑的地方练太极，看不太清楚，只能看到他们的影子在动。公园的晚上比白天安静，但是并不是没有人——只是换了一些人。
text_pinyin: Báitiān de gōngyuán shì gěi xiǎohái hé lǎorén de. Dànshì tiān hēi yǐhòu, gōngyuán biàn le. Pǎobù de rén chūlái le, zài lùdēng xiàmiàn pǎo. Yǒu rén zài hēi de dìfang liàn tàijí, kàn bú tài qīngchu, zhǐ néng kàn dào tāmen de yǐngzi zài dòng. Gōngyuán de wǎnshang bǐ báitiān ānjìng, dànshì bìng búshì méiyǒu rén——zhǐshì huàn le yìxiē rén.
text_en: The daytime park belongs to children and the elderly. But after dark, the park changes. Runners appear, running under streetlights. Some practice tai chi in the dark — you can't see clearly, only their shadows moving. The park at night is quieter than daytime, but it's not empty — just different people.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么样的变化？
questions.q_en: What kind of change does this passage suggest?
questions.explanation: The park transforms at night into something different — the change suggests inner transformation too.

questions.q_zh: 天黑以后，有人在做什么？
questions.q_en: What do people do after dark?
questions.explanation: After dark, 'runners appear' and 'some practice tai chi' — different people, different activities.

questions.q_zh: 晚上的公园跟白天比怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the night park compare to daytime?

options.pinyin: gèng ānjìng, dàn huàn le yìxiē rén

questions.explanation: The night park is quieter with different people — runners and tai chi practitioners replace children.

title: The Post Office Queue
text_zh: 邮局里只有两个窗口开着。排队的人很多，但是大家都不说话。突然一个人从前面走出来，跟后面的人说：「这个窗口的人做得比那个快，你可以换到这边。」几个人听了就换了。我觉得在一个安静的地方，说话的那个人比别人都重要。
text_pinyin: Yóujú lǐ zhǐ yǒu liǎng gè chuāngkǒu kāizhe. Páiduì de rén hěn duō, dànshì dàjiā dōu bù shuō huà. Tūrán yí gè rén cóng qiánmiàn zǒu chūlái, gēn hòumiàn de rén shuō:「Zhège chuāngkǒu de rén zuò de bǐ nàge kuài, nǐ kěyǐ huàn dào zhè biān.」Jǐ gè rén tīng le jiù huàn le. Wǒ juéde zài yí gè ānjìng de dìfang, shuō huà de nàge rén bǐ biéren dōu zhòngyào.
text_en: The post office has only two windows open. Many people are in line, but nobody speaks. Suddenly someone walks out from the front and tells those in back: 'This window is faster than that one, you can switch to this side.' Several people switched. I think in a quiet place, the person who speaks up matters more than everyone else.

questions.q_zh: 如果另一个人来到同样的场景，会有同样的感受吗？
questions.q_en: Would another person in the same setting have the same feelings?

options.text: 不一定，取决于个人经历
options.pinyin: bù yídìng, qǔjué yú gèrén jīnglì
options.text_en: not necessarily, it depends on personal experience

questions.explanation: Different people would react differently to waiting — it depends on who you are and what you've been through.

questions.q_zh: 那个人告诉大家什么？
questions.q_en: What did that person tell everyone?

options.text_en: this window is faster than that one
it will take a long time

questions.explanation: That person told everyone 'this window is faster than that one.'

questions.q_zh: 文中哪个细节最能体现作者的态度？
questions.q_en: Which detail best reflects the author's attitude?
questions.explanation: The narrator notices the small social dynamics in a post office line — ordinary things that reveal character.

title: The New Teacher
text_zh: 我们的数学老师走了，来了一个新的。新老师比以前的年轻，说话也比以前的慢。第一天上课，他没有打开书。他问我们：「你们觉得数学难吗？」大家都说难。他笑了，说：「我以前也觉得难。所以我知道怎么教你们。」
text_pinyin: Wǒmen de shùxué lǎoshī zǒu le, lái le yí gè xīn de. Xīn lǎoshī bǐ yǐqián de niánqīng, shuō huà yě bǐ yǐqián de màn. Dì yī tiān shàng kè, tā méiyǒu dǎkāi shū. Tā wèn wǒmen:「Nǐmen juéde shùxué nán ma?」Dàjiā dōu shuō nán. Tā xiào le, shuō:「Wǒ yǐqián yě juéde nán. Suǒyǐ wǒ zhīdào zěnme jiāo nǐmen.」
text_en: Our math teacher left and a new one came. The new teacher is younger and speaks more slowly. On the first day of class, he didn't open the book. He asked us: 'Do you think math is hard?' Everyone said yes. He smiled: 'I used to think it was hard too. So I know how to teach you.'

questions.q_zh: 新老师跟以前的老师比怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the new teacher compare to the old one?

options.pinyin: gèng lǎo, shuō huà gèng kuài
gèng niánqīng, shuō huà gèng màn

questions.explanation: 新老师更年轻,说话更慢 — two direct comparisons: younger in age and slower in speech than the previous teacher.

questions.q_zh: 第一天上课他做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did he do on the first day?

options.text: 问学生觉得数学难不难
options.pinyin: wèn xuéshēng juéde shùxué nán bu nán
options.text_en: asked students if math is hard

questions.explanation: On the first day he 'didn't open the book' — he asked students if math is hard.

questions.q_zh: 他为什么说他知道怎么教？
questions.q_en: Why does he say he knows how to teach?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā xué le hěn duō nián

options.text: 因为他以前也觉得数学难
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā yǐqián yě juéde shùxué nán
options.text_en: because he also used to find math hard

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā kàn le hěn duō shū

questions.explanation: He said 'because I also used to find math hard' — personal experience guides his teaching.

title: The Company's Coffee Machine
text_zh: 我们公司有一个咖啡机，在三楼。虽然每个人的办公室都在不同的楼层，但是大家都去三楼喝咖啡。因为这样，三楼变成了大家说话的地方。有时候在那里说几分钟的话，比开一个小时的会还有用。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen gōngsī yǒu yí gè kāfēijī, zài sān lóu. Suīrán měi gè rén de bàngōngshì dōu zài bùtóng de lóucéng, dànshì dàjiā dōu qù sān lóu hē kāfēi. Yīnwèi zhèyàng, sān lóu biànchéng le dàjiā shuō huà de dìfang. Yǒu shíhou zài nàli shuō jǐ fēnzhōng de huà, bǐ kāi yí gè xiǎoshí de huì hái yǒuyòng.
text_en: Our company has one coffee machine, on the third floor. Although everyone's office is on a different floor, everyone goes to the third floor for coffee. Because of this, the third floor became the place where people talk. Sometimes a few minutes of conversation there is more useful than a one-hour meeting.

questions.q_zh: 咖啡机在几楼？
questions.q_en: What floor is the coffee machine on?
questions.explanation: 在三楼 states the location directly — third floor.

questions.q_zh: 三楼变成了什么样的地方？
questions.q_en: What did the third floor become?
questions.explanation: The third floor became 'where everyone talks' — the coffee machine created a gathering spot.

questions.q_zh: 在三楼说几分钟话跟开会比怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does a few minutes of chat on the 3rd floor compare to meetings?

options.text: 比开一个小时的会还有用
options.pinyin: bǐ kāi yí gè xiǎoshí de huì hái yǒuyòng
options.text_en: more useful than a one-hour meeting

questions.explanation: 几分钟的聊天比一个小时的会有用 — informal coffee-line conversation produces better results than formal hour-long meetings.

title: The Gym's Unspoken Hours
text_zh: 我住的地方有一个小健身房。早上六点到八点，来的都是老人。中午来的是上班的人，他们只有半个小时，所以做得很快。晚上九点以后，来的人最少，也最安静。我喜欢晚上去，因为那时候没有人跟你说话，你可以一个人想事情。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de dìfang yǒu yí gè xiǎo jiànshēnfáng. Zǎoshang liù diǎn dào bā diǎn, lái de dōu shì lǎorén. Zhōngwǔ lái de shì shàngbān de rén, tāmen zhǐ yǒu bàn gè xiǎoshí, suǒyǐ zuò de hěn kuài. Wǎnshang jiǔ diǎn yǐhòu, lái de rén zuì shǎo, yě zuì ānjìng. Wǒ xǐhuan wǎnshang qù, yīnwèi nà shíhou méiyǒu rén gēn nǐ shuō huà, nǐ kěyǐ yí gè rén xiǎng shìqing.
text_en: Where I live there's a small gym. From six to eight AM, only the elderly come. At noon it's office workers — they only have half an hour, so they're fast. After nine PM, the fewest people come, and it's the quietest. I like going at night, because nobody talks to you, and you can think by yourself.

questions.q_zh: 早上六点到八点，谁来健身房？
questions.q_en: Who comes to the gym from 6-8 AM?
questions.explanation: 从六点到八点只有老人来 — the early morning slot belongs exclusively to elderly visitors.

questions.q_zh: 中午来的人为什么做得很快？
questions.q_en: Why are the noon visitors fast?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhǐ yǒu bàn gè xiǎoshí
options.text_en: because they only have half an hour

questions.explanation: Office workers 'only have half an hour' at noon, so they exercise fast.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么喜欢晚上去？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' like going at night?

options.text: 因为人少，可以一个人想事情
options.pinyin: yīnwèi rén shǎo, kěyǐ yí gè rén xiǎng shìqing

options.text_en: because no time during the day

questions.explanation: The narrator likes going at night because there are 'few people' and can 'think alone.'

title: The Letter Without a Name
text_zh: 今天我在门口发现了一封信。信上没有写是谁寄的，也没有写给谁。里面只有一句话：「谢谢你那天的帮助。」我想了很久，不知道我什么时候帮了谁。也许是我不知道的时候帮的。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān wǒ zài ménkǒu fāxiàn le yì fēng xìn. Xìn shàng méiyǒu xiě shì shéi jì de, yě méiyǒu xiě gěi shéi. Lǐmiàn zhǐ yǒu yí jù huà:「Xièxie nǐ nà tiān de bāngzhù.」Wǒ xiǎng le hěn jiǔ, bù zhīdào wǒ shénme shíhou bāng le shéi. Yěxǔ shì wǒ bù zhīdào de shíhou bāng de.
text_en: Today I found a letter at my door. It didn't say who sent it or who it was for. Inside was just one sentence: 'Thank you for your help that day.' I thought about it for a long time, not knowing when I helped anyone. Maybe I helped without knowing.

questions.q_zh: 如果你是文中的人物，你会有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: If you were the character, how would you feel?
questions.explanation: A kind mystery with no resolution needed — the narrator can just accept it and feel good.

questions.q_zh: 信里写了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the letter say?

options.pinyin: xièxie nǐ nà tiān de bāngzhù
options.text_en: thank you for your help that day

questions.explanation: The letter said 'thank you for your help that day.'

questions.q_zh: 「我」知道是谁写的吗？
questions.q_en: Does 'I' know who wrote it?
questions.explanation: The narrator thought hard but 'doesn't know' who could have written it.

title: The Photo in the Book
text_zh: 我在图书馆借了一本书。回家以后打开，发现里面有一张旧照片。照片上有两个年轻人，在一个我不认识的地方。照片后面写着一个日期：一九九八年。这张照片在这本书里放了多少年了？那两个人现在在哪里？
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài túshūguǎn jiè le yì běn shū. Huí jiā yǐhòu dǎkāi, fāxiàn lǐmiàn yǒu yì zhāng jiù zhàopiàn. Zhàopiàn shàng yǒu liǎng gè niánqīng rén, zài yí gè wǒ bú rènshi de dìfang. Zhàopiàn hòumiàn xiězhe yí gè rìqī: yī jiǔ jiǔ bā nián. Zhè zhāng zhàopiàn zài zhè běn shū lǐ fàng le duōshao nián le? Nà liǎng gè rén xiànzài zài nǎli?
text_en: I borrowed a book from the library. When I opened it at home, I found an old photograph inside. In the photo, two young people stand in a place I don't recognize. On the back was a date: 1998. How many years has this photo been in this book? Where are those two people now?

questions.q_zh: 这个地方对人物有什么意义？
questions.q_en: What significance does this place hold for the character?

options.pinyin: yǒu tèbié de huíyì huò gǎnqíng

questions.explanation: An old photo of unknown people in an unknown place — it stirs feelings about connection and memory.

questions.q_zh: 旅途/路上的描写暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the journey description imply?

options.text: 内心也在经历一段旅程
options.pinyin: nèixīn yě zài jīnglì yí duàn lǚchéng
options.text_en: an inner journey is happening too

questions.explanation: Finding someone else's photo triggers thoughts about where they went — an inner journey too.

questions.q_zh: 照片后面写了什么？
questions.q_en: What was written on the back of the photo?
questions.explanation: The back of the photo had 'a date' written on it.

title: The Second Floor Light
text_zh: 对面的二楼已经没有人住了，但是每天晚上七点，灯会自己亮。八点的时候又自己关了。邻居们都觉得很奇怪。后来有人进去看了一下——原来是以前住在那里的人设了一个定时的灯。他走了，但是灯还在按他的时间工作。
text_pinyin: Duìmiàn de èr lóu yǐjīng méiyǒu rén zhù le, dànshì měi tiān wǎnshang qī diǎn, dēng huì zìjǐ liàng. Bā diǎn de shíhou yòu zìjǐ guān le. Línjūmen dōu juéde hěn qíguài. Hòulái yǒu rén jìnqù kàn le yíxià——yuánlái shì yǐqián zhù zài nàli de rén shè le yí gè dìngshí de dēng. Tā zǒu le, dànshì dēng hái zài àn tā de shíjiān gōngzuò.
text_en: The second floor across the way has been empty, but every evening at seven the light turns on by itself. At eight it turns off again. The neighbors all found it strange. Later someone went inside to check — it turns out the previous resident had set the light on a timer. He left, but the light still follows his schedule.

questions.q_zh: 二楼现在有人住吗？
questions.q_en: Does anyone live on the second floor now?
questions.explanation: 没有人住 confirms it's vacant — the light turning on by itself is what made this strange.

questions.q_zh: 这种坚持说明这个人怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does this persistence tell us about the person?

options.text_en: has no ideas of their own

questions.explanation: The light turns on and off on schedule every single day — whoever set it was committed.

questions.q_zh: 灯为什么会自己亮？
questions.q_en: Why does the light turn on by itself?

options.text: 因为以前的人设了定时
options.pinyin: yīnwèi yǐqián de rén shè le dìngshí

options.text: 因为有人偷偷住在那里
options.pinyin: yīnwèi yǒu rén tōutōu zhù zài nàli

questions.explanation: 以前住的人设了一个定时器 — a timer left behind by the former tenant explains the automatic light schedule.

title: The Garden That Waters Itself
text_zh: 邻居出去旅行了，说要两个星期才回来。但是他家门口的花每天都很好看，好像有人在浇水。我每天早上都看一次，但是从来没有看到谁在那里。有一天我起得很早，终于看到了——是一只猫在打翻他门口的水碗，水刚好流到花那里。
text_pinyin: Línjū chūqù lǚxíng le, shuō yào liǎng gè xīngqī cái huílái. Dànshì tā jiā ménkǒu de huā měi tiān dōu hěn hǎokàn, hǎoxiàng yǒu rén zài jiāo shuǐ. Wǒ měi tiān zǎoshang dōu kàn yí cì, dànshì cónglái méiyǒu kàn dào shéi zài nàli. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ qǐ de hěn zǎo, zhōngyú kàn dào le——shì yì zhī māo zài dǎ fān tā ménkǒu de shuǐ wǎn, shuǐ gānghǎo liú dào huā nàli.
text_en: The neighbor went traveling, saying they wouldn't be back for two weeks. But the flowers at their door look nice every day, as if someone waters them. I check every morning but never see anyone. One day I got up very early and finally saw it — a cat knocking over the water bowl at the door, and the water flowing right to the flowers.

questions.q_zh: 重复的行为暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the repeated behavior imply?

options.text_en: this matters a lot to them

options.pinyin: tā méiyǒu bié de shì zuò
options.text_en: they have nothing else to do
others require them to do it

questions.explanation: The flowers keep getting watered mysteriously — someone or something cares enough to keep them alive.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得奇怪的是什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' find strange?

options.pinyin: huā hǎoxiàng yǒu rén zài jiāo shuǐ

questions.explanation: The narrator found it strange that 'flowers seem to be watered' while the neighbor was away.

questions.q_zh: 最后发现是怎么回事？
questions.q_en: What was the explanation?

options.pinyin: línjū qǐng le rén jiāo shuǐ

options.text: 猫打翻了水碗，水流到花那里
options.pinyin: māo dǎ fān le shuǐ wǎn, shuǐ liú dào huā nàli
options.text_en: cat knocked over water bowl, water flowed to flowers

questions.explanation: The cat knocked over its water bowl and the water flowed to the flowers — accidental watering.

title: The Name I Don't Use
text_zh: 我有两个名字。一个是中文名字，一个是英文名字。在家里大家叫我中文名字，在公司大家叫我英文名字。有时候我觉得这两个名字是两个不同的人。在家里的那个人比较安静，在公司的那个人说话比较多。但是他们都是我。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu liǎng gè míngzi. Yí gè shì Zhōngwén míngzi, yí gè shì Yīngwén míngzi. Zài jiā lǐ dàjiā jiào wǒ Zhōngwén míngzi, zài gōngsī dàjiā jiào wǒ Yīngwén míngzi. Yǒu shíhou wǒ juéde zhè liǎng gè míngzi shì liǎng gè bùtóng de rén. Zài jiā lǐ de nàge rén bǐjiào ānjìng, zài gōngsī de nàge rén shuō huà bǐjiào duō. Dànshì tāmen dōu shì wǒ.
text_en: I have two names. One Chinese, one English. At home everyone calls me the Chinese name. At the company, the English one. Sometimes I feel these two names are two different people. The one at home is quieter; the one at the company talks more. But they're both me.

questions.q_zh: 在公司大家叫他什么名字？
questions.q_en: What name does everyone use at the company?
questions.explanation: 在公司 identifies the work setting as where the English name is used, contrasting with home.

questions.q_zh: 在家里的「我」和在公司的「我」有什么不同？
questions.q_en: How is 'I' different at home vs. at work?

options.text: 在家里说话多，在公司安静
options.pinyin: zài jiā lǐ shuō huà duō, zài gōngsī ānjìng
options.text_en: talks more at home, quiet at work

options.text: 在家里比较安静，在公司说话多
options.pinyin: zài jiā lǐ bǐjiào ānjìng, zài gōngsī shuō huà duō
options.text_en: quieter at home, talks more at work

questions.explanation: At home the narrator is quieter; at work, talks more — two different versions of the same person.

questions.q_zh: 从职业选择可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we infer from the career choice?
questions.explanation: The career and name choices reflect what the narrator values in each world.

title: The Accent That Stays
text_zh: 我在北京住了十年了，但是说话的时候还有老家的口音。有人听了就问我：「你是南方人吧？」我说是。虽然我已经习惯了北京的生活，但是一开口说话，大家就知道我不是本地人。有时候我想改，但是又觉得这个口音是我的一部分。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài Běijīng zhù le shí nián le, dànshì shuō huà de shíhou hái yǒu lǎojiā de kǒuyīn. Yǒu rén tīng le jiù wèn wǒ:「Nǐ shì nánfāng rén ba?」Wǒ shuō shì. Suīrán wǒ yǐjīng xíguàn le Běijīng de shēnghuó, dànshì yì kāikǒu shuō huà, dàjiā jiù zhīdào wǒ búshì běndì rén. Yǒu shíhou wǒ xiǎng gǎi, dànshì yòu juéde zhège kǒuyīn shì wǒ de yí bùfen.
text_en: I've lived in Beijing for ten years, but when I speak I still have my hometown accent. People hear it and ask: 'You're from the south, right?' I say yes. Even though I'm used to Beijing life, the moment I open my mouth, everyone knows I'm not local. Sometimes I want to change it, but then I feel this accent is part of me.

questions.q_zh: 「我」在北京住了多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long has 'I' lived in Beijing?
questions.explanation: 我在北京住了十年了 — 十年 directly answers the duration: ten years.

questions.q_zh: 别人怎么知道「我」不是北京人？
questions.q_en: How do others know 'I' isn't from Beijing?

options.pinyin: tīng tā shuō huà de kǒuyīn

questions.explanation: People hear the southern accent and ask where the narrator is from.

questions.q_zh: 「我」想改口音吗？
questions.q_en: Does 'I' want to change the accent?

options.text: 有时候想改，但觉得是自己的一部分
options.pinyin: yǒu shíhou xiǎng gǎi, dàn juéde shì zìjǐ de yí bùfen
options.text_en: sometimes wants to, but feels it's part of self

questions.explanation: The narrator sometimes wants to change it but feels 'it's part of who I am.'

title: What My Handwriting Says
text_zh: 老师说从一个人写的字可以看到这个人。我写的字很小，一个一个挨得很近。朋友说我写的字像我这个人——不想让别人看到太多。我想了想，觉得他说得有道理。但是我不想改，因为这就是我。
text_pinyin: Lǎoshī shuō cóng yí gè rén xiě de zì kěyǐ kàn dào zhège rén. Wǒ xiě de zì hěn xiǎo, yí gè yí gè āi de hěn jìn. Péngyou shuō wǒ xiě de zì xiàng wǒ zhège rén——bù xiǎng ràng biéren kàn dào tài duō. Wǒ xiǎng le xiǎng, juéde tā shuō de yǒu dàoli. Dànshì wǒ bù xiǎng gǎi, yīnwèi zhè jiùshì wǒ.
text_en: My teacher says you can see a person through their handwriting. My characters are small, packed close together. A friend said my writing is like me — not wanting others to see too much. I thought about it and felt he had a point. But I don't want to change, because this is who I am.

questions.q_zh: 这些人之间最重要的是什么？
questions.q_en: What matters most between these people?
questions.explanation: The friend reads personality from handwriting — that takes real understanding of each other.

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: A friend accurately describes the narrator through handwriting — they clearly know each other well.

questions.q_zh: 「我」想改自己的字吗？
questions.q_en: Does 'I' want to change the handwriting?

options.pinyin: bù xiǎng gǎi, yīnwèi zhè jiùshì wǒ
options.text_en: no, because this is who I am

options.pinyin: zài xiǎng yào bu yào gǎi

questions.explanation: The narrator says 'no, because this is who I am' — the handwriting is part of identity.

title: My Father's Trade
text_zh: 爸爸是做木头东西的。他的手很大，上面有很多旧的伤。小时候我觉得他的工作不好看。长大以后我才知道，他做的每一个东西都是用心做的。现在有人问我爸爸做什么工作，我会很高兴地告诉他们。
text_pinyin: Bàba shì zuò mùtou dōngxi de. Tā de shǒu hěn dà, shàngmiàn yǒu hěn duō jiù de shāng. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ juéde tā de gōngzuò bù hǎokàn. Zhǎng dà yǐhòu wǒ cái zhīdào, tā zuò de měi yí gè dōngxi dōu shì yòngxīn zuò de. Xiànzài yǒu rén wèn wǒ bàba zuò shénme gōngzuò, wǒ huì hěn gāoxìng de gàosu tāmen.
text_en: My father makes things from wood. His hands are large, covered in old scars. When I was little I thought his job wasn't impressive. Only after growing up did I realize everything he makes is made with care. Now when people ask what my father does, I'm happy to tell them.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么不直接说出结论？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the author state the conclusion directly?

options.text_en: to let the reader experience it themselves
the author doesn't know the answer

questions.explanation: The passage lets you watch the narrator's view change over time — you discover the lesson yourself.

questions.q_zh: 小时候「我」觉得爸爸的工作怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel about Dad's job as a child?
questions.explanation: As a child, the narrator 'thought Dad's job wasn't impressive.'

questions.q_zh: 长大以后「我」的想法变了吗？
questions.q_en: Did 'I's thinking change after growing up?

options.text: 变了，现在很高兴地告诉别人
options.pinyin: biàn le, xiànzài hěn gāoxìng de gàosu biéren

options.text_en: doesn't want to talk about it

questions.explanation: After growing up, the narrator 'happily tells others' about Dad's work — a complete reversal.

title: The Train Timetable
text_zh: 我喜欢看火车站的时刻表。上面有很多城市的名字，有的我去过，有的我没去过。每一列火车都要去一个地方，带着不同的人。看着时刻表，我就觉得好像自己也可以去很远的地方。虽然今天我只是来送朋友的。
text_pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuan kàn huǒchē zhàn de shíkèbiǎo. Shàngmiàn yǒu hěn duō chéngshì de míngzi, yǒude wǒ qù guò, yǒude wǒ méi qù guò. Měi yí liè huǒchē dōu yào qù yí gè dìfang, dàizhe bùtóng de rén. Kànzhe shíkèbiǎo, wǒ jiù juéde hǎoxiàng zìjǐ yě kěyǐ qù hěn yuǎn de dìfang. Suīrán jīntiān wǒ zhǐshì lái sòng péngyou de.
text_en: I like looking at the train station timetable. It has the names of many cities — some I've been to, some I haven't. Every train goes somewhere, carrying different people. Looking at the timetable, I feel like I too could go somewhere far. Even though today I'm only here to see a friend off.

questions.q_zh: 时刻表上有什么？
questions.q_en: What's on the timetable?
questions.explanation: The timetable lists 'many city names' — places the narrator has and hasn't been.

questions.q_zh: 看时刻表的时候，「我」有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel looking at the timetable?

options.text: 觉得自己也可以去很远的地方
options.pinyin: juéde zìjǐ yě kěyǐ qù hěn yuǎn de dìfang
options.text_en: feels could go far away too

questions.explanation: Looking at all those destinations, the narrator 'feels like I could go far away too.'

questions.q_zh: 「我」今天来火车站做什么？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' come to the station today?
questions.explanation: 今天是来送朋友的 — 送 means to see someone off; the narrator came for a farewell, not personal travel.

title: The Overnight Train
text_zh: 第一次坐夜里的火车，我很兴奋。但是上了车以后，发现旁边的人都睡了。车里的灯关了，只有窗外面的光一直在动。我看了一夜的窗外，看到了很多我不知道名字的小地方。早上到了的时候，我虽然很累，但是觉得这一夜比睡觉有意思多了。
text_pinyin: Dì yī cì zuò yè lǐ de huǒchē, wǒ hěn xīngfèn. Dànshì shàng le chē yǐhòu, fāxiàn pángbiān de rén dōu shuì le. Chē lǐ de dēng guān le, zhǐ yǒu chuāng wàimiàn de guāng yìzhí zài dòng. Wǒ kàn le yí yè de chuāng wài, kàn dào le hěn duō wǒ bù zhīdào míngzi de xiǎo dìfang. Zǎoshang dào le de shíhou, wǒ suīrán hěn lèi, dànshì juéde zhè yí yè bǐ shuì jiào yǒu yìsi duō le.
text_en: The first time I took an overnight train, I was excited. But after boarding, everyone around me was asleep. The lights were off — only the light outside the window kept moving. I watched out the window all night, seeing many small places whose names I didn't know. When morning came, I was tired, but I felt this night was far more interesting than sleeping.

questions.q_zh: 上了车以后，别人在做什么？
questions.q_en: What were others doing after boarding?
questions.explanation: After boarding, 'everyone around was asleep' — the narrator was the only one awake.

questions.q_zh: 「我」在车上做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' do on the train?

options.pinyin: kàn le yí yè de chuāng wài
options.text_en: watched out the window all night

questions.explanation: The narrator 'watched out the window all night' — couldn't sleep from excitement.

questions.q_zh: 到了以后「我」觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel upon arrival?

options.text: 虽然很累，但是觉得很有意思
options.pinyin: suīrán hěn lèi, dànshì juéde hěn yǒu yìsi

questions.explanation: Upon arrival, the narrator was 'tired but found it interesting.'

title: The Barber's Mirror
text_zh: 理发店的镜子里可以看到很多东西。有人坐在椅子上看手机，不说话。有人跟理发的人聊天，聊得很高兴。我喜欢从镜子里看外面的街——人在走，车在过，但是在镜子里看起来好像是另一个世界。
text_pinyin: Lǐfà diàn de jìngzi lǐ kěyǐ kàn dào hěn duō dōngxi. Yǒu rén zuò zài yǐzi shàng kàn shǒujī, bù shuō huà. Yǒu rén gēn lǐfà de rén liáotiān, liáo de hěn gāoxìng. Wǒ xǐhuan cóng jìngzi lǐ kàn wàimiàn de jiē——rén zài zǒu, chē zài guò, dànshì zài jìngzi lǐ kàn qǐlái hǎoxiàng shì lìng yí gè shìjiè.
text_en: You can see a lot in the barbershop mirror. Some sit in the chair looking at phones, saying nothing. Some chat happily with the barber. I like watching the street outside through the mirror — people walking, cars passing, but in the mirror it looks like another world.

questions.q_zh: 有的人在理发店做什么？
questions.q_en: What do some people do at the barbershop?
questions.explanation: Some look at phones, some chat — the passage describes both types.

questions.q_zh: 为什么作者注意到这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why does the author notice these details?

options.text: 这些细节揭示了更深的意义
options.pinyin: zhèxiē xìjié jiēshì le gèng shēn de yìyì

questions.explanation: The narrator watches people in mirrors, noticing behavior differences — small details that say a lot.

questions.q_zh: 为什么作者注意到这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why does the author notice these details?

options.text: 这些细节揭示了更深的意义
options.pinyin: zhèxiē xìjié jiēshì le gèng shēn de yìyì

questions.explanation: Phone users vs. chatters, expressions in the mirror — these small details reveal how people differ.

title: The Cigarette Break
text_zh: 公司楼下有一个地方，大家都去那里抽烟。虽然我不抽烟，但是有时候也去那里站一会儿。因为在那里，不同部门的人会说话，而且说的都是真话。五分钟的休息时间，我听到的比开会的时候多。
text_pinyin: Gōngsī lóu xià yǒu yí gè dìfang, dàjiā dōu qù nàli chōu yān. Suīrán wǒ bù chōu yān, dànshì yǒu shíhou yě qù nàli zhàn yíhuìr. Yīnwèi zài nàli, bùtóng bùmén de rén huì shuō huà, érqiě shuō de dōu shì zhēn huà. Wǔ fēnzhōng de xiūxi shíjiān, wǒ tīng dào de bǐ kāi huì de shíhou duō.
text_en: Downstairs from the office there's a spot where everyone goes to smoke. Although I don't smoke, sometimes I stand there for a while. Because there, people from different departments talk, and they tell the truth. In five minutes of break time, I hear more than in meetings.

questions.q_zh: 「我」抽烟吗？
questions.q_en: Does 'I' smoke?
questions.explanation: 我不抽烟 — despite not smoking, the narrator visits for the cross-department conversations that happen there.

questions.q_zh: 在抽烟的地方，人们说话有什么特别的？
questions.q_en: What's special about how people talk there?

options.pinyin: shuō de dōu shì zhēn huà

questions.explanation: At the smoking spot, 'people tell the truth' — it's more honest than formal settings.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得在那里听到的跟开会比怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' compare it to meetings?

options.pinyin: tīng dào de bǐ kāi huì shǎo
tīng dào de bǐ kāi huì duō

questions.explanation: The narrator says 'I hear more there than in meetings' — real talk happens outside the office.

title: The Old Couple on the Bench
text_zh: 每天下午，公园的长椅上都坐着两个老人。他们不怎么说话，有时候一个人看报纸，另一个人看远方。但是他们总是坐得很近。有一次我看到老太太冷了，老先生什么都没说，把自己的外套给了她。好像几十年了，他们已经不需要说话了。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān xiàwǔ, gōngyuán de cháng yǐ shàng dōu zuòzhe liǎng gè lǎorén. Tāmen bù zěnme shuō huà, yǒu shíhou yí gè rén kàn bàozhǐ, lìng yí gè rén kàn yuǎnfāng. Dànshì tāmen zǒngshì zuò de hěn jìn. Yǒu yí cì wǒ kàn dào lǎo tàitai lěng le, lǎo xiānsheng shénme dōu méi shuō, bǎ zìjǐ de wàitào gěi le tā. Hǎoxiàng jǐ shí nián le, tāmen yǐjīng bù xūyào shuō huà le.
text_en: Every afternoon, two old people sit on the park bench. They don't talk much — one reads the newspaper, the other looks into the distance. But they always sit close together. Once I saw the old lady was cold. The old man said nothing, just gave her his coat. As if after decades, they no longer need words.

questions.q_zh: 两个老人在公园做什么？
questions.q_en: What do the two old people do in the park?

options.text: 坐在长椅上，不怎么说话
options.pinyin: zuò zài cháng yǐ shàng, bù zěnme shuō huà
options.text_en: sit on the bench, don't talk much

questions.explanation: They 'don't talk much' — one reads, the other looks into the distance. Quiet companionship.

questions.q_zh: 老太太冷的时候，老先生怎么做了？
questions.q_en: What did the old man do when the lady was cold?

options.pinyin: wèn tā yào bu yào huí jiā
options.text_en: asked if she wanted to go home

options.text: 什么都没说，把外套给了她
options.pinyin: shénme dōu méi shuō, bǎ wàitào gěi le tā
options.text_en: said nothing, gave her his coat

options.pinyin: qù mǎi le yì bēi rè chá

questions.explanation: Without a word, 'he gave her his coat' — actions speak louder than words.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得他们为什么不说话？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think they don't talk?

options.text: 因为几十年了，不需要说话了
options.pinyin: yīnwèi jǐ shí nián le, bù xūyào shuō huà le
options.text_en: after decades, no longer need words

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tāmen bú huì shuō huà
yīnwèi gōngyuán lǐ bù néng shuō huà
options.text_en: because you can't talk in the park

questions.explanation: The narrator thinks 'after decades, they no longer need words' — they understand each other already.

title: The Delivery Driver
text_zh: 每天中午，送外卖的人很忙。他们骑着车，在车和人中间穿过去。有一次我在楼下等外卖，那个人跑上来的时候满头都是汗。他笑着把饭给我，说：「还好没有晚。」我说了谢谢，但是觉得只说谢谢好像不够。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zhōngwǔ, sòng wàimài de rén hěn máng. Tāmen qízhe chē, zài chē hé rén zhōngjiān chuān guòqù. Yǒu yí cì wǒ zài lóu xià děng wàimài, nàge rén pǎo shànglái de shíhou mǎn tóu dōu shì hàn. Tā xiàozhe bǎ fàn gěi wǒ, shuō:「Hái hǎo méiyǒu wǎn.」Wǒ shuō le xièxie, dànshì juéde zhǐ shuō xièxie hǎoxiàng bú gòu.
text_en: Every noon, delivery drivers are busy. They ride through traffic and crowds. Once I waited downstairs for a delivery. When the driver ran up, his head was covered in sweat. He smiled, handed me the food, and said: 'Good thing I wasn't late.' I said thank you, but felt like just saying thank you wasn't enough.

questions.q_zh: 送外卖的人中午怎么样？
questions.q_en: How are delivery drivers at noon?
questions.explanation: 外卖员很忙 — noon is peak delivery time, so they ride through traffic and crowds to meet demand.

questions.q_zh: 那个人跑上来的时候看起来怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did the driver look when he ran up?
questions.explanation: When the driver ran up, 'his head was covered in sweat.'

questions.q_zh: 「我」说了谢谢以后觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel after saying thank you?

options.pinyin: juéde zhǐ shuō xièxie bú gòu

questions.explanation: After seeing the driver's sweat and effort, 'thank you' felt like it wasn't enough.

title: The Stray Cat's Schedule
text_zh: 小区里有一只流浪猫。它好像有自己的时间表：早上在一楼老太太门口吃东西，中午在三楼的阳台上睡觉，下午在花园里走来走去。虽然没有人是它的主人，但是好像每个人都在照顾它。它比我们都自由。
text_pinyin: Xiǎoqū lǐ yǒu yì zhī liúlàng māo. Tā hǎoxiàng yǒu zìjǐ de shíjiānbiǎo: zǎoshang zài yī lóu lǎo tàitai ménkǒu chī dōngxi, zhōngwǔ zài sān lóu de yángtái shàng shuì jiào, xiàwǔ zài huāyuán lǐ zǒu lái zǒu qù. Suīrán méiyǒu rén shì tā de zhǔrén, dànshì hǎoxiàng měi gè rén dōu zài zhàogù tā. Tā bǐ wǒmen dōu zìyóu.
text_en: There's a stray cat in the compound. It seems to have its own schedule: morning, it eats at the first-floor old lady's door; noon, it sleeps on the third-floor balcony; afternoon, it wanders the garden. Although nobody owns it, everyone seems to take care of it. It's freer than all of us.

questions.q_zh: 食物在这段话中代表什么？
questions.q_en: What does food represent in this passage?
questions.explanation: The old lady feeds the cat every morning — the food is an act of care and warmth.

questions.q_zh: 做饭/吃饭的场景暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the eating/cooking scene imply?
questions.explanation: The cat eats, sleeps, and wanders on its own schedule — simple daily happiness.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得猫跟人比怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' compare the cat to people?

options.text_en: cat is freer than all of us
cat is the same as people

questions.explanation: The narrator says the cat 'is freer than all of us' — no schedule, no obligations.

title: The Closed Bookshop
text_zh: 路口的那家书店关了。以前每次走过那里，都能看到里面亮着灯。老板总是坐在最里面看书。现在门上贴了一张纸，上面写着「谢谢大家这些年」。我站在门口看了一会儿，觉得这条街少了什么。
text_pinyin: Lùkǒu de nà jiā shūdiàn guān le. Yǐqián měi cì zǒu guò nàli, dōu néng kàn dào lǐmiàn liàngzhe dēng. Lǎobǎn zǒngshì zuò zài zuì lǐmiàn kàn shū. Xiànzài mén shàng tiē le yì zhāng zhǐ, shàngmiàn xiězhe「xièxie dàjiā zhèxiē nián」. Wǒ zhàn zài ménkǒu kàn le yíhuìr, juéde zhè tiáo jiē shǎo le shénme.
text_en: The bookshop at the intersection has closed. Before, every time I walked past, the lights were on inside. The owner always sat in the back reading. Now there's a note on the door: 'Thank you everyone for these years.' I stood at the entrance for a while, feeling like something was missing from this street.

questions.q_zh: 书店的老板以前总是在做什么？
questions.q_en: What did the bookshop owner always do?

options.pinyin: zuò zài zuì lǐmiàn kàn shū

questions.explanation: The owner always 'sat in the back reading' — that was the constant image.

questions.q_zh: 门上的纸写了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the note on the door say?

options.text_en: thank you everyone for these years

options.pinyin: zài xiū, hěn kuài jiù hǎo

questions.explanation: The note on the door said 'thank you everyone for these years.'

questions.q_zh: 「我」站在门口有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel standing at the entrance?

options.pinyin: juéde zhè tiáo jiē shǎo le shénme
options.text_en: felt something was missing from the street

questions.explanation: Standing at the closed entrance, the narrator 'felt something was missing from the street.'

title: The Alley Between Buildings
text_zh: 两栋楼之间有一条很窄的小巷。大多数人都不走那里。但是如果你走进去，会发现一个老人在那里种花。花长在各种各样的旧东西里——旧鞋、旧杯子、旧碗。他说：「什么东西都可以种花，别扔了。」
text_pinyin: Liǎng dòng lóu zhījiān yǒu yì tiáo hěn zhǎi de xiǎo xiàng. Dàduōshù rén dōu bù zǒu nàli. Dànshì rúguǒ nǐ zǒu jìnqù, huì fāxiàn yí gè lǎorén zài nàli zhòng huā. Huā zhǎng zài gè zhǒng gè yàng de jiù dōngxi lǐ——jiù xié, jiù bēizi, jiù wǎn. Tā shuō:「Shénme dōngxi dōu kěyǐ zhòng huā, bié rēng le.」
text_en: Between two buildings there's a narrow alley. Most people don't go there. But if you walk in, you'll find an old man growing flowers. The flowers grow in all sorts of old things — old shoes, old cups, old bowls. He says: 'You can grow flowers in anything. Don't throw things away.'

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么样的变化？
questions.q_en: What kind of change does this passage suggest?
questions.explanation: Finding hidden beauty in a neglected alley — the discovery represents a shift in perspective.

questions.q_zh: 花长在什么里面？
questions.q_en: What do the flowers grow in?
questions.explanation: The flowers grow in 'various old things' — repurposed containers the old man collected.

questions.q_zh: 老人说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the old man say?

options.text: 什么东西都可以种花，别扔了
options.pinyin: shénme dōngxi dōu kěyǐ zhòng huā, bié rēng le
options.text_en: you can grow flowers in anything, don't throw things away

options.pinyin: zhèxiē huā shì wǒ mǎi de

questions.explanation: The old man said 'you can grow flowers in anything, don't throw things away.'

title: The Crosswalk
text_zh: 红灯的时候，大家都站在路口等。有的人看手机，有的人看对面。绿灯一亮，大家一起走过去。从这边到那边，只要二十秒。在这二十秒里，很多不认识的人一起走在同一条路上。然后到了对面，大家又各走各的路了。
text_pinyin: Hóng dēng de shíhou, dàjiā dōu zhàn zài lùkǒu děng. Yǒude rén kàn shǒujī, yǒude rén kàn duìmiàn. Lǜ dēng yí liàng, dàjiā yìqǐ zǒu guòqù. Cóng zhè biān dào nà biān, zhǐ yào èrshí miǎo. Zài zhè èrshí miǎo lǐ, hěn duō bú rènshi de rén yìqǐ zǒu zài tóng yì tiáo lù shàng. Ránhòu dào le duìmiàn, dàjiā yòu gè zǒu gè de lù le.
text_en: When the light is red, everyone stands at the intersection waiting. Some look at phones, some watch across. When green lights up, everyone walks across together. From this side to that — only twenty seconds. In those twenty seconds, many strangers walk the same path together. Then they reach the other side and go their separate ways.

questions.q_zh: 从这边走到那边要多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long to cross?
questions.explanation: 只要二十秒 — the crossing distance is measured at just twenty seconds.

questions.q_zh: 过马路的人互相认识吗？
questions.q_en: Do the people crossing know each other?
questions.explanation: 他们互相不认识 — complete strangers briefly walk together, then scatter on the other side.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中的'重复'有什么深意？
questions.q_en: What deeper meaning does the 'repetition' in this passage carry?
questions.explanation: The same crossing happens every day, same pattern — the repetition provides a sense of steadiness.

title: The Corner Store Owner
text_zh: 街角有一个小店，什么都卖。老板是一个中年女人，一天到晚都在店里。她认识住在这里的每一个人。你买东西的时候，她会说：「你妈妈昨天也来买了这个。」或者「你们家的小孩上学了吧？」她知道的比谁都多。
text_pinyin: Jiē jiǎo yǒu yí gè xiǎo diàn, shénme dōu mài. Lǎobǎn shì yí gè zhōngnián nǚrén, yì tiān dào wǎn dōu zài diàn lǐ. Tā rènshi zhù zài zhèlǐ de měi yí gè rén. Nǐ mǎi dōngxi de shíhou, tā huì shuō:「Nǐ māma zuótiān yě lái mǎi le zhège.」Huòzhě「nǐmen jiā de xiǎohái shàng xué le ba?」Tā zhīdào de bǐ shéi dōu duō.
text_en: On the corner there's a small shop that sells everything. The owner is a middle-aged woman who's in the shop from morning to night. She knows every person who lives here. When you buy something, she'll say: 'Your mom bought this yesterday too.' Or: 'Your kid has started school, right?' She knows more than anyone.

questions.q_zh: 岁月的描写想要传达什么？
questions.q_en: What is the description of time meant to convey?
questions.explanation: The owner's whole life is in the shop, morning to night — the time details reflect on a life's rhythm.

questions.q_zh: 老板认识谁？
questions.q_en: Who does the owner know?

options.pinyin: zhù zài zhèlǐ de měi yí gè rén

questions.explanation: 她认识这里住的每一个人 — years at the corner shop mean she's familiar with every local resident.

questions.q_zh: 这个选择反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this choice reflect?
questions.explanation: Staying in one small shop for years reflects a choice about what matters — personal values.

title: The Sound of the City Waking Up
text_zh: 如果你五点起来，会听到城市醒来的声音。先是扫地的人，然后是早饭店开门的声音。接着是公共汽车开过的声音。最后是人说话的声音。每天都是这个顺序，从来不变。我觉得一个城市的早上比晚上更真实。
text_pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ wǔ diǎn qǐlái, huì tīng dào chéngshì xǐng lái de shēngyīn. Xiān shì sǎo dì de rén, ránhòu shì zǎofàn diàn kāi mén de shēngyīn. Jiēzhe shì gōnggòng qìchē kāi guò de shēngyīn. Zuìhòu shì rén shuō huà de shēngyīn. Měi tiān dōu shì zhège shùnxù, cónglái bú biàn. Wǒ juéde yí gè chéngshì de zǎoshang bǐ wǎnshang gèng zhēnshí.
text_en: If you wake up at five, you hear the city waking up. First the sweepers, then the breakfast shops opening. Then buses passing. Finally, people talking. Every day the same order, never changing. I think a city's morning is more real than its night.

questions.q_zh: 城市早上最先听到什么声音？
questions.q_en: What's the first sound in the morning?
questions.explanation: 先是扫地的人 — sweepers come first in the sequence, before shops, buses, or voices.

questions.q_zh: 这些声音的顺序会变吗？
questions.q_en: Does the order of sounds change?
questions.explanation: 每天顺序都一样,从来不变 — the fixed sequence repeats identically each morning, giving the city a reliable rhythm.

questions.q_zh: 这种坚持说明这个人怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does this persistence tell us about the person?

options.text_en: has no ideas of their own

questions.explanation: The same sounds, same order, every day without fail — that consistency shows commitment.

title: The Bus Stop Conversations
text_zh: 等公共汽车的时候，有时候旁边的人会跟你说话。今天一个老太太问我几点了。我告诉她以后，她又问我去哪里。然后她说她女儿也住在那个方向。等车来的时候，我已经知道了她女儿的工作、她家的猫、还有她最喜欢的饭馆。
text_pinyin: Děng gōnggòng qìchē de shíhou, yǒu shíhou pángbiān de rén huì gēn nǐ shuō huà. Jīntiān yí gè lǎo tàitai wèn wǒ jǐ diǎn le. Wǒ gàosu tā yǐhòu, tā yòu wèn wǒ qù nǎli. Ránhòu tā shuō tā nǚ'ér yě zhù zài nàge fāngxiàng. Děng chē lái de shíhou, wǒ yǐjīng zhīdào le tā nǚ'ér de gōngzuò, tā jiā de māo, háiyǒu tā zuì xǐhuan de fànguǎn.
text_en: While waiting for the bus, sometimes the person next to you talks to you. Today an old lady asked me what time it was. After I told her, she asked where I was going. Then she said her daughter also lives in that direction. By the time the bus came, I already knew her daughter's job, her cat, and her favorite restaurant.

questions.q_zh: 老太太先问了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the old lady ask first?
questions.explanation: The old lady started by asking 'what time is it.'

questions.q_zh: 从职业选择可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we infer from the career choice?
questions.explanation: Through a simple bus-stop chat, the old lady shares her whole life — that openness reflects her values.

questions.q_zh: 车来的时候，「我」知道了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' know by the time the bus came?

options.pinyin: zhǐ zhīdào tā zhù zài nǎli

options.text: 她女儿的工作、她家的猫和她最喜欢的饭馆
options.pinyin: tā nǚ'ér de gōngzuò, tā jiā de māo hé tā zuì xǐhuan de fànguǎn
options.text_en: daughter's job, her cat, favorite restaurant

questions.explanation: By the time the bus came, the narrator knew about 'her daughter's job, her cat, and favorite restaurant.'

title: The Office Thermostat War
text_zh: 我们办公室每天都有一个问题：空调的温度。坐在窗旁边的人觉得太热，要开低一点。坐在里面的人觉得太冷，要开高一点。每天有人调高，就有人调低。最后大家决定轮流管空调——一天一个人。但是问题还是一样。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen bàngōngshì měi tiān dōu yǒu yí gè wèntí: kōngtiáo de wēndù. Zuò zài chuāng pángbiān de rén juéde tài rè, yào kāi dī yìdiǎn. Zuò zài lǐmiàn de rén juéde tài lěng, yào kāi gāo yìdiǎn. Měi tiān yǒu rén tiáo gāo, jiù yǒu rén tiáo dī. Zuìhòu dàjiā juédìng lúnliú guǎn kōngtiáo——yì tiān yí gè rén. Dànshì wèntí háishi yíyàng.
text_en: Our office has a daily problem: the air conditioner temperature. People by the window think it's too hot, wanting it lower. People inside think it's too cold, wanting it higher. Every day someone turns it up, someone turns it down. Finally everyone decided to take turns controlling it — one person per day. But the problem is still the same.

questions.q_zh: 办公室每天有什么问题？
questions.q_en: What daily problem does the office have?
questions.explanation: 空调的温度 is the daily dispute — window-side people want it colder, interior people want it warmer.

questions.q_zh: 坐在窗旁边的人觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How do the people by the window feel?
questions.explanation: People by the window 'think it's too hot' and want it lower.

questions.q_zh: 大家的解决办法有用吗？
questions.q_en: Did their solution work?

options.text_en: no, problem is still the same

questions.explanation: Despite attempts, 'the problem is still the same' — no solution worked.

title: The School Notice Board
text_zh: 学校门口有一个公告栏。上面有很多纸条。有找猫的，有卖自行车的，有找人一起学英语的。我最喜欢看的是一张很旧的纸条，上面写着：「找一个人一起吃午饭。」下面只有一个电话号码。我不知道这个人找到人了没有，但是我觉得这是最诚实的一张。
text_pinyin: Xuéxiào ménkǒu yǒu yí gè gōnggào lán. Shàngmiàn yǒu hěn duō zhǐtiáo. Yǒu zhǎo māo de, yǒu mài zìxíngchē de, yǒu zhǎo rén yìqǐ xué Yīngyǔ de. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan kàn de shì yì zhāng hěn jiù de zhǐtiáo, shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Zhǎo yí gè rén yìqǐ chī wǔfàn.」Xiàmiàn zhǐ yǒu yí gè diànhuà hàomǎ. Wǒ bù zhīdào zhège rén zhǎo dào rén le méiyǒu, dànshì wǒ juéde zhè shì zuì chéngshí de yì zhāng.
text_en: There's a notice board at the school entrance. Many notes on it. Some looking for lost cats, some selling bicycles, some looking for English study partners. My favorite is an old note that reads: 'Looking for someone to eat lunch together.' Just a phone number below. I don't know if they found someone, but I think it's the most honest note.

questions.q_zh: 这个选择反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this choice reflect?
questions.explanation: Choosing what to post on the board reflects what each person values and needs.

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: The different notes show what each person needs — the variety reveals how well the narrator reads people.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么觉得那张纸条特别？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think that note is special?
questions.explanation: That particular note stood out because 'it's the most honest' — direct and real.

title: The Company's Best Day
text_zh: 我们公司每个星期五下午三点以后不开会。这是公司最老的规矩。有人说这个规矩是因为以前的老板觉得星期五下午大家都想回家。因为这样，星期五下午变成了大家最喜欢的时间。没有人打电话，没有人发消息，大家安安静静地做自己的事情。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen gōngsī měi gè xīngqī wǔ xiàwǔ sān diǎn yǐhòu bù kāi huì. Zhè shì gōngsī zuì lǎo de guījǔ. Yǒu rén shuō zhège guījǔ shì yīnwèi yǐqián de lǎobǎn juéde xīngqī wǔ xiàwǔ dàjiā dōu xiǎng huí jiā. Yīnwèi zhèyàng, xīngqī wǔ xiàwǔ biànchéng le dàjiā zuì xǐhuan de shíjiān. Méiyǒu rén dǎ diànhuà, méiyǒu rén fā xiāoxi, dàjiā ān'ānjìngjìng de zuò zìjǐ de shìqing.
text_en: Our company doesn't hold meetings after 3 PM on Fridays. It's the oldest rule in the company. Some say it started because the former boss felt everyone wanted to go home on Friday afternoons. Because of this, Friday afternoon became everyone's favorite time. Nobody calls, nobody messages. Everyone quietly does their own thing.

questions.q_zh: 星期五下午三点以后不做什么？
questions.q_en: What doesn't happen after 3 PM on Fridays?
questions.explanation: 星期五下午三点以后不开会 — this is the company's oldest rule, giving everyone an early mental break.

questions.q_zh: 这个规矩是怎么来的？
questions.q_en: How did this rule originate?

options.text: 以前的老板觉得大家想回家
options.pinyin: yǐqián de lǎobǎn juéde dàjiā xiǎng huí jiā
options.text_en: former boss felt everyone wanted to go home

questions.explanation: The former boss 'felt everyone wanted to go home' on Friday afternoons, so he made the rule.

questions.q_zh: 从职业选择可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we infer from the career choice?
questions.explanation: Keeping a rule that prioritizes rest over productivity shows what the company values.

title: The Park's Morning Radio
text_zh: 每天早上六点半，公园的广播就开了。放的是做早操的音乐。虽然现在年轻人都不做早操了，但是老人们还是每天来。音乐一响，他们就开始动。这个广播已经放了十几年了，音乐从来没有换过。也许对他们来说，换了音乐就不一样了。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn, gōngyuán de guǎngbō jiù kāi le. Fàng de shì zuò zǎocāo de yīnyuè. Suīrán xiànzài niánqīng rén dōu bú zuò zǎocāo le, dànshì lǎorénmen háishi měi tiān lái. Yīnyuè yí xiǎng, tāmen jiù kāishǐ dòng. Zhège guǎngbō yǐjīng fàng le shí jǐ nián le, yīnyuè cónglái méiyǒu huàn guò. Yěxǔ duì tāmen lái shuō, huàn le yīnyuè jiù bù yíyàng le.
text_en: Every morning at six-thirty, the park broadcast starts. It plays morning exercise music. Although young people don't do morning exercises anymore, the elderly still come every day. When the music starts, they begin to move. This broadcast has played for over ten years. The music has never changed. Perhaps for them, different music wouldn't be the same.

questions.q_zh: 如果打破了这个习惯，可能会怎样？
questions.q_en: What might happen if this routine were broken?

options.pinyin: huì zhǎodào gèng hǎo de xíguàn

questions.explanation: The broadcast and music are part of daily routine — without them, the elderly would feel something's wrong.

questions.q_zh: 现在谁还做早操？
questions.q_en: Who still does morning exercises?
questions.explanation: Young people don't do morning exercises anymore, but 'the elderly' still come every day.

questions.q_zh: 音乐换过吗？
questions.q_en: Has the music been changed?
questions.explanation: 音乐从来没有换过 — 从来没有 ('never once') emphasizes the songs have stayed identical for years.

title: The GPS Argument
text_zh: 爸爸开车的时候用手机导航。导航说往左，爸爸觉得应该往右。妈妈说：「听导航的。」爸爸说：「我在这里住了二十年了，我比手机知道得多。」最后我们走错了路。爸爸安静了五分钟，然后又开了导航。
text_pinyin: Bàba kāi chē de shíhou yòng shǒujī dǎoháng. Dǎoháng shuō wǎng zuǒ, bàba juéde yīnggāi wǎng yòu. Māma shuō:「Tīng dǎoháng de.」Bàba shuō:「Wǒ zài zhèlǐ zhù le èrshí nián le, wǒ bǐ shǒujī zhīdào de duō.」Zuìhòu wǒmen zǒu cuò le lù. Bàba ānjìng le wǔ fēnzhōng, ránhòu yòu kāi le dǎoháng.
text_en: When Dad drives, he uses phone navigation. The GPS said go left; Dad thought it should be right. Mom said: 'Listen to the GPS.' Dad said: 'I've lived here twenty years. I know more than a phone.' We ended up going the wrong way. Dad was quiet for five minutes, then turned the GPS back on.

questions.q_zh: 为什么作者注意到这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why does the author notice these details?

options.text: 这些细节揭示了更深的意义
options.pinyin: zhèxiē xìjié jiēshì le gèng shēn de yìyì

questions.explanation: Dad arguing with GPS, going wrong, silently turning it back on — these details reveal stubborn pride.

questions.q_zh: 爸爸为什么觉得自己对？
questions.q_en: Why did Dad think he was right?

options.text_en: because he looked at a map

options.text: 因为他在这里住了二十年
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā zài zhèlǐ zhù le èrshí nián

questions.explanation: Dad said 'I've lived here 20 years' — local experience vs. technology.

questions.q_zh: 走错路以后爸爸怎么做了？
questions.q_en: What did Dad do after going the wrong way?

options.text: 安静了五分钟，然后又开了导航
options.pinyin: ānjìng le wǔ fēnzhōng, ránhòu yòu kāi le dǎoháng
options.text_en: was quiet 5 min, turned GPS back on

questions.explanation: After going the wrong way, Dad was quiet for 5 minutes then turned the GPS back on.

title: The Photo That Won't Work
text_zh: 我们一家人去公园拍照。妈妈让大家站好，说「一二三」。但是每次都有人没准备好。第一次弟弟在看别的地方。第二次爸爸的眼睛闭着。第三次我在笑。拍了十多次以后，妈妈看了看手机里的照片，发现每一张里只有她自己是完美的。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen yì jiā rén qù gōngyuán pāi zhào. Māma ràng dàjiā zhàn hǎo, shuō「yī èr sān」. Dànshì měi cì dōu yǒu rén méi zhǔnbèi hǎo. Dì yī cì dìdi zài kàn biéde dìfang. Dì èr cì bàba de yǎnjing bìzhe. Dì sān cì wǒ zài xiào. Pāi le shí duō cì yǐhòu, māma shuō:「Suàn le, zhèxiē bù wánměi de zhàopiàn kěnéng bǐ wánměi de gèng hǎokàn.」
text_en: Our whole family went to the park to take photos. Mom told everyone to stand ready and said 'one two three.' But every time someone wasn't ready. First time, my brother was looking elsewhere. Second time, Dad's eyes were closed. Third time, I was laughing. After more than ten tries, Mom looked through the photos on her phone and realized the only one perfect in every shot was herself.

questions.q_zh: 第一次拍照的时候，谁没准备好？
questions.q_en: Who wasn't ready the first time?
questions.explanation: The first time, 'younger brother' wasn't ready.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: The messy attempts, the laughter — the passage wants you to feel the family's joy.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈最后说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did Mom say in the end?

options.text: 不完美的照片可能更好看
options.pinyin: bù wánměi de zhàopiàn kěnéng gèng hǎokàn

questions.explanation: Mom finally said 'imperfect photos might be better' — accepting the chaos.

title: The Alarm That Doesn't Work
text_zh: 我设了三个闹钟：六点、六点十分、六点二十分。第一个响的时候，我关了继续睡。第二个响的时候，我又关了。第三个响的时候，我把手机放到了很远的地方。但是我起来关了以后，又回去睡了。最后叫醒我的是妈妈。她比所有的闹钟都管用。
text_pinyin: Wǒ shè le sān gè nàozhōng: liù diǎn, liù diǎn shí fēn, liù diǎn èrshí fēn. Dì yī gè xiǎng de shíhou, wǒ guān le jìxù shuì. Dì èr gè xiǎng de shíhou, wǒ yòu guān le. Dì sān gè xiǎng de shíhou, wǒ bǎ shǒujī fàng dào le hěn yuǎn de dìfang. Dànshì wǒ qǐlái guān le yǐhòu, yòu huíqù shuì le. Zuìhòu jiào xǐng wǒ de shì māma. Tā bǐ suǒyǒu de nàozhōng dōu guǎnyòng.
text_en: I set three alarms: six, six-ten, six-twenty. When the first one rang, I turned it off and kept sleeping. When the second rang, I turned it off again. For the third, I put my phone far away. But after getting up to turn it off, I went back to sleep. In the end, it was Mom who woke me. She's more effective than all the alarms.

questions.q_zh: 这段话想告诉我们什么？
questions.q_en: What is this passage trying to tell us?

options.text: 生活中的小事也很有意义
options.pinyin: shēnghuó zhōng de xiǎoshì yě hěn yǒu yìyì
options.text_en: small things in life are meaningful too

options.text: 这只是一个普通的故事
options.pinyin: zhè zhǐ shì yí gè pǔtōng de gùshi
options.text_en: this is just an ordinary story

questions.explanation: Three alarms, all ignored — a tiny daily battle that everyone recognizes as meaningful.

questions.q_zh: 第三个闹钟的时候「我」怎么做的？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' do for the third alarm?

options.pinyin: qǐlái guān le yòu huíqù shuì le
options.text_en: got up, turned it off, went back to sleep

questions.explanation: For the third alarm, the narrator 'got up, turned it off, and went back to sleep' — three for three.

questions.q_zh: 最后是谁叫醒了「我」？
questions.q_en: Who finally woke 'I' up?
questions.explanation: After all three alarms failed, 'Mom' finally woke the narrator up.

title: The Cooking Competition
text_zh: 我和哥哥比赛做饭。我做了炒饭，他做了面条。我们让妈妈来尝。妈妈先吃了我的，说：「还可以。」然后吃了哥哥的，也说：「还可以。」我们问她哪个更好吃。她想了很久，说：「都不怎么好吃，但是我很高兴你们会做饭了。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ hé gēge bǐsài zuò fàn. Wǒ zuò le chǎofàn, tā zuò le miàntiáo. Wǒmen ràng māma lái cháng. Māma xiān chī le wǒ de, shuō:「Hái kěyǐ.」Ránhòu chī le gēge de, yě shuō:「Hái kěyǐ.」Wǒmen wèn tā nǎge gèng hǎochī. Tā xiǎng le hěn jiǔ, shuō:「Dōu bù zěnme hǎochī, dànshì wǒ hěn gāoxìng nǐmen huì zuò fàn le.」
text_en: My brother and I had a cooking competition. I made fried rice, he made noodles. We had Mom taste them. She tried mine first and said: 'It's okay.' Then she tried my brother's and said: 'It's okay.' We asked which was better. She thought for a long time: 'Neither is good, but I'm happy you can cook now.'

questions.q_zh: 「我」做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' cook?
questions.explanation: 我做了炒饭 — fried rice is stated as the narrator's dish in the cooking competition.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈觉得谁做的更好吃？
questions.q_en: Whose cooking did Mom think was better?
questions.explanation: Mom's real opinion was 'neither was very good' — honest and fair.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈高兴的原因是什么？
questions.q_en: Why was Mom happy?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tāmen huì zuò fàn le
yīnwèi bú yòng zìjǐ zuò fàn
options.text_en: because she doesn't have to cook

questions.explanation: Mom was happy 'because they can cook now' — the skill matters more than the taste.

title: The Wrong Floor
text_zh: 昨天晚上很累，我走到五楼，用钥匙开门——开不了。试了三次都不行。我正要打电话给房东，突然看到门上的号码：502。我住的是602。我上了一层楼，门一下子就开了。进门的时候，我听到楼下502的门打开了。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān wǎnshang hěn lèi, wǒ zǒu dào wǔ lóu, yòng yàoshi kāi mén——kāi bù liǎo. Shì le sān cì dōu bù xíng. Wǒ zhèng yào dǎ diànhuà gěi fángdōng, tūrán kàn dào mén shàng de hàomǎ: wǔ líng èr. Wǒ zhù de shì liù líng èr. Wǒ shàng le yì céng lóu, mén yíxiàzi jiù kāi le. Xīwàng wǔ lóu de línjū méiyǒu tīng dào wǒ zài tāmen ménkǒu shì le sān cì yàoshi.
text_en: Last night I was tired. I walked to the fifth floor and tried to open the door with my key — it wouldn't open. Tried three times, no luck. Just as I was about to call the landlord, I noticed the number on the door: 502. I live in 602. I went up one floor and the door opened right away. As I stepped inside, I heard the door of 502 open below me.

questions.q_zh: 「我」走到了几楼？
questions.q_en: What floor did 'I' go to?
questions.explanation: The narrator went to 'the fifth floor' instead of the correct one.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以看出什么样的关系？
questions.q_en: What kind of relationship does this passage reveal?
questions.explanation: The neighbor laughed and helped — a warm, genuine interaction between people who know each other.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么走错了楼层？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' go to the wrong floor?
questions.explanation: The narrator went to the wrong floor 'because very tired' — exhaustion caused the mistake.

title: The Breakfast That Tells Time
text_zh: 我不用看表就知道几点了。六点，卖豆浆的人来了。六点半，卖包子的开门了。七点，买早饭的人排队了。七点半，卖早饭的人开始忙起来了。我妈妈说：「你不是在看时间，你是在看肚子。」她说得对。
text_pinyin: Wǒ bú yòng kàn biǎo jiù zhīdào jǐ diǎn le. Liù diǎn, mài dòujiāng de rén lái le. Liù diǎn bàn, mài bāozi de kāi mén le. Qī diǎn, mǎi zǎofàn de rén páiduì le. Qī diǎn bàn, mài zǎofàn de rén kāishǐ máng qǐlái le. Wǒ māma shuō:「Nǐ búshì zài kàn shíjiān, nǐ shì zài kàn dùzi.」Tā shuō de duì.
text_en: I can tell the time without looking at a watch. At six, the soy milk seller arrives. At six-thirty, the bun shop opens. At seven, people line up for breakfast. At seven-thirty, the sellers get busy. My mom says: 'You're not watching the time, you're watching your stomach.' She's right.

questions.q_zh: 六点半发生了什么？
questions.q_en: What happens at six-thirty?

options.pinyin: mài dòujiāng de rén lái le
mài bāozi de kāi mén le
mài zǎofàn de rén máng qǐlái le

questions.explanation: 六点半包子店开门 — the bun shop's opening time marks the six-thirty moment in the morning sequence.

questions.q_zh: 做饭/吃饭的场景暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the eating/cooking scene imply?
questions.explanation: Food sellers arriving in sequence, Mom cooking at noon — food marks the rhythm of daily happiness.

questions.q_zh: 「我」同意妈妈说的吗？
questions.q_en: Does 'I' agree with Mom?
questions.explanation: The narrator agrees with Mom that 'you can tell the time by what people eat.'

title: The Secret Menu
text_zh: 这个饭馆的菜单上有二十个菜。但是老客人都知道，还有三个菜没有写在上面。你要跟老板说「来个老样子」，他就知道做什么了。我去了很多次以后，终于有一天老板问我：「要不要老样子？」那一天，我觉得自己变成了这里的人。
text_pinyin: Zhège fànguǎn de càidān shàng yǒu èrshí gè cài. Dànshì lǎo kèrén dōu zhīdào, hái yǒu sān gè cài méiyǒu xiě zài shàngmiàn. Nǐ yào gēn lǎobǎn shuō「lái gè lǎo yàngzi」, tā jiù zhīdào zuò shénme le. Wǒ qù le hěn duō cì yǐhòu, zhōngyú yǒu yì tiān lǎobǎn wèn wǒ:「Yào bu yào lǎo yàngzi?」Nà yì tiān, wǒ juéde zìjǐ biànchéng le zhèlǐ de rén.
text_en: This restaurant's menu has twenty dishes. But regulars know there are three more not listed. You tell the owner 'the usual' and he knows what to make. After going many times, one day the owner finally asked me: 'Want the usual?' That day, I felt like I'd become a local.

questions.q_zh: 做饭/吃饭的场景暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the eating/cooking scene imply?
questions.explanation: Regulars ordering 'the usual' and the owner knowing exactly what to make — food carries belonging.

questions.q_zh: 要怎么点隐藏的菜？
questions.q_en: How do you order the hidden dishes?

options.pinyin: gēn lǎobǎn shuō「lái gè lǎo yàngzi」

questions.explanation: You tell the owner 'the usual' and he knows what to make — no menu needed.

questions.q_zh: 老板问「要不要老样子」的时候，「我」有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel when the owner asked about 'the usual'?

options.text: 觉得自己变成了这里的人
options.pinyin: juéde zìjǐ biànchéng le zhèlǐ de rén

questions.explanation: When the owner asked about 'the usual,' the narrator 'felt like becoming a local.'

title: Tea vs Coffee
text_zh: 爸爸喝茶，妈妈喝咖啡。他们从来不喝对方的。爸爸说茶让人安静，妈妈说咖啡让人有力气。有一天我问他们要不要试试对方的。爸爸喝了一口咖啡，说：「太苦了。」妈妈喝了一口茶，说：「没有味道。」然后他们笑了，继续喝自己的。
text_pinyin: Bàba hē chá, māma hē kāfēi. Tāmen cónglái bù hē duìfāng de. Bàba shuō chá ràng rén ānjìng, māma shuō kāfēi ràng rén yǒu lìqi. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ wèn tāmen yào bu yào shìshi duìfāng de. Bàba hē le yì kǒu kāfēi, shuō:「Tài kǔ le.」Māma hē le yì kǒu chá, shuō:「Méiyǒu wèidào.」Ránhòu tāmen xiào le, jìxù hē zìjǐ de.
text_en: Dad drinks tea, Mom drinks coffee. They never drink each other's. Dad says tea makes people calm, Mom says coffee gives energy. One day I asked if they'd try each other's. Dad took a sip of coffee: 'Too bitter.' Mom took a sip of tea: 'No flavor.' Then they laughed and went back to their own.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: Dad and Mom each have their drink, they joke about it, they stick with what they like — warm and easygoing.

questions.q_zh: 爸爸觉得咖啡怎么样？
questions.q_en: What did Dad think of coffee?
questions.explanation: Dad tried coffee once and said it was 'too bitter.'

questions.q_zh: 最后他们怎么做了？
questions.q_en: What did they do in the end?
questions.explanation: Despite trying each other's drinks, they 'kept drinking their own' — comfortable with their preferences.

title: Why We Say 'Hello' on the Phone
text_zh: 你有没有想过，为什么中国人打电话的时候都说「喂」？「喂」这个字本来是用来叫人的。因为以前的电话声音不太清楚，所以人们要先说一个字让对方知道电话通了。后来大家都说「喂」了，就变成了打电话的习惯。现在虽然电话的声音已经很清楚了，但是大家还是会说。
text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méi yǒu xiǎng guò, wèishénme Zhōngguó rén dǎ diànhuà de shíhou dōu shuō「wèi」? 「Wèi」zhège zì běnlái shì yòng lái jiào rén de. Yīnwèi yǐqián de diànhuà shēngyīn bú tài qīngchu, suǒyǐ rénmen yào xiān shuō yí gè zì ràng duìfāng zhīdào diànhuà tōng le. Hòulái dàjiā dōu shuō「wèi」le, jiù biànchéng le dǎ diànhuà de xíguàn. Xiànzài suīrán diànhuà de shēngyīn yǐjīng hěn qīngchu le, dànshì dàjiā háishi huì shuō.
text_en: Have you ever wondered why Chinese people say 'wei' when answering the phone? The character 'wei' originally means to call someone's attention. Because old phones had unclear sound, people needed to say a word first to let the other person know the call connected. Later everyone said 'wei' and it became a phone habit. Now the sound is clear, but people still say it.

questions.q_zh: 「喂」这个字本来是做什么用的？
questions.q_en: What was 'wei' originally used for?
questions.explanation: 喂原来的意思是叫人注意 — the character 喂 started as an attention-getting word, which suited early phone calls.

questions.q_zh: 以前为什么要先说「喂」？
questions.q_en: Why did people used to say 'wei' first?
questions.explanation: Early phones had poor sound, so people said 'wei' first to check if the other person could hear.

questions.q_zh: 现在电话声音怎么样了？
questions.q_en: How is phone sound quality now?
questions.explanation: 现在电话的声音已经很清楚了 — 已经很清楚 ('already very clear') describes the current state of phone audio.

title: Why Left and Right Are Different
text_zh: 大多数人用右手写字，但是有的人用左手。用左手的人比用右手的少很多。为什么会这样？没有人完全知道。但是有一个有意思的事情：用左手的人做有的事情的时候，比用右手的人快。所以用左手并不是不好——只是跟别人不一样。
text_pinyin: Dàduōshù rén yòng yòu shǒu xiě zì, dànshì yǒude rén yòng zuǒ shǒu. Yòng zuǒ shǒu de rén bǐ yòng yòu shǒu de shǎo hěn duō. Wèishénme huì zhèyàng? Méiyǒu rén wánquán zhīdào. Dànshì yǒu yí gè yǒu yìsi de shìqing: yòng zuǒ shǒu de rén zuò yǒude shìqing de shíhou, bǐ yòng yòu shǒu de rén kuài. Suǒyǐ yòng zuǒ shǒu bìng búshì bù hǎo——zhǐshì gēn biéren bù yíyàng.
text_en: Most people write with their right hand, but some use the left. Left-handed people are far fewer. Why? Nobody fully knows. But here's something interesting: left-handed people are faster at certain tasks. So being left-handed isn't bad — just different from others.

questions.q_zh: 用左手的人多还是用右手的人多？
questions.q_en: Are there more left-handed or right-handed people?
questions.explanation: 用左手的人少得多 — the comparison shows right-handed people significantly outnumber left-handed ones.

questions.q_zh: 为什么有的人用左手？
questions.q_en: Why do some people use their left hand?
questions.explanation: 没有人完全知道 — the cause remains unexplained; it's presented as an open question.

questions.q_zh: 用左手的人有什么特别的？
questions.q_en: What's special about left-handed people?

options.text: 做有的事情比右手的人快
options.pinyin: zuò yǒude shìqing bǐ yòu shǒu de rén kuài

questions.explanation: 左手的人做某些事情更快 — this is noted as an interesting advantage, though the reason is still unknown.

title: The Repairman's Hands
text_zh: 楼下有一个修鞋的老人。他的手很大，上面有很多小的伤。我问他疼不疼，他笑了笑说：「每一个伤都是一双鞋的故事。」从那以后，我觉得他的手比我见过的所有手都好看。因为那些手让旧的东西又变成新的了。
text_pinyin: Lóu xià yǒu yí gè xiū xié de lǎorén. Tā de shǒu hěn dà, shàngmiàn yǒu hěn duō xiǎo de shāng. Wǒ wèn tā téng bù téng, tā xiào le xiào shuō: 「Měi yí gè shāng dōu shì yì shuāng xié de gùshi.」 Cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ juéde tā de shǒu bǐ wǒ jiàn guò de suǒyǒu shǒu dōu hǎokàn. Yīnwèi nàxiē shǒu ràng jiù de dōngxi yòu biàn chéng xīn de le.
text_en: Downstairs there's an old man who repairs shoes. His hands are big, covered with small scars. I asked if they hurt. He smiled and said: 'Every scar is the story of a pair of shoes.' Since then, I think his hands are more beautiful than any I've seen. Because those hands make old things new again.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: Scars as stories, old things made new — the passage invites you to see beauty in worn hands.

questions.q_zh: 老人说他的伤是什么？
questions.q_en: What did the old man say about his scars?

options.text: 每一个伤都是一双鞋的故事
options.pinyin: měi yí gè shāng dōu shì yì shuāng xié de gùshi
options.text_en: every scar is the story of a pair of shoes

questions.explanation: He said 'every scar is the story of a pair of shoes' — each mark has meaning.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么觉得老人的手好看？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think the old man's hands are beautiful?

options.text: 因为那些手让旧的东西又变新了
options.pinyin: yīnwèi nàxiē shǒu ràng jiù de dōngxi yòu biàn xīn le
options.text_en: because those hands make old things new
because the old man is friendly

questions.explanation: Those hands 'make old things new again' — the narrator finds that beautiful.

title: The Last Customer
text_zh: 咖啡店要关门了，但是还有一个女人坐在角落里。她面前的咖啡已经冷了，可是她还在看窗外。服务员没有让她走，只是把灯关了一些。那个女人好像不是在等人，她只是需要一个安静的地方坐一会儿。
text_pinyin: Kāfēi diàn yào guān mén le, dànshì hái yǒu yí gè nǚrén zuò zài jiǎoluò lǐ. Tā miànqián de kāfēi yǐjīng lěng le, kěshì tā hái zài kàn chuāng wài. Fúwùyuán méiyǒu ràng tā zǒu, zhǐshì bǎ dēng guān le yìxiē. Nàge nǚrén hǎoxiàng búshì zài děng rén, tā zhǐshì xūyào yí gè ānjìng de dìfang zuò yíhuìr.
text_en: The coffee shop was about to close, but a woman still sat in the corner. The coffee in front of her had gone cold, yet she kept looking out the window. The server didn't ask her to leave, just dimmed some lights. The woman didn't seem to be waiting for anyone — she just needed a quiet place to sit for a while.

questions.q_zh: 咖啡店快要怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What was the coffee shop about to do?
questions.explanation: 咖啡店快要关门了 — 快要 ('about to') signals closing time, setting up the scene of the lingering customer.

questions.q_zh: 服务员做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the server do?

options.pinyin: gěi tā huàn le xīn kāfēi

questions.explanation: The server 'dimmed some lights' but didn't ask her to leave.

questions.q_zh: 那个女人在做什么？
questions.q_en: What was the woman doing?

options.text: 需要一个安静的地方坐一会儿
options.pinyin: xūyào yí gè ānjìng de dìfang zuò yíhuìr
options.text_en: needed a quiet place to sit

questions.explanation: She wasn't waiting for anyone — she just 'needed a quiet place to sit.'

title: The Umbrella Exchange
text_zh: 下雨了。地铁站外面，一个年轻人把自己的大伞给了一个老人，然后拿了老人的小伞。老人说：「你的伞比我的好。」年轻人说：「没关系，我走得快。」虽然只是很小的事，但是我看了以后心里很暖。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ le. Dìtiě zhàn wàimiàn, yí gè niánqīng rén bǎ zìjǐ de dà sǎn gěi le yí gè lǎorén, ránhòu ná le lǎorén de xiǎo sǎn. Lǎorén shuō: 「Nǐ de sǎn bǐ wǒ de hǎo.」 Niánqīng rén shuō: 「Méi guānxi, wǒ zǒu de kuài.」 Suīrán zhǐshì hěn xiǎo de shì, dànshì wǒ kàn le yǐhòu xīnlǐ hěn nuǎn.
text_en: It was raining. Outside the subway station, a young person gave their big umbrella to an elderly person and took the old person's small one. The old person said: 'Your umbrella is better than mine.' The young person said: 'It's okay, I walk fast.' Although it was a small thing, watching it made my heart warm.

questions.q_zh: 年轻人做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the young person do?

options.pinyin: bǎ dà sǎn gěi le lǎorén
options.text_en: gave the big umbrella to the old person

options.pinyin: mǎi le yì bǎ xīn sǎn

questions.explanation: The young person 'gave the big umbrella to the old person' and took the small one.

questions.q_zh: 年轻人为什么说没关系？
questions.q_en: Why did the young person say it's okay?

options.text_en: because they're not afraid of rain

questions.explanation: The young person said it's fine 'because they walk fast' — a simple, practical reason.

questions.q_zh: 「我」看了以后有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel after watching?
questions.explanation: After watching the exchange, the narrator's 'heart felt warm.'

title: The Sleeping Passenger
text_zh: 地铁上，坐在我旁边的人睡着了。他的头慢慢地靠到了我的肩膀上。我没有动。因为他看起来太累了，好像很久没有好好睡觉了。到站的时候，他醒了，看着我说了一声「对不起」。我说：「没事，你睡得好吗？」他笑了。
text_pinyin: Dìtiě shàng, zuò zài wǒ pángbiān de rén shuìzháo le. Tā de tóu mànman de kào dào le wǒ de jiānbǎng shàng. Wǒ méiyǒu dòng. Yīnwèi tā kàn qǐlái tài lèi le, hǎoxiàng hěn jiǔ méiyǒu hǎohāo shuì jiào le. Dào zhàn de shíhou, tā xǐng le, kànzhe wǒ shuō le yì shēng「duìbuqǐ」. Wǒ shuō: 「Méi shì, nǐ shuì de hǎo ma?」 Tā xiào le.
text_en: On the subway, the person next to me fell asleep. His head slowly leaned onto my shoulder. I didn't move. Because he looked so tired, like he hadn't slept well in a long time. When we reached the station, he woke up and said 'sorry.' I said: 'It's fine, did you sleep well?' He smiled.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: A stranger asleep on your shoulder, choosing not to move — the feeling is gentle and warm.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么没有动？
questions.q_en: Why didn't 'I' move?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā kàn qǐlái tài lèi le

questions.explanation: The narrator didn't move 'because he looked too tired' — compassion for a stranger.

questions.q_zh: 醒了以后，那个人说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the person say after waking up?
questions.explanation: When the person woke up, he said 'sorry' — embarrassed but grateful.

title: The Plant on the Windowsill
text_zh: 对面楼的一个窗户上，总是放着一盆花。春天的时候花开了，很好看。夏天太热了，花有点不好了。但是有人每天给它浇水。秋天花又好了。虽然我不知道那个人是谁，但是我觉得他一定很有耐心。
text_pinyin: Duìmiàn lóu de yí gè chuānghu shàng, zǒngshì fàngzhe yì pén huā. Chūntiān de shíhou huā kāi le, hěn hǎokàn. Xiàtiān tài rè le, huā yǒudiǎn bù hǎo le.
