Dànshì yǒu rén měi tiān gěi tā jiāo shuǐ. Qiūtiān huā yòu hǎo le. Suīrán wǒ bù zhīdào nàge rén shì shéi, dànshì wǒ juéde tā yídìng hěn yǒu nàixīn.
text_en: On a window of the building across from mine, there's always a pot of flowers. In spring the flowers bloomed beautifully. In summer it was too hot and the plant looked unwell. But someone watered it every day. In autumn the flowers recovered. Although I don't know who that person is, I think they must be very patient.

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: Spring blooming, summer wilting, autumn revival — the seasons shape the story's mood.

questions.q_zh: 夏天的时候花怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What happened to the plant in summer?
questions.explanation: In summer 'it was too hot and the plant looked unwell.'

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: The changing seasons and the plant's cycle create the emotional backdrop of the passage.

title: The Dog Walker's Routine
text_zh: 每天早上六点半，一个男人带着他的狗从我家门前走过。狗很老了，走得很慢。但是那个男人从来不让狗走快。他总是慢慢地走，有时候还停下来等狗闻一闻路边的树。我觉得他比很多人都懂什么是陪伴。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn, yí gè nánrén dàizhe tā de gǒu cóng wǒ jiā mén qián zǒu guò. Gǒu hěn lǎo le, zǒu de hěn màn. Dànshì nàge nánrén cónglái bú ràng gǒu zǒu kuài. Tā zǒngshì mànman de zǒu, yǒu shíhou hái tíng xiàlái děng gǒu wén yì wén lù biān de shù. Wǒ juéde tā bǐ hěn duō rén dōu dǒng shénme shì péibàn.
text_en: Every morning at six thirty, a man walks past my door with his dog. The dog is old and walks slowly. But the man never makes the dog walk faster. He always walks slowly, sometimes stopping to let the dog sniff the trees by the road. I think he understands companionship better than most people.

questions.q_zh: 重复的行为暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the repeated behavior imply?

options.text_en: this matters a lot to them

options.pinyin: tā méiyǒu bié de shì zuò
options.text_en: they have nothing else to do
others require them to do it

questions.explanation: Walking slowly every morning without rushing the dog — this daily care clearly matters deeply to him.

questions.q_zh: 那个男人让狗走快吗？
questions.q_en: Does the man make the dog walk fast?
questions.explanation: 他从来不让狗走快 — 从来不 ('never') shows consistent patience; he matches the old dog's pace every time.

questions.q_zh: 重复的行为暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the repeated behavior imply?

options.text_en: this matters a lot to them

options.pinyin: tā méiyǒu bié de shì zuò
options.text_en: they have nothing else to do
others require them to do it

questions.explanation: The same walk, same pace, every morning — this routine is important to both of them.

title: The Night Market's End
text_zh: 夜市快结束的时候，卖东西的人开始收摊了。一个卖水果的阿姨把剩下的水果装到箱子里。她旁边的人帮她搬了一个很重的箱子。他们好像不认识，但是别人有困难的时候，帮忙是很自然的事。夜市的灯一个一个地关了，街上又安静了。
text_pinyin: Yèshì kuài jiéshù de shíhou, mài dōngxi de rén kāishǐ shōu tān le. Yí gè mài shuǐguǒ de āyí bǎ shèng xià de shuǐguǒ zhuāng dào xiāngzi lǐ. Tā pángbiān de rén bāng tā bān le yí gè hěn zhòng de xiāngzi. Tāmen hǎoxiàng bú rènshi, dànshì biéren yǒu kùnnan de shíhou, bāngmáng shì hěn zìrán de shì. Yèshì de dēng yí gè yí gè de guān le, jiē shàng yòu ānjìng le.
text_en: When the night market was ending, vendors started packing up. A fruit-selling auntie put the remaining fruit into boxes. The person next to her helped carry a heavy box. They didn't seem to know each other, but when someone has difficulty, helping is the most natural thing. The night market lights went off one by one, and the street became quiet again.

questions.q_zh: 夜市快结束的时候，人们做什么？
questions.q_en: What did people do as the night market was ending?
questions.explanation: 卖东西的人开始收拾 — 收拾 ('pack up/tidy') describes the winding-down activity as the market closes.

questions.q_zh: 旁边的人做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the person nearby do?

options.text: 帮她搬了一个重的箱子
options.pinyin: bāng tā bān le yí gè zhòng de xiāngzi

questions.explanation: The person nearby 'helped carry a heavy box' for the fruit auntie.

questions.q_zh: 他们认识吗？
questions.q_en: Did they know each other?
questions.explanation: 他们好像不认识 — 好像不认识 ('seemed not to know each other') makes the help remarkable: a stranger pitched in.

title: The Name I Chose
text_zh: 我有一个中文名字，是我自己选的。老师说可以用跟我英文名字发音像的字。我选了两个字，一个是「安」，一个是「宁」。因为我觉得安静的生活比什么都好。朋友们叫我「安宁」的时候，我觉得自己真的变成了另一个人。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu yí gè Zhōngwén míngzi, shì wǒ zìjǐ xuǎn de. Lǎoshī shuō kěyǐ yòng gēn wǒ Yīngwén míngzi fāyīn xiàng de zì. Wǒ xuǎn le liǎng gè zì, yí gè shì「ān」, yí gè shì「níng」. Yīnwèi wǒ juéde ānjìng de shēnghuó bǐ shénme dōu hǎo. Péngyoumen jiào wǒ「Ānníng」de shíhou, wǒ juéde zìjǐ zhēn de biàn chéng le lìng yí gè rén.
text_en: I have a Chinese name that I chose myself. The teacher said I could use characters that sound like my English name. I chose two characters: 'an' and 'ning.' Because I think a quiet life is better than anything. When friends call me 'Anning,' I feel like I've truly become another person.

questions.q_zh: 中文名字是谁选的？
questions.q_en: Who chose the Chinese name?
questions.explanation: 我自己选的 — the teacher offered options, but the narrator made the final choice of 安 and 宁.

questions.q_zh: 为什么选了「安宁」这个名字？
questions.q_en: Why was the name 'Anning' chosen?

options.text: 因为觉得安静的生活比什么都好
options.pinyin: yīnwèi juéde ānjìng de shēnghuó bǐ shénme dōu hǎo
options.text_en: because a quiet life is best

questions.explanation: The narrator chose 'Anning' because 'a quiet life is best' — the meaning mattered.

questions.q_zh: 朋友叫他中文名字的时候，他有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How does he feel when friends call him by the Chinese name?

options.text: 觉得自己变成了另一个人
options.pinyin: juéde zìjǐ biàn chéng le lìng yí gè rén

questions.explanation: When friends use the Chinese name, the narrator 'feels like becoming another person.'

title: The Language in Between
text_zh: 说中文的时候，我觉得自己比较安静。说英文的时候，我说话比较快，也比较多。妈妈问我：「你用中文想东西还是用英文？」我想了想说：「有时候两个都用。」她笑了，说：「那你的脑子一定很忙。」
text_pinyin: Shuō Zhōngwén de shíhou, wǒ juéde zìjǐ bǐjiào ānjìng. Shuō Yīngwén de shíhou, wǒ shuō huà bǐjiào kuài, yě bǐjiào duō. Māma wèn wǒ: 「Nǐ yòng Zhōngwén xiǎng dōngxi háishi yòng Yīngwén?」 Wǒ xiǎng le xiǎng shuō: 「Yǒu shíhou liǎng gè dōu yòng.」 Tā xiào le, shuō: 「Nà nǐ de nǎozi yídìng hěn máng.」
text_en: When I speak Chinese, I feel quieter. When I speak English, I talk faster and more. Mom asked me: 'Do you think in Chinese or English?' I thought about it and said: 'Sometimes both.' She laughed and said: 'Then your brain must be busy.'

questions.q_zh: 说中文的时候，「我」觉得自己怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel when speaking Chinese?
questions.explanation: 说中文的时候觉得自己更安静 — speaking Chinese produces a calmer, quieter version of the narrator compared to English.

questions.q_zh: 「我」用什么语言想东西？
questions.q_en: What language does 'I' think in?

options.pinyin: yǒu shíhou liǎng gè dōu yòng

questions.explanation: The narrator thought about it and said 'sometimes both' — neither one dominates.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did mom say?

options.pinyin: nǐ de nǎozi yídìng hěn máng
options.text_en: your brain must be very busy

questions.explanation: Mom joked 'your brain must be very busy' — switching between two languages all the time.

title: The Old Photograph
text_zh: 搬家的时候，我找到了一张旧照片。照片里是十年前的我，站在学校门前。那时候我还不高，笑得很开心。我看了很久，想从那个笑里找到现在的自己。虽然长大了，但是有些东西没有变。比如，我还是喜欢一个人安安静静地看天。
text_pinyin: Bān jiā de shíhou, wǒ zhǎo dào le yì zhāng jiù zhàopiàn. Zhàopiàn lǐ shì shí nián qián de wǒ, zhàn zài xuéxiào mén qián. Nà shíhou wǒ hái bù gāo, xiào de hěn kāixīn. Wǒ kàn le hěn jiǔ, xiǎng cóng nàge xiào lǐ zhǎo dào xiànzài de zìjǐ. Suīrán zhǎng dà le, dànshì yǒuxiē dōngxi méiyǒu biàn. Bǐrú, wǒ háishi xǐhuan yí gè rén ān'ānjìngjìng de kàn tiān.
text_en: While moving, I found an old photograph. In it was me from ten years ago, standing in front of the school. Back then I wasn't tall yet, smiling happily. I looked at it for a long time, trying to find my present self in that smile. Although I've grown up, some things haven't changed. For example, I still like to quietly watch the sky alone.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: Finding an old photo, remembering the past self — the tone is gentle and warm.

questions.q_zh: 「我」在照片里做什么？
questions.q_en: What was 'I' doing in the photo?

options.text: 站在学校门前，笑得很开心
options.pinyin: zhàn zài xuéxiào mén qián, xiào de hěn kāixīn
options.text_en: standing in front of school, smiling happily

questions.explanation: In the photo, the narrator was 'standing in front of school, smiling happily.'

questions.q_zh: 什么东西没有变？
questions.q_en: What hasn't changed?

options.text: 喜欢一个人安静地看天
options.pinyin: xǐhuan yí gè rén ānjìng de kàn tiān
options.text_en: likes to quietly watch the sky alone

questions.explanation: Despite all the changes, the narrator still 'likes to quietly watch the sky alone.'

title: The Accent
text_zh: 有人跟我说：「你说中文有口音。」我以前觉得这不好。但是后来一个中国朋友对我说：「口音就是你从哪里来的证明。别想让它消失。」从那以后，我不再觉得口音是一个问题了。因为它是我的一部分，从我的家乡一直跟着我到这里。
text_pinyin: Yǒu rén gēn wǒ shuō: 「Nǐ shuō Zhōngwén yǒu kǒuyīn.」 Wǒ yǐqián juéde zhè bù hǎo. Dànshì hòulái yí gè Zhōngguó péngyou duì wǒ shuō: 「Kǒuyīn jiùshì nǐ cóng nǎli lái de zhèngmíng. Bié xiǎng ràng tā xiāoshī.」 Cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ bú zài juéde kǒuyīn shì yí gè wèntí le. Yīnwèi tā shì wǒ de yí bùfen, cóng wǒ de jiāxiāng yìzhí gēnzhe wǒ dào zhèlǐ.
text_en: Someone told me: 'You speak Chinese with an accent.' I used to think that was bad. But later a Chinese friend said to me: 'An accent is proof of where you come from. Don't try to make it disappear.' Since then, I no longer think an accent is a problem. Because it's part of me, following me from my hometown all the way here.

questions.q_zh: 「我」以前觉得口音怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' used to feel about the accent?
questions.explanation: The narrator 'used to think it was bad' to have an accent.

questions.q_zh: 中国朋友说口音是什么？
questions.q_en: What did the Chinese friend say an accent is?

options.text_en: proof of where you come from

questions.explanation: The friend said an accent is 'proof of where you come from' — it carries your history.

questions.q_zh: 现在「我」怎么看口音？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' view the accent now?

options.pinyin: juéde shì zìjǐ de yí bùfen

options.text_en: doesn't want to speak Chinese anymore

questions.explanation: Now the narrator 'thinks it's part of themselves' — no longer ashamed of it.

title: The Missing Spoon
text_zh: 办公室的厨房里有五个勺子。星期一变成了四个。没有人说拿了。星期三又变成了三个。我很好奇，所以每天早上都去看。星期五的时候，勺子又变成五个了。但是有两个是新的。因为有人发现勺子少了，买了新的放回去。
text_pinyin: Bàngōngshì de chúfáng lǐ yǒu wǔ gè sháozi. Xīngqīyī biàn chéng le sì gè. Méiyǒu rén shuō ná le. Xīngqīsān yòu biàn chéng le sān gè. Wǒ hěn hàoqí, suǒyǐ měi tiān zǎoshang dōu qù kàn. Xīngqīwǔ de shíhou, sháozi yòu biàn chéng wǔ gè le. Dànshì yǒu liǎng gè shì xīn de. Yīnwèi yǒu rén fāxiàn sháozi shǎo le, mǎi le xīn de fàng huíqù.
text_en: The office kitchen had five spoons. On Monday it became four. No one said they took one. Wednesday it became three. I was curious, so I checked every morning. By Friday, there were five again. But two were new. Someone noticed the spoons were disappearing and bought new ones to put back.

questions.q_zh: 这种坚持说明这个人怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does this persistence tell us about the person?

options.text_en: has no ideas of their own

questions.explanation: Checking every morning despite it being trivial — that persistence shows real determination.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么每天早上去看？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' check every morning?
questions.explanation: The narrator checked every morning 'because of curiosity' — had to solve the mystery.

questions.q_zh: 星期五多出来的勺子是怎么回事？
questions.q_en: What happened with the extra spoons on Friday?

options.pinyin: yǒu rén mǎi le xīn de fàng huíqù

questions.explanation: On Friday, 'someone bought new ones' — the mystery resolved itself practically.

title: The Note Under the Door
text_zh: 每个星期二，我的门下面都会出现一张小纸条。上面写着一句话，比如「今天会是好的一天」或者「别忘了喝水」。我问了邻居，他们都说不是自己写的。有一天，纸条上写着：「你笑的时候，整个楼都亮了。」我还是不知道是谁，但是我每个星期二都很期待。
text_pinyin: Měi gè Xīngqī'èr, wǒ de mén xiàmiàn dōu huì chūxiàn yì zhāng xiǎo zhǐtiáo. Shàngmiàn xiězhe yí jù huà, bǐrú「jīntiān huì shì hǎo de yì tiān」huòzhě「bié wàng le hē shuǐ」. Wǒ wèn le línjū, tāmen dōu shuō búshì zìjǐ xiě de. Yǒu yì tiān, zhǐtiáo shàng xiězhe:「Nǐ xiào de shíhou, zhěng gè lóu dōu liàng le.」 Wǒ háishi bù zhīdào shì shéi, dànshì wǒ měi gè Xīngqī'èr dōu hěn qīdài.
text_en: Every Tuesday, a small note appears under my door. On it is one sentence, like 'today will be a good day' or 'don't forget to drink water.' I asked my neighbors and they all said they didn't write it. One day the note said: 'When you smile, the whole building lights up.' I still don't know who it is, but every Tuesday I look forward to it.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: Anonymous kind notes every Tuesday — the passage wants you to feel that quiet happiness.

questions.q_zh: 邻居说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the neighbors say?

options.pinyin: dōu shuō búshì zìjǐ xiě de
options.text_en: all said they didn't write them

questions.explanation: The narrator asked, but all the neighbors 'said they didn't write them.'

questions.q_zh: 「我」对纸条是什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel about the notes?
questions.explanation: The narrator 'looks forward to them' every Tuesday — they've become part of the week.

title: The Wrong Coffee
text_zh: 今天在咖啡店，我拿了一杯咖啡。喝了一口，味道不对。我看了看杯子上的名字——不是我的。但是上面写着：「给今天累了的人，加了一点糖。」我想，是不是咖啡店的人故意写的？还是真的有人把自己的咖啡给了别人？不管怎样，那杯咖啡比我点的好喝。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān zài kāfēi diàn, wǒ ná le yì bēi kāfēi. Hē le yì kǒu, wèidào bú duì. Wǒ kàn le kàn bēizi shàng de míngzi——búshì wǒ de. Dànshì shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Gěi jīntiān lèi le de rén, jiā le yìdiǎn táng.」 Wǒ xiǎng, shì bu shì kāfēi diàn de rén gùyì xiě de? Háishi zhēn de yǒu rén bǎ zìjǐ de kāfēi gěi le biéren? Bùguǎn zěnyàng, nà bēi kāfēi bǐ wǒ diǎn de hǎohē.
text_en: Today at the coffee shop, I picked up a cup of coffee. After one sip, the taste was wrong. I looked at the name on the cup — it wasn't mine. But it said: 'For someone tired today, added a little sugar.' I wondered, did the barista write it on purpose? Or did someone really give their coffee away? Either way, that coffee tasted better than what I ordered.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么觉得不对？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' think something was wrong?
questions.explanation: After one sip, 'the taste was wrong' — that's how the narrator knew it wasn't the right cup.

questions.q_zh: 杯子上写了什么？
questions.q_en: What was written on the cup?

options.text: 给今天累了的人，加了一点糖
options.pinyin: gěi jīntiān lèi le de rén, jiā le yìdiǎn táng
options.text_en: for someone tired today, added sugar

questions.explanation: The cup said 'for someone tired today, added sugar' — a stranger's kind note.

questions.q_zh: 那杯咖啡跟「我」点的比怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did that coffee compare to what 'I' ordered?
questions.explanation: The wrong coffee 'tasted better than what I ordered' — the sugar and kindness made it special.

title: The Garden That Appears at Night
text_zh: 我家旁边有一个小花园。白天看起来很普通。但是晚上，花园里的灯亮了，花的颜色变得很不一样。有一天晚上，我看到一个人在花园里安安静静地种花。我问他为什么晚上种。他说：「因为晚上安静，花也比较放松。」我不知道这是不是真的，但是从那以后，我觉得晚上的花园比白天好看多了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā pángbiān yǒu yí gè xiǎo huāyuán. Báitiān kàn qǐlái hěn pǔtōng. Dànshì wǎnshang, huāyuán lǐ de dēng liàng le, huā de yánsè biàn de hěn bù yíyàng. Yǒu yì tiān wǎnshang, wǒ kàn dào yí gè rén zài huāyuán lǐ ān'ānjìngjìng de zhòng huā. Wǒ wèn tā wèishénme wǎnshang zhòng. Tā shuō:「Yīnwèi wǎnshang ānjìng, huā yě bǐjiào fàngsōng.」 Wǒ bù zhīdào zhè shì bu shì zhēn de, dànshì cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ juéde wǎnshang de huāyuán bǐ báitiān hǎokàn duō le.
text_en: Next to my home there's a small garden. During the day it looks ordinary. But at night, the garden lights come on and the flower colors become very different. One evening I saw someone quietly planting flowers. I asked why they plant at night. They said: 'Because it's quiet at night, and the flowers are more relaxed too.' I don't know if that's true, but since then, the garden at night seems much more beautiful than during the day.

questions.q_zh: 从前后的对比可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we learn from the before-and-after contrast?

options.pinyin: jīnglì dàilái le xīn de rènshi

questions.explanation: Ordinary by day, beautiful at night — seeing the garden differently brings new understanding.

questions.q_zh: 那个人为什么晚上种花？
questions.q_en: Why does the person plant at night?

options.text: 因为晚上安静，花比较放松
options.pinyin: yīnwèi wǎnshang ānjìng, huā bǐjiào fàngsōng
options.text_en: because it's quiet and flowers relax

options.pinyin: yīnwèi bù xiǎng ràng biéren kàn dào

questions.explanation: The person plants at night 'because it's quiet and flowers relax' — nighttime suits the work.

questions.q_zh: 从前后的对比可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we learn from the before-and-after contrast?

options.pinyin: jīnglì dàilái le xīn de rènshi

questions.explanation: The day vs. night contrast shows a second look can completely change your understanding.

title: The Library Book
text_zh: 我从图书馆借了一本书。打开的时候，里面有一张纸条，上面写着：「如果你也喜欢这本书，请在第三十页留一个记号。」我翻到第三十页——已经有五个小记号了。我也画了一个。虽然我们不认识，但是因为一本书，我们好像成了朋友。
text_pinyin: Wǒ cóng túshūguǎn jiè le yì běn shū. Dǎkāi de shíhou, lǐmiàn yǒu yì zhāng zhǐtiáo, shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Rúguǒ nǐ yě xǐhuan zhè běn shū, qǐng zài dì sānshí yè liú yí gè jìhao.」 Wǒ fān dào dì sānshí yè——yǐjīng yǒu wǔ gè xiǎo jìhao le. Wǒ yě huà le yí gè. Suīrán wǒmen bú rènshi, dànshì yīnwèi yì běn shū, wǒmen hǎoxiàng chéng le péngyou.
text_en: I borrowed a book from the library. When I opened it, there was a note inside: 'If you also like this book, please leave a mark on page thirty.' I turned to page thirty — there were already five small marks. I drew one too. Although we don't know each other, because of one book, it's like we've become friends.

questions.q_zh: 纸条上写了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the note say?

options.text: 如果喜欢，请在第三十页留记号
options.pinyin: rúguǒ xǐhuan, qǐng zài dì sānshí yè liú jìhao
options.text_en: if you like it, leave a mark on page 30

questions.explanation: The note said 'if you like this book, leave a mark on page 30.'

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以看出什么样的关系？
questions.q_en: What kind of relationship does this passage reveal?
questions.explanation: Strangers connected by a shared book — the bond feels real and genuine despite never meeting.

questions.q_zh: 这些人之间最重要的是什么？
questions.q_en: What matters most between these people?
questions.explanation: Multiple readers leaving marks for each other — they care about this invisible connection.

title: The Slow Train
text_zh: 从这个城市到那个城市，坐高铁只要两个小时。但是我选了慢火车，要六个小时。因为慢火车会在很多小站停。每一个小站都有不一样的风景。有的站旁边是田，有的站旁边是河。我觉得旅行不是从一个地方到另一个地方，而是看路上的风景。
text_pinyin: Cóng zhège chéngshì dào nàge chéngshì, zuò gāotiě zhǐ yào liǎng gè xiǎoshí. Dànshì wǒ xuǎn le màn huǒchē, yào liù gè xiǎoshí. Yīnwèi màn huǒchē huì zài hěn duō xiǎo zhàn tíng. Měi yí gè xiǎo zhàn dōu yǒu bù yíyàng de fēngjǐng. Yǒu de zhàn pángbiān shì tián, yǒu de zhàn pángbiān shì hé. Wǒ juéde lǚxíng búshì cóng yí gè dìfang dào lìng yí gè dìfang, érshì kàn lù shàng de fēngjǐng.
text_en: From this city to that city, the high-speed train takes only two hours. But I chose the slow train — six hours. Because the slow train stops at many small stations. Each station has different scenery. Some stations have fields nearby, others rivers. I think travel isn't going from one place to another, but seeing the scenery along the way.

questions.q_zh: 坐高铁要多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long does the high-speed train take?
questions.explanation: 高铁只要两个小时 — the high-speed option is fast at two hours, but the narrator chose the six-hour slow train instead.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么选了慢火车？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' choose the slow train?

options.text: 因为慢火车在很多小站停，风景不一样
options.pinyin: yīnwèi màn huǒchē zài hěn duō xiǎo zhàn tíng, fēngjǐng bù yíyàng
options.text_en: because it stops at many stations with different views

questions.explanation: The slow train 'stops at many stations' with different scenery at each — that's why.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: The changing views outside match the narrator's shifting thoughts — weather mirrors mood.

title: The Wrong Bus
text_zh: 我坐错了公共汽车，到了一个不认识的地方。我有一点紧张，但是没有马上回去。我在那个地方走了走，找到了一个很小的公园。公园里只有几棵树和一张椅子。我坐在那里休息了一会儿。虽然坐错了车，但是我发现了一个安静的好地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zuò cuò le gōnggòng qìchē, dào le yí gè bú rènshi de dìfang. Wǒ yǒu yìdiǎn jǐnzhāng, dànshì méiyǒu mǎshàng huíqù. Wǒ zài nàge dìfang zǒu le zǒu, zhǎo dào le yí gè hěn xiǎo de gōngyuán. Gōngyuán lǐ zhǐ yǒu jǐ kē shù hé yì zhāng yǐzi. Wǒ zuò zài nàli xiūxi le yíhuìr. Suīrán zuò cuò le chē, dànshì wǒ fāxiàn le yí gè ānjìng de hǎo dìfang.
text_en: I took the wrong bus and arrived at a place I didn't know. I was a little nervous, but didn't go back right away. I walked around and found a small park. The park had only a few trees and one bench. I sat there and rested a while. Although I took the wrong bus, I discovered a nice quiet spot.

questions.q_zh: 这个选择反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this choice reflect?
questions.explanation: Staying to explore instead of going back immediately — that choice reveals openness and curiosity.

questions.q_zh: 「我」找到了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' find?

options.pinyin: yí gè hěn xiǎo de gōngyuán

questions.explanation: Walking around the unknown place, the narrator found 'a very small park.'

questions.q_zh: 虽然坐错了车，结果怎么样？
questions.q_en: What was the result despite taking the wrong bus?

options.text: 发现了一个安静的好地方
options.pinyin: fāxiàn le yí gè ānjìng de hǎo dìfang

questions.explanation: Despite the wrong bus, the narrator 'discovered a nice quiet spot' — a happy accident.

title: The Hotel Window
text_zh: 旅馆的窗户打开以后，我看到了一条很老的街。街上没什么人，只有几家小店。一个老人坐在门前喝茶。从旅馆到这里的路很远，我很累。但是看到这个安安静静的地方，我觉得来对了。有时候旅行最好的部分，是到了以后从窗户看出去的那一刻。
text_pinyin: Lǚguǎn de chuānghu dǎkāi yǐhòu, wǒ kàn dào le yì tiáo hěn lǎo de jiē. Jiē shàng méi shénme rén, zhǐ yǒu jǐ jiā xiǎo diàn. Yí gè lǎorén zuò zài mén qián hē chá. Cóng lǚguǎn dào zhèlǐ de lù hěn yuǎn, wǒ hěn lèi. Dànshì kàn dào zhège ān'ānjìngjìng de dìfang, wǒ juéde lái duì le. Yǒu shíhou lǚxíng zuì hǎo de bùfen, shì dào le yǐhòu cóng chuānghu kàn chūqù de nà yí kè.
text_en: After opening the hotel window, I saw a old street. Few people on the street, just a few small shops. An old man sat in front of his door drinking tea. The journey here was long and I was tired. But seeing this peaceful place, I felt I'd come to the right spot. Sometimes the best part of travel is that moment when you look out the window after arriving.

questions.q_zh: 窗户外面是什么？
questions.q_en: What was outside the window?

options.pinyin: yì tiáo hěn lǎo de jiē

questions.explanation: 窗外是一条很旧的街 — the view revealed an old street with few people and small shops.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得来这里怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel about coming here?
questions.explanation: Seeing the quiet old street, the narrator felt 'I came to the right place.'

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: The quiet street and tea-drinking old man set the tone — the setting deepens that calm mood.

title: Why Bookstores Have Cafes
text_zh: 现在很多书店里面都有咖啡店。因为很多人不只是想买书，他们还想有一个地方可以安安静静地坐一会儿。买一杯咖啡，看一本书，不用走，不用说话。所以书店加了咖啡以后，来的人比以前多了。有时候一个安静的地方比什么都重要。
text_pinyin: Xiànzài hěn duō shūdiàn lǐmiàn dōu yǒu kāfēi diàn. Yīnwèi hěn duō rén bù zhǐshì xiǎng mǎi shū, tāmen hái xiǎng yǒu yí gè dìfang kěyǐ ān'ānjìngjìng de zuò yíhuìr. Mǎi yì bēi kāfēi, kàn yì běn shū, bú yòng zǒu, bú yòng shuō huà. Suǒyǐ shūdiàn jiā le kāfēi yǐhòu, lái de rén bǐ yǐqián duō le. Yǒu shíhou yí gè ānjìng de dìfang bǐ shénme dōu zhòngyào.
text_en: Now many bookstores have coffee shops inside. Because many people don't just want to buy books — they want a place to sit quietly for a while. Buy a cup of coffee, read a book, no need to go anywhere, no need to talk. So after bookstores added coffee, more people come than before. Sometimes a quiet place is more important than anything.

questions.q_zh: 为什么书店加了咖啡？
questions.q_en: Why did bookstores add coffee?

options.text: 因为人们想有地方安静地坐一会儿
options.pinyin: yīnwèi rénmen xiǎng yǒu dìfang ānjìng de zuò yíhuìr
options.text_en: people want a quiet place to sit

options.pinyin: yīnwèi shū bù hǎo mài le

questions.explanation: People 'don't just want to buy books — they want a quiet place to sit.'

questions.q_zh: 加了咖啡以后，来的人怎么样？
questions.q_en: After adding coffee, what happened to the visitors?
questions.explanation: After adding coffee, visitors became 'more than before' — it worked.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么样的变化？
questions.q_en: What kind of change does this passage suggest?
questions.explanation: The bookstore evolved to meet a deeper need — that adaptation is a kind of growth.

title: Why Rain Sounds Are Comforting
text_zh: 很多人说下雨的时候睡得好。这是因为雨声是一种很均匀的声音——不大不小，不快不慢。它让你的脑子不用去想别的事情。所以有的人在手机上听雨声睡觉。但是我觉得真的雨比手机上的好。因为真的雨还有风的味道。
text_pinyin: Hěn duō rén shuō xià yǔ de shíhou shuì de hǎo. Zhè shì yīnwèi yǔ shēng shì yì zhǒng hěn jūnyún de shēngyīn——bú dà bù xiǎo, bú kuài bú màn. Tā ràng nǐ de nǎozi bú yòng qù xiǎng biéde shìqing. Suǒyǐ yǒu de rén zài shǒujī shàng tīng yǔ shēng shuì jiào. Dànshì wǒ juéde zhēn de yǔ bǐ shǒujī shàng de hǎo. Yīnwèi zhēn de yǔ hái yǒu fēng de wèidào.
text_en: Many people say they sleep better when it rains. That's because rain is an even sound — not too loud, not too soft, not too fast, not too slow. It lets your brain stop thinking about other things. So some people listen to rain sounds on their phone to sleep. But I think real rain is better than on the phone. Because real rain also has the smell of wind.

questions.q_zh: 为什么雨声让人睡得好？
questions.q_en: Why does rain help people sleep?

options.text: 因为雨声很均匀，让脑子不想别的
options.pinyin: yīnwèi yǔ shēng hěn jūnyún, ràng nǎozi bù xiǎng biéde
options.text_en: rain is even and stops the brain from thinking

options.pinyin: yīnwèi xià yǔ de shíhou lěng
yīnwèi xià yǔ de shíhou ānjìng

questions.explanation: Rain is 'even, not too loud, not too soft' — it calms the brain and stops overthinking.

questions.q_zh: 有的人怎么听雨声？
questions.q_en: How do some people listen to rain sounds?
questions.explanation: Some people 'listen on their phone' — recorded rain sounds as a substitute.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么觉得真的雨比较好？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think real rain is better?

options.text: 因为真的雨还有风的味道
options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhēn de yǔ hái yǒu fēng de wèidào
options.text_en: real rain has the smell of wind too

questions.explanation: Real rain is better because 'it has the smell of wind too' — phones can't replicate that.

title: Why Old Neighborhoods Feel Different
title_zh: 老小区为什么感觉不一样
text_zh: 新小区的楼很高，很漂亮，但是邻居之间不怎么说话。老小区不一样。楼不高，但是楼下总是有人在聊天。老太太在晒太阳，小孩在玩。因为住的时间长了，大家都认识。所以老小区虽然旧了一点，但是比新的暖。
text_pinyin: Xīn xiǎoqū de lóu hěn gāo, hěn piàoliang, dànshì línjū zhījiān bù zěnme shuō huà. Lǎo xiǎoqū bù yíyàng. Lóu bù gāo, dànshì lóu xià zǒngshì yǒu rén zài liáotiān. Lǎo tàitai zài shài tàiyáng, xiǎohái zài wán. Yīnwèi zhù de shíjiān cháng le, dàjiā dōu rènshi. Suǒyǐ lǎo xiǎoqū suīrán jiù le yìdiǎn, dànshì bǐ xīn de nuǎn.
text_en: New housing complexes have tall, pretty buildings, but neighbors don't talk much. Old neighborhoods are different. Buildings aren't tall, but downstairs people are always chatting. Old ladies sunbathe, kids play. Because everyone has lived there a long time, they all know each other. So old neighborhoods may be a bit worn, but they're warmer than new ones.

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: The contrast between old warmth and new isolation — the setting shapes the story's emotional tone.

questions.q_zh: 老小区楼下有什么？
questions.q_en: What's downstairs in old neighborhoods?
questions.explanation: In old neighborhoods, 'people are always chatting' downstairs.

questions.q_zh: 文章觉得老小区比新小区怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the passage compare old and new neighborhoods?
questions.explanation: Old neighborhoods are 'warmer' — people actually know and talk to each other.

title: The Group Chat Name
text_zh: 我们宿舍四个人有一个群聊。名字换了很多次。第一次叫「好朋友」。后来有人改成了「减肥群」，但是没有人减肥。又有人改成了「学习群」，但是我们只在里面点外卖。现在的名字是「别问，问就是在学习」。我觉得这个名字最真实。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen sùshè sì gè rén yǒu yí gè qúnliáo. Míngzi huàn le hěn duō cì. Dì yí cì jiào「hǎo péngyou」. Hòulái yǒu rén gǎi chéng le「jiǎnféi qún」, dànshì méiyǒu rén jiǎnféi. Yòu yǒu rén gǎi chéng le「xuéxí qún」, dànshì wǒmen zhǐ zài lǐmiàn diǎn wàimài. Xiànzài de míngzi shì「bié wèn, wèn jiùshì zài xuéxí」. Wǒ juéde zhège míngzi zuì zhēnshí.
text_en: The four of us in the dorm have a group chat. The name has changed many times. First it was 'Good Friends.' Then someone changed it to 'Weight Loss Group,' but no one lost weight. Then someone changed it to 'Study Group,' but we only used it to order takeout. Now it's called 'Don't Ask, If You Ask We're Studying.' I think this name is the most honest.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以看出什么样的关系？
questions.q_en: What kind of relationship does this passage reveal?
questions.explanation: Changing the group name from 'Study Group' to ordering takeout — that's genuine closeness.

questions.q_zh: 「学习群」里他们做什么？
questions.q_en: What did they do in the 'Study Group'?
questions.explanation: In the 'Study Group' they actually 'ordered takeout' — the name was just for show.

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: The joke names, the shared habits — these small things show how well they know each other.

title: The Office Plant Manager
text_zh: 办公室里有几盆花，没有人管。后来老板说：「谁愿意管花？每个月多给一百块。」所有人都举手了。最后小李赢了，因为他说他在家里养了三十盆花。但是第一个星期，他就浇太多水，花快要死了。我们开玩笑说：「那三十盆花是假的吧？」
text_pinyin: Bàngōngshì lǐ yǒu jǐ pén huā, méiyǒu rén guǎn. Hòulái lǎobǎn shuō:「Shéi yuànyì guǎn huā? Měi gè yuè duō gěi yì bǎi kuài.」 Suǒyǒu rén dōu jǔ shǒu le. Zuìhòu Xiǎo Lǐ yíng le, yīnwèi tā shuō tā zài jiā lǐ yǎng le sānshí pén huā. Dànshì dì yí gè xīngqī, tā jiù jiāo tài duō shuǐ, huā kuài yào sǐ le. Wǒmen kāi wánxiào shuō:「Nà sānshí pén huā shì jiǎ de ba?」
text_en: The office had a few plants that no one took care of. Then the boss said: 'Who wants to manage the plants? Extra 100 yuan per month.' Everyone raised their hands. Xiao Li won because he said he had thirty plants at home. But in the first week, he overwatered them and they almost died. We joked: 'Were those thirty plants fake?'

questions.q_zh: 老板说管花每个月多给多少钱？
questions.q_en: How much extra per month did the boss offer?
questions.explanation: The boss offered '100 yuan per month' extra for managing the plants.

questions.q_zh: 小李第一个星期做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did Xiao Li do in the first week?
questions.explanation: In the first week, Xiao Li 'overwatered' the plants — too enthusiastic.

questions.q_zh: 同事们开了什么玩笑？
questions.q_en: What did the colleagues joke about?

options.text: 小李家的三十盆花是假的
options.pinyin: Xiǎo Lǐ jiā de sānshí pén huā shì jiǎ de
options.text_en: his 30 plants at home were fake

questions.explanation: Colleagues joked that 'his 30 plants at home were all fake' — that's why he can't keep real ones alive.

title: The Reply-All Disaster
text_zh: 同事小王给全公司发了一封邮件，说：「明天开会，请大家准时到。」我想回复小王说「好的」。但是我不小心点了「回复所有人」。五分钟以后，又有十个人回复了所有人，都是「好的」。然后有人回复所有人说：「别再回复所有人了！」他也回复了所有人。
text_pinyin: Tóngshì Xiǎo Wáng gěi quán gōngsī fā le yì fēng yóujiàn, shuō:「Míngtiān kāi huì, qǐng dàjiā zhǔnshí dào.」 Wǒ xiǎng huífù Xiǎo Wáng shuō「hǎo de」. Dànshì wǒ bù xiǎoxīn diǎn le「huífù suǒyǒu rén」. Wǔ fēnzhōng yǐhòu, yòu yǒu shí gè rén huífù le suǒyǒu rén, dōu shì「hǎo de」. Ránhòu yǒu rén huífù suǒyǒu rén shuō:「Bié zài huífù suǒyǒu rén le!」 Tā yě huífù le suǒyǒu rén.
text_en: Colleague Xiao Wang sent an email to the whole company: 'Meeting tomorrow, please be on time.' I wanted to reply to Xiao Wang saying 'okay.' But I accidentally hit 'reply all.' Five minutes later, ten more people replied all with 'okay.' Then someone replied all saying: 'Stop replying all!' They also replied all.

questions.q_zh: 小王的邮件说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did Xiao Wang's email say?

options.pinyin: míngtiān kāi huì, qǐng zhǔnshí dào

questions.explanation: Xiao Wang's email said 'meeting tomorrow, please be on time.'

questions.q_zh: 「我」做错了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' do wrong?

options.text: 不小心点了回复所有人
options.pinyin: bù xiǎoxīn diǎn le huífù suǒyǒu rén

questions.explanation: The narrator 'accidentally hit reply all' instead of replying just to Xiao Wang.

questions.q_zh: 说「别再回复所有人了」的人做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the person who said 'stop replying all' do?
questions.explanation: The person who told everyone to 'stop replying all' also replied all — making the same mistake.
